Listing Page: Integration
description:
Background
With the listing page skeleton and data fetching in place, this story covers wiring the queried data into the listing page and rendering it correctly. Each URL must render the correct page header, active filter tab, active category pill, article tiles in the listing grid, and pagination controls. The page must reflect the URL the user has landed on across all routes.
Category pills are links each one navigates to its corresponding URL. When a user switches between the Equity and Credit filter tabs, the page defaults to the first category pill within the selected filter.
Pre-Requisite
Listing page skeleton story is complete

 
Listing page query and data fetching story is complete

 
USER STORY 
As a user,

 I want to see a list of relevant articles when I land on a listing page, with the correct filter tab and category pill active, 

So that I can browse content relevant to my chosen section.
Scope
Rendering of article tiles in the listing grid using data fetched in 

 
Active filter tab state driven by the URL (Equity or Credit)
Active category pill state driven by the URL
All category pills rendered as links to their corresponding URLs
Default to first category pill when switching between Equity and Credit filter tabs
Page header renders dynamically based on the URL
Pagination controls rendered below the listing grid, next page triggers fetch of next 10 articles
Article tile renders the following fields per article: category label, title, excerpt, read time, published date, and thumbnail image
Business Rules
IF a user lands on a listing page URL THEN the filter tab matching that URL's filter (Equity or Credit) renders as active
IF a user lands on a listing page URL THEN the category pill matching that URL's category renders as active
IF a user clicks a category pill THEN they are navigated to the corresponding URL for that pill as per the reference table
IF a user clicks the Equity filter tab THEN the page navigates to the first category URL within Equity and the All category pill renders as active
IF a user clicks the Credit filter tab THEN the page navigates to the first category URL within Credit and the All category pill renders as active
IF the listing page loads THEN 10 article tiles are rendered in the listing grid
IF a user navigates to the next page THEN the next 10 articles are rendered in the listing grid
IF an article tile is rendered THEN the following fields are displayed: Primary 
category label
, title, excerpt, read time, published date, and thumbnail image
IF the Primary category label field is empty THEN the category label is not displayed on the tile
IF the thumbnail image field is empty THEN the fallback image should be displayed
IF the Deals pill is present THEN it renders with an external link icon and clicking it opens 

  as an external link

Assumptions

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
AC1 -Active filter tab reflects the URL
 GIVEN a user lands on any listing page URL WHEN the page loads THEN the filter tab matching that URL's filter (Equity or Credit) renders as active
AC2 : Active category pill reflects the URL
 GIVEN a user lands on any listing page URL WHEN the page loads THEN the category pill matching that URL's category renders as active
AC3 — Category pills are links
 GIVEN a user is on any listing page WHEN they click a category pill THEN they are navigated to the corresponding URL for that pill as per the reference table
AC4 — Switching to Equity tab defaults to All
 GIVEN a user is on a Credit listing page WHEN they click the Equity filter tab THEN the page navigates to the first category URL within Equity and the All category pill renders as active
AC5 — Switching to Credit tab defaults to All

AC6 — Article tiles are rendered in the listing grid
 GIVEN a user lands on any listing page URL WHEN the page loads THEN 10 article tiles are rendered in the listing grid with the correct fields: category label, title, excerpt, read time, published date, and thumbnail image
AC7 — Article tile renders without category label if field is empty
 GIVEN an article has no category label WHEN the article tile is rendered THEN no category label is displayed on the tile
AC8 — Article tile renders without image if thumbnail is empty

AC9 — Pagination renders and loads next set of articles
 GIVEN a user is on any listing page WHEN they navigate to the next page THEN the next 10 articles are fetched and rendered in the listing grid
AC10 — Deals pill renders as an external link
 GIVEN the Deals pill is present in the category filter bar WHEN a user clicks it THEN they are navigated to 

  and it opens as an external link

title: Listing Pages: Backend Querying Work
description:
Background
This story covers setting up the queries and data fetching logic for all listing page routes. Each URL maps to a specific query against either PERE News WP or PERE Credit WP. The correct articles must be fetched based on the URL, with pagination handled at 10 articles per page. Each article must also return the fields required to render the article tile. No rendering is involved in this story — data fetching only.
URLs are defined explicitly rather than via pattern matching, as inconsistencies in URL structure across the Equity and Credit sections make a pattern-based approach unviable.e.
Pre-Requisite
Listing page skeleton story is complete

 
Top-level taxonomy categories are confirmed and applied for Equity content (Fundraising, Managers, Investors, Deals, People)
Credit taxonomy alignment is complete
PERE News WP and PERE Credit WP GraphQL fields are exposed and available to query
USER STORY 
As a developer,

I need the correct articles and their tile fields to be fetched for each listing page URL 

So that the data is available to be rendered in the subsequent layout and integration story.
Scope
Query setup for all URLs listed in the reference table below
Articles fetched from PERE News WP or PERE Credit WP as specified per URL
The following fields fetched per article: category label, title, excerpt, read time, published date, and thumbnail image
Pagination logic: 10 articles per page, next page fetches the next 10
Deals URLs are external links and do not require a query
Business Rules
IF a user lands on a listing page URL THEN the query corresponding to that URL is used to fetch articles from the correct WP source
IF the URL maps to an Equity route THEN articles are fetched from PERE News WP
IF the URL maps to a Credit route THEN articles are fetched from PERE Credit WP
IF the listing page loads THEN 10 articles are fetched for that URL
IF a user navigates to page 2 or beyond THEN the next set of 10 articles is fetched using the same query with the appropriate offset
IF an article is returned by the query THEN the following fields must be fetched for each article: category label, title, excerpt, read time, published date, and thumbnail image
IF the category label field is empty THEN the category label is not displayed on the tile
IF the thumbnail image field is empty THEN no image is displayed and the tile renders without a broken image placeholder
IF the URL is 

  THEN no query is required as it is an external link

PAYWALL BEHAVOUR on II OBX preview
description:
Bug: Paywall behaviour incorrectly displayed on article preview in II OBX
Description
 When a user clicks Preview on an article in the WP Admin, paywall behaviour is being applied. Preview mode is intended solely for editorial purposes , to allow content editors to see how an article will look before publishing. Paywall rules should not be triggered in this context, as the user is not a site visitor consuming content.
Environments / Browsers / Versions / URLs
Environments: TBC
Browsers: TBC
Versions: TBC
URLs: II OBX - WP Admin article preview URL
Steps to Replicate
Log in to WP Admin on II OBX
Open any article (paywalled or otherwise)
Click Preview
Observe the article preview in the browser
Expected Behaviour
 The article preview should render the full article content without any paywall behaviour applied. Preview mode is an editorial tool and should bypass all paywall gate logic regardless of the previewing user's subscription tier.
Observed Behaviour
 Paywall behaviour is being applied when previewing an article via WP Admin. The paywall gate is triggering on the preview URL, obscuring article content that the editor needs to review.

title: PERE - News and Analysis- Update the article exclusion logic
description:
USER STORY 
As a
 PEI user 
I want
 certain content to be excluded from the News and Analysis section of the PERE OBX homepage 
So that
 I only see relevant articles
Scope
To implement exclusion logic on the PERE OBX homepage News & Analysis section so that articles tagged with the following items are excluded across the three PERE WordPress sources:
PERE News
PERE Credit
PERE Deals
Business Rules
PERE News exclusions
IF an editor creates an article in PERE News WP Editorial AND assigns the 
"Past Events"
 or 
"Newsletters"
 news category tag to the article, THEN do not display the article in the News & Insights section of the PERE OBX Homepage.
IF an editor creates an article in PERE News WP Editorial AND selects the content type as 
"Deep Dive"
 or 
"Data Snapshot"
, THEN do not display the article in the News & Insights section of the PERE OBX Homepage. Data Snapshot content should never appear in the News & Insights section, it should only appear in the bento box.
IF an article in PERE News has multiple news categories tags assigned AND one of those news categories is 
"Network"
, 
"Past Events"
, or 
"Newsletters"
, THEN the article should not appear in the News & Insights section regardless of the other news category tags that have been applied.
IF an article in PERE News has the parent category 
"Network"
 tagged (with or without sub-tags), THEN the article should not appear in the News & Insights section.
PERE Credit exclusions
IF an editor creates an article in PERE Credit WP Editorial AND selects the content type as 
"Deep Dive"
, THEN do not display the article in the News & Insights section of the PERE OBX Homepage.
PERE Deals exclusions
No exclusions apply for PERE Deals — all articles from PERE Deals WordPress are eligible for the News & Insights section.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — PERE News: Articles assigned to an excluded category are not displayed
 
GIVEN 
an article in PERE News has any one of the following categories: Past Events, Newsletters, or Network (parent) 
WHEN 
the News & Insights section loads on the PERE OBX homepage 
THEN 
that article should not appear in the section
AC2 — PERE News: Articles with excluded content type are not displayed
 
GIVEN 
an article in PERE News has a content type of "Deep Dive" or "Data Snapshot" 
WHEN 
the News & Insights section loads on the PERE OBX homepage 
THEN 
that article should not appear in the section
AC3 — PERE News: Articles with multiple categories are excluded if any category matches
 
GIVEN 
an article in PERE News is assigned multiple categories 
AND 
at least one of those categories matches the exclusion list (Network, Past Events, Newsletters) WHEN the News & Insights section loads on the PERE OBX homepage 
THEN 
that article should not appear in the section regardless of its other assigned categories
AC4 — PERE Credit: Articles with "Deep Dive" content type are not displayed
 
GIVEN 
an article in PERE Credit has a content type of "Deep Dive" 
WHEN 
the News & Insights section loads on the PERE OBX homepage 
THEN 
that article should not appear in the section
Rules - 
no exclusions apply for PERE Deals

 — No regression to existing II behaviour

OBX PERE - SEO - Redirect - Internal URL's  Redirect via Cloudfront using Key Value store (KVS)
description:
Background
Pre- Requisite - 
Should be done on the actual domain. Devops setup for the New domain should be completed.
Order of execution
Extract the keys and put in the place by the time devops setup for the new domain is in place
Be ready with the prefix solution.
Devops work to add the file in the pipeline
Simon and emma have decided to go with -   /equity/slug  and /credit/slug
User Story
AS A
 user following a link to an article that has been redirected,

I WANT
 to be taken to the correct destination automatically,

SO THAT
 I do not land on a broken or incorrect page due to a redirect not being respected.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement a CloudFront KeyValueStore containing all WordPress redirect mappings, and a CloudFront Function to intercept viewer requests and action 301 redirects to the new URL at the edge before requests reach Fusion.
Technical Solution
The CloudFront Function must be associated with the CloudFront distribution as a Viewer Request trigger.
Redirects must be actioned at the edge before requests reach Fusion, ensuring no dependency on WordPress or Fusion for redirect resolution.
Business Rules
A new folder for OBX PERE must be created in the monorepo under 
CloudFrontKBConfig
, populated with the required JSON key-value pairs for redirects, following the structure used for II.
The DevOps team must be engaged to ensure the new PERE folder is included in the pipeline for redirect updates.
The CloudFront redirect function logic must support brand-specific prefixes (e.g., “equity” and “credit”) as per requirements.
Potential duplicate key conflicts arising from merging PERE News and PERE Credit redirects must be identified and resolved, including the use of brand-specific prefixes in the key-value store if required.
Path-level redirects (e.g., /abc → /abc2) to be handled via Cloudflare and KVS, following the same pattern as II OBX. Must be in place during Beta.

title: Add RUM view-context tagging to article pages on app-portfolio for accurate Datadog dashboards
description:
Background
Article pages on app-portfolio (Infrastructure Investor, PEI, etc.) currently land in Datadog RUM with no custom context distinguishing them from other pages. Because article URLs live at the root level (
/<brand>/<slug>
) and share the same URL shape as 
/memberships
, 
/search
, 
/watchlist
, etc., there's no reliable way for Datadog dashboards to filter "article views" from "everything else."
The existing workaround in dashboards uses 
@view.url_path:*article*
 substring matching, which:
Misses real article URLs
 — high-traffic slugs like 
/gip-to-target-10bn-for-new-gcc-central-asia-fund
 (3,605 views) and 
/taaleri-launches-e1bn-fourth-renewables-fund-exclusive
 (3,248 views) don't contain the word "article."
Falsely includes non-article pages
 when other paths happen to contain the substring.
This means our article performance dashboards are showing incomplete and contaminated data.
Goal
Tag every article view event in Datadog RUM with three custom view-context properties, enabling clean filtering and grouping in dashboards:
Key
Values
@context.page_type
"article"
 (paywall/user flow) or 
"article_seo"
 (no-paywall/crawler flow)
@context.article_slug
The post's slug
@context.brand
The brand (
ii
, 
pei
, etc.)
After this lands, dashboards can use the clean filter 
@context.page_type:article
 instead of fragile URL substring matching, and per-article breakdowns become possible via 
@context.article_slug
.
Acceptance Criteria
A new client component 
ArticleRumTag
 exists in 
apps/app-portfolio/components/article/
 that sets 
page_type
, 
article_slug
, and 
brand
 as RUM view-context properties via 
datadogRum.setViewContextProperty
.
The component accepts a 
pageType
 prop (
"article"
 or 
"article_seo"
) with a default of 
"article"
.
In Datadog RUM Explorer, the query 
@type:view @context.page_type:article env:<env>
 returns article view events with the context visible in the event detail panel.
Why view-context vs global-context
setViewContextProperty
 is 
view-scoped
 in the Datadog browser SDK — properties only apply to the current view and are automatically reset on SPA navigation. This means non-article pages never inherit article tags. 
setGlobalContextProperty
 (would pollute every future event) was rejected for this reason.
On deployed environment (dev/stage)
After merge:
Wait ~2 min for traffic.
Datadog → 
RUM → Explorer
 → query 
env:<env> @context.page_type:article
.
Confirm rows appear and that each row's event detail shows 
@context.page_type
, 
@context.article_slug
, 
@context.brand
 populated.
Same check for 
@context.page_type:article_seo
.
Out of scope (track as follow-up tickets)
Extending the same 
setViewContextProperty
 pattern to other key sections (search, memberships, watchlist) for full section-level analytics. Separate work item.

title: Conditionally Render Icons on Nav bar
[QA] OBX PERE - SEO - Performance - Analyze SEO core web vitals for improvements
OBX PERE -SEO-  SSR - Marketing Page
description:
Background
SEO validation identified that the Marketing Landing page is not rendering its body content in the initial HTML response on OBX II. 
Currently, the initial HTML mainly contains navigation and footer content, while the page-specific marketing content is delivered through RSC streaming payloads and client-side hydration. As a result, key commercial and indexable copy is not reliably available to search engines before JavaScript execution.
This is causing the Marketing Landing page to fail SEO expectations for server-rendered content. To improve indexability and ensure SEO-critical content is discoverable by search engines, the page body content needs to be rendered directly in the initial HTML response.
SEO Feedback 
- The page may be technically server-routed by Next.js, but the body content is hydrated client-side via RSC streaming. For SEO purposes i.e. what Googlebot reliably indexes that's not server rendering
The feedback has already been addressed in OBX II, and we are now incorporating the same changes into OBX PERE.
Scope 
New indexable marketing pages need their body content to be SSR rendered in the initial HTML response to rank.
Out of Scope
Adding the schema in the <Head> section of the page. This was not implemented in OBX II .
Business rules
Ensure that the body content is server-side rendered (SSR) in the initial HTML and not limited to RSC payloads. This applies to all key sections Hero, Bento Grid, Membership Cards, Feature Comparison, and FAQ.
Total page size 606KB. After stripping all script blocks, 42KB remains. 93% of the page is RSC streaming payloads inside self.__next_f.push() calls.
Initial HTML contains 0 h1 elements and 0 h2 elements. The 8 h3 elements are sister-brand nav links (Infrastructure, Private Equity, Private Real Estate, Private Debt, Secondaries).
Value-prop content from the page (4,000+ fund profiles, $2.5trn tracked deals, the FAQ block, "Depending on the package..." subscription detail, etc.) is present in the source but only inside RSC payloads. None of it appears in the static HTML.
What Google receives in the initial response: header nav, brand sitemap, footer. No page-specific content.
Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 - Validate Marketing Landing Page Body Content is Server Rendered in Initial HTML Response
GIVEN 
the Marketing Landing page is loaded and the initial HTML response is inspected before JavaScript execution
WHEN 
the page source is validated for server-rendered content
THEN 
the initial HTML response should contain page-specific marketing content
AND 
the body content is server-side rendered (SSR) for Hero, Bento Grid, Membership Cards, Feature Comparison, and FAQ.
AND 
the initial HTML should contain valid 
<h1>
 and 
<h2>
 elements
AND 
value-prop content such as “4,000+ fund profiles”, “$2.5trn tracked deals”, FAQ content, and subscription/package details should be present in the static HTML response
AND 
the page content should not be rendered only through RSC streaming payloads inside 
self.__next_f.push()
 calls
AND 
the initial HTML response should not contain only header navigation, brand sitemap links, and footer content
AND 
the page should expose SEO-critical content in the initial HTML response received by Googlebot.

title: OBX PERE - SEO - Sitemap - Generate OBX PERE domain sitemap
[QA] OBX PERE - Create new Zephr site add rules
description:
Background 
A new domain has been introduced for the OBX PERE platform. To support this, a separate site needs to be configured in Zephr so that rules, registration flows, and entitlements can be managed independently from other platforms.
User Story 
As a
 developer 
I want
 a new site to be configured in Zephr for the OBX PERE Domain
So that
 the correct rules, registration flows, entitlements & user journey cab ne managed independently for PERE platform
Scope 
To verify that the new Zephr site is set up for the OBX PERE domain
To verify that the packages have been added in Zephr
To verify that the rules for the packages are configured for the OBX PERE site in Zephr
Note
:
 This scope is only to check and confirm that the above items are set up in Zephr.
Business rules
The Zephr site should be set up for the new 
pei-privaterealestate.com
 domain
All packages and rules should be configured for this domain in Zephr
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Dependency 1 - Zephr site must be set up for the new domain
Dependency 2 - Packages and rules must be added in Zephr for the new domain
Acceptance Criteria
 
Scenario 1 - Site Setup
GIVEN 
the OBX PERE domain is created
WHEN 
I check Zephr
THEN 
the new site should be set up for the OBX PERE domain
Scenario 2 - Packages
GIVEN 
the OBX PERE site is set up in Zephr
WHEN 
I check the packages
THEN 
the required packages should be added to the site
Scenario 3 -Package Rules
GIVEN 
the packages are added to the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I check the rules
THEN 
the correct rules should be configured for each package

OBX II & OBX PERE - Marketing Takeover Overlay Page - Sticky Close Icon
description:
Background 
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, we’re introducing a marketing landing page for the PERE portfolio site, with its own dedicated URL to showcase PEI’s value proposition, product range and available subscriptions. We now need to create the marketing takeover overly, so that it can be accessed from the logged-out homepage.
User Story
 
As a
 PEI Marketer,
I want 
customers to be able to close the marketing takeover overlay from any section,
So that
 they can return to the logged-out homepage.
Scope 
To implement a sticky close (“X”) icon on the marketing takeover overlay of OBX II & OBX PERE
Business Rules 
The close (“X”) icon must remain sticky and fixed to the top-right corner of the marketing takeover overlay.
The “X” icon must remain visible at all times during internal overlay scrolling.
The “X” icon must be accessible from all sections of the marketing takeover overlay.
Users must be able to close the marketing takeover overlay by clicking the “X” icon located on the right-hand side of the overlay.
IF the “X” icon is clicked, THEN the overlay should be closed, AND the user should remain on the logged-out homepage.
Figma Link: 
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=37-1983&t=OG5No0m6dhy2JBw3-0
  
UX / UI 
Logged Out OBX Homepage - Marketing Takeover Overlay 
 Figma link: 
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=37-1983&t=OG5No0m6dhy2JBw3-0
  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Open Question - The Logged out OBX PERE do not have the Banners with Find out more CTA implemented. 
Acceptance Criteria
 
Scenario - Close Marketing Takeover Overlay Using Sticky “X” Icon
GIVEN
 I am a PEI marketer,
AND
 I am on the marketing takeover overlay,
AND
 a sticky “X” icon is visible on the overlay,
WHEN
 I click the sticky “X” icon,
THEN
 the overlay should close,
AND
 I should be remain on the logged-out homepage.

title: OBX PERE - Marketing Takeover Overlay - Linking the overlay to the logged out homepage
description:

Background 
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, we’re introducing a marketing landing page for the PERE portfolio site, with its own dedicated URL to showcase PEI’s value proposition, product range and available subscriptions. We now need to create the marketing takeover overly, so that it can be accessed from the logged-out homepage.
User Story
 
As a
 PEI Marketer,
I want 
a marketing takeover overlay to be created on the logged-out OBX PERE homepage, 
So that 
customers can access the marketing page in situ. 
Scope 
To import all existing components from the existing marketing landing page into the marketing takeover overlay for OBX PERE.
Business Rules 
Ensure to import all the sections of the OBX PERE marketing landing page to the marketing takeover overlay.
Ensure the EXACT same sections and descriptions from the marketing landing page are imported and displayed on the marketing takeover overlay.
The marketing landing page and the marketing takeover overlay should ALWAYS be in sync and display the same content. 
IF the marketing takeover overlay is open, THEN do not display the OBX page header and primary navigation menu 
Users must be able to close the marketing takeover overlay by clicking the “X” icon in the right-hand side of the overlay
IF the “X” icon is clicked, THEN the overlay should be closed, AND the user should remain on the logged-out homepage.
Ensure the marketing takeover overlay displays ‘Request a demo’ & ‘Explore the Product’ CTA
IF users click ‘Request a demo’ CTA THEN user should be navigated to the <OBX PERE> /request-demo page and the page opens in the same tab.
IF users click  ‘Explore the Product’ CTA  THEN user should be navigated to the OBX PERE Homepage.
Ensure test data-ids are added to all components on the marketing takeover overlay.

1, Conferences & Events
Heading
Event Description
Agenda setting conferences and events
Speak with our team to explore our global event calendar and see how to maximise your access through your membership.
Request a Demo CTA
Preferential rates 
Secure tickets for your team at member-only prices
High-value networking
Connect with investors, managers, advisors and policymakers
Attend exclusive events
Participate in the industry's most influential forums
FAQ - Questions & Answers
What does a PEI Private Real Estate membership include?

Depending on the option you choose, your membership provides full access to real-time news, deep market analysis, 14,000+ fund profiles, 9,500+ investor profiles, 34,000 LP commitments, performance benchmarking tools, and $30bn of tracked deals, as well as access to our Private Real Estate community via our network events and content-rich conferences
Who is the platform designed for?

PEI Private Real Estate supports fund managers, investors, and service providers who need data-driven intelligence, performance analytics, deal insights, and networking opportunities to make smarter investment decisions.
How often is the data updated?

Data is updated continuously across fundraising, investor commitments, deals, fund performance, and news - ensuring you always have current, accurate insights for decision-making.
Which membership is right for me?

Base Membership offers access to core news, analysis, data profiles, Ask PEI (our AI interface) and a network of over 5,000 private real estate investment professionals.
       Unlimited Membership provides uncapped database access, full functionality, and export capability for all licensed users. This Unlimited Membership is the best choice for teams who want maximum value from the PEI Private Real Estate platform to support all areas of your investing workflow..
 Can I try the platform before joining?

Yes. You can request a demo to explore features and workflows. Free registration also unlocks basic tools like watchlists and selected content previews.
Do you offer team or enterprise discounts?

Yes. Discounts are available for teams of 5+ users, and customised enterprise packages can provide broader access, integrations, and tailored support

description:
Background
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, we are introducing a marketing landing page for the OBX PERE site, with its own dedicated URL to showcase PEI's value proposition, product range and available subscriptions. To build this marketing page, we will be using a tool called 
Builder.io
.
User Story
As a
 PEI Marketer,
I want 
the data coverage section to be imported and displayed on the marketing landing page,
So that 
customers can get a preview of the breadth of PEI’s data
Scope 
To import the Data Coverage into 
builder.io
 for the OBX PERE marketing landing page.
Heading and description
Investors data card
Funds data card
LP commitments data card
Deal value data card
The marketer must be able to update these assets in 
http://Builder.io
 .
Out of Scope
Data cards hyperlink for Investors, Funds, LP commitments & Deal value. This will be handled separately. 
Business Rules
The data coverage section should be displayed above the editorial coverage section on the marketing landing page.
Ensure the data coverage section of the marketing landing page is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
The heading in the data coverage section should be implemented with H2 tag and read as
 ‘
Vital intelligence’
.
The description in the data coverage section should read as 
'Our comprehensive database covers the entire private real estate investment ecosystem, from fundraising and deals to investor relationships and fund performance.’
The card labels Investors, Funds, LP commitment & Deal value should be implemented with a H3 tag.
Ensure each card displayed in the data coverage section on the marketing page is hyperlinked.
IF user clicks a data card displayed in this section, THEN they should be navigated to the relevant database page. 
Investor data card - URL/Slug 
Funds data card -  URL/Slug 
LP commitments data card - URL/Slug 
Deal value data card - URL/Slug 
The page should open in the same tab.
The marketer must be able to update the following assets in 
builder.io
Heading
Description 
Metrics 
Data card labels
Data card URLs 
Ensure data test ids are added for this section of the marketing landing page.
Ensure this Figma link is used for the import 
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
UX / UI 
Marketing Landing Page > Data Coverage Section
Figma Link >
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Dependency - URL/slug for the hyperlinking of cards is dependent on the Slug to be defined and implemented by ST11. 
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Data Coverage Section Rendered Correctly
GIVEN 
that I am a PEI marketer,
AND 
the data section has successfully been imported from Figma into 
builder.io
,
WHEN
 I navigate to the marketing landing page,
THEN
 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND 
the section heading should be implemented with a H2 tag
AND 
the card labels Investors, Funds, LP commitment & Deal value should be implemented with a H3 tag.
AND 
the data content should be displayed correctly as mentioned above,
AND 
the correct meta data should be displayed.
Scenario 2 - Update the Data Coverage Section in 

  
GIVEN
 scenario 1,
WHEN
 I navigate to 
builder.io
,
THEN
 I should be able to update the heading, description, metric, data card label and data card URL
 
Scenario 3 - Hyperlinks Working Correctly
GIVEN
 scenario 1
WHEN
 I click on card displayed in the data coverage section of the marketing landing page,
THEN
 I should be navigated to the relevant database page
AND
 the page should open in a same tab

title: Update II and Classic Footer for Sponsorship Opportunities
description:
Background
The sponsorship opportunities link in the footer of the OBX II new platform and the classic rewritten footer is currently pointing to an outdated version. The link must be updated to the 2027 version across both footers.
User Story
AS A
 user clicking the sponsorship opportunities link in the OBX II footer,

I WANT
 to be taken to the correct 2027 sponsorship opportunities page,

SO THAT
 I am not directed to an outdated or incorrect destination.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to update the sponsorship opportunities link in the OBX II new platform footer and the classic rewritten footer to the 2027 URL.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the footer design or layout
Any changes to any other footer links
Business Rules:
The sponsorship opportunities link in the OBX II new platform footer must be updated to: 
https://info.pei.group/II-Acquisition-IIGS-2027-Sponsorship-2027-8360_IIGS-resource-request-Spex-2.html
The sponsorship opportunities link in the classic rewritten footer must be updated to the same URL.
The link text must remain unchanged — only the destination URL must be updated.
NFRs:
No regression to any other footer links or footer functionality as a result of this change.
Add data test IDs to the result card.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
No design changes expected. The link text must remain as-is with only the destination URL updated.
 
Figma Link:  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
This ticket is dependent on the search results page layout ticket [

 ] being completed first.
Acceptance Criteria
We need to update on the II new platform footer and classic.  
I need to update the sponsorship opportunities link to the 2027 version which can be found here: 
https://info.pei.group/II-Acquisition-IIGS-2027-Sponsorship-2027-8360_IIGS-resource-request-Spex-2.html

title: Setup signed off OBX-PERE Domain (pei-privaterealestate.com)
description:
Background
The PERE OBX platform was initially set up and deployed on a temporary domain [

 ] while the final domain was being confirmed by the business. The business has now signed off on the production domain: 
pei-privaterealestate.com
. This ticket covers the migration from the temporary domain to the confirmed production domain and ensures the platform is correctly configured and accessible on the new domain.
USER STORY 
AS A
 developer and user accessing the PERE OBX platform,

I WANT
 the platform to be accessible on the confirmed production domain 
pei-privaterealestate.com
,

SO THAT
 the platform is ready for use on its official domain ahead of launch.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to replicate the II OBX platform as a bare-bones skeleton for PERE OBX, including the header, footer, and basic page structure. The skeleton must be accessible via a temporary domain until the final domain is confirmed by the business.
Outcomes: 
Set up PERE OBX as a new brand within the existing monorepo to ensure that the codebase recognizes it as a distinct site.
The new PERE OBX site will have the same layout as the II OBX including header, footer, and no functionalities added/configured.
Devops support will be needed for the 
Domain Setup & Deployment Pipeline
 
The site will be deployed on a temporary domain until the final domain is confirmed by the business.
Basic auth will be applied to the temporary domain to prevent unintended public access prior to launch.
NFRs
Responsive across the 375, 720, 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Regression Safety for II OBX 
- The shared codebase must be structured so that PERE OBX and II OBX configurations are clearly separated to minimize the risk of unintended cross-brand impact. - [
SPIKE?
]
Out of Scope
Any PERE OBX-specific features, content, or branding beyond the bare-bones skeleton
SSO login user flow
Any changes to the II OBX platform as part of this work
Final domain configuration (to be addressed once confirmed by the business)
Any data or API integrations specific to PERE OBX
UX/UI
No new design required at this stage. The skeleton must replicate the existing II OBX structure as a starting point for future PERE OBX design work.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks/ Open Questions
Dependencies - DevOps  to deploy the new temporary domain 
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: PERE OBX Skeleton Available
GIVEN 
the implementation has been completed,

WHEN 
the temporary domain is accessed,

THEN 
a bare-bones PERE OBX skeleton is rendered, including header, footer, and basic page structure replicating the II OBX platform.
AC2: Temporary Domain Accessible
GIVEN 
the skeleton has been deployed,

WHEN 
the temporary domain is accessed by an authorised user,

THEN 
the PERE OBX skeleton loads correctly on the temporary domain.
AC3: II OBX Site Unaffected
GIVEN 
the PERE OBX skeleton has been implemented within the shared codebase,

WHEN 
the II OBX site is accessed and reviewed,

THEN 
the existing II OBX site is fully functional with no regressions or unintended changes introduced as a result of this work.
AC4: Brand Configuration Separated
GIVEN 
the shared codebase is reviewed,

WHEN 
II OBX and PERE OBX configurations are inspected,

THEN 
brand-specific configuration is clearly separated so that changes to one brand do not unintentionally affect the other.
AC5: Basic Auth on Temporary Domain
GIVEN 
the skeleton has been deployed to the temporary domain,

WHEN 
an unauthorized user attempts to access it,

THEN 
basic authentication is enforced and access is restricted to authorized users only.
AC6: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user accesses the PERE OBX skeleton at 375px, 720px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN 
the page renders,

THEN 
the header, footer, and basic page structure are correctly displayed across all supported breakpoints.

title: OBX PERE - Registration Page (Anonymous Users)- Zephr Linking to PERE Web Activities / Hooks
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story
As a
 Sales Manager
I want 
the registration form to be configured for OBX PERE
So that
 it can be integrated in Zephr with PERE specific web activities. 
Scope 
To link the new registration form on OBX PERE with Zephr/CRM to create a first-time user account in Zephr.
Business Rules: 
IF a prospect successfully submits the registration form, AND no existing user account is found in Zephr for the provided email address, THEN a new user account contact should be automatically created in Zephr, AND a web activity should be created against that contact in Zephr 
and linked to the existing web activity in CRM.
The following Zephr web activity attributes should be sent to CRM.
 Web activity brand
 
=
 
“PEIPRE” (Dev & Stage) (This is set in AWS Parameter store and is not part of the ticket) 
The Web Activity Sub-Type =
 This is set in the Zephr webhook.
Ensure that the web activity is associated only with the PERE product and does not map to any other brand.
IF a prospect successfully submits the registration form, AND an existing user account is already present in Zephr for the provided email address, THEN no new user account should be created in Zephr.
On form submission in this scenario, the existing error message should be displayed
 “This email address is already registered. Sign in or use another email address.”
Note: A free account should be created in Zephr when prospect successfully submits the form and relevant newsletters should be sent and should work as per existing Zephr flow.
The existing CRM web activity rules should be triggered based on the above web activity attributes. No changes are required to the existing CRM flow.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Successfully Submit Registration Form and Trigger Zephr/CRM Flow
GIVEN 
that I am an anonymous user on OBX PERE site
AND 
I enter all mandatory fields on the registration form,
WHEN 
I successfully submit the registration request,
THEN 
IF no existing user account is found in Zephr for the provided email address, a new user account should be created in Zephr,
AND 
a web activity should be created in Zephr against the account created and linked to the existing web activity in CRM,
AND 
Zephr should send the above web activity attributes to CRM
AND 
the existing CRM rules should be triggered based on these web activity attributes,
AND 
a free account should be created as per the existing Zephr flow
AND 
the web activity is associated only with the PERE product 
Scenario 2 - Existing User Registration Attempt
GIVEN 
that I am an anonymous user on OBX PERE site
AND 
I enter an email address that already exists in Zephr,
WHEN 
I submit the registration form,
THEN 
no new user account should be created in Zephr,
AND 
the the already implemented error message should be displayed: “This email address is already registered. 
Sign in 
or use another email address.”

title: SEO (on-live) - crawler to view articles / hompages with a single H1 tag
description:
Background
 Identified via post-GA Screaming Frog crawl (text-only) supplied by PEI. Single-component fix; high-leverage. Cross-reference: see Multiple H1 row (this single fix resolves the bulk of that finding). Open Brain entry created 13 May 2026.
Issue: 
On OBX-templated article pages, the H1 element contains the site brand name 'PEI Infrastructure Investor' (emitted from a site-logo wrapper component), not the article headline. The article headline is rendered as H2 instead. Affects 21,051 articles — essentially the entire OBX article corpus. SEO impact: H1 is a primary topical signal. Identical brand-name H1s across 21k articles flatten topical differentiation site-wide and make article-level keyword relevance harder for Google to identify. This is the root cause of most of the 'Multiple H1 per page' finding (see related row) — the brand-name H1 from the logo wrapper plus the article headline (which should be the H1) produces two H1s per page.
Impacts
 
Artcile pages
Homepage
Solution:
Single templating change at the site-logo wrapper component in the OBX article template. The brand-name element should not be wrapped in an H1 tag (use a span, div, or aria-label-only logo image instead). The article headline element should be the H1. One component fix resolves this finding and the related Multiple H1 finding simultaneously
Pre-Requisite
 OBX-II is released
USER STORY 
As a crawler viewing the OBX II article pages and homepage

I want to be able to see single H1 tag on the article page

So that Google's initial crawl sees the structured data and doesn’t create duplicate H1 on the page
Scope
Business Rules
To ensure the artcile page reflects a single h1 tag for the Article Title only
To ensure, when in mobile responsive mode, for article pages with Article and Data tab, should demote semantic markup from H1 tags to a div, as this impacts google crawler 
Hompage data tabs will also be impacted due to this, where the JBTD data section will  demote semantic markup from H1 tags to a div,
Out of Scope
None
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
UI - Article Page
UI - Homepage 
SEO Audit 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
None
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: OBX II article pages should have a single H1 tag only
Given I am on the OBX II article page
WHEN I view the content that has both Article and Data 

Then the Article and Data tabs should not be H1 tag as per SEO guidelines
AC2: OBX II Homepage  should have a single H1 tag only
Given I am on the OBX II Homepage page
WHEN I view the content of JBTD section in mobile view
Then the JBTD tabs should not be H1 tag as per SEO guidelines

title: PERE OBX > Homepage > Bento - News & Analysis Hero Tile (backend + integration)
description:
Background
This story wires up the backend + integration to surface live news articles in the carousel.
For PERE, the carousel follows the same "OBX Primary tag" approach used on II, but content is sourced across 
three PERE WordPress sources
 — PERE News, PERE Credit, and PERE Deals — with 
2 articles from each source
 (6 articles in total).
Pre-Requisite
Domain of PERE needs to be setup and it must be up and running
Across stage, dev and prod
Hero Carousel frontend UI is already built (same as II) and live on stage
All three PERE WordPress sources (PERE News, PERE Credit, PERE Deals) are configured and reachable
"OBX Primary" admin tag is available on PERE News, PERE Credit, and 
PERE Deals WordPress
 (ST3 setup) - 

 to get back
USER STORY 
As a 
logged-in / logged-out user
I want 
to see the latest editorial content tagged as OBX Primary across PERE News, Credit and Deals in the Hero Carousel on the PERE OBX homepage 
So that 
I can quickly browse prominent editorial content across all three brands
Scope
 
Wire up the backend + integration to surface live news articles in the Hero Carousel in the Bento section of the PERE OBX homepage
Business Rules
Content sourcing
Only editorial content that has the 
"OBX Primary"
 tag applied is eligible for display in the carousel (same logic as II)
The carousel must surface 6 articles in total, sourced as follows:
2 latest OBX Primary articles from 
PERE News
2 latest OBX Primary articles from 
PERE Credit
2 latest OBX Primary articles from 
PERE Deals
Articles must be ordered by publication date (latest to oldest) within each brand source
Rendering
The carousel renders 6 cards in total (2 × PERE News + 2 × PERE Credit + 2 × PERE Deals)
Each card must always display:
Category tag
Article headline / title
Standfirst / description
Reading time
Publication timestamp
Featured image — if no image is selected on the article, use the existing fallback image (existing II behaviour)
Progress bar
 is displayed above the carousel cards, indicating the current card position out of total
Carousel navigation arrows (left / right) allow the user to advance through cards
Carousel state does not persist upon navigation to other page (same as II)
Click-through
Each card click navigates the user to the corresponding article detail page on the respective brand source
Error & empty states
If a content source returns an error, the carousel renders a graceful error state with no unhandled exceptions - same as II
If fewer than 2 OBX Primary articles are available from a brand source, only the available articles from that brand source are rendered (carousel surfaces fewer than 6 total) -
Render whatever is shown
Error state existing in current II build to be checked - 

 
News and analysis (part of other icket) - 1st card to be OBX_primary
Out of Scope
Hero Carousel frontend UI changes — UI is already built and same as II
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
UI:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Assumption 
- "OBX Primary" admin tag must be available on all three PERE WordPress sources (PERE News, PERE Credit, PERE Deals)
Dependency 
- Fall back images required
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Only OBX Primary tagged articles eligible
Given
 a user navigates to the PERE OBX homepage 
When
 the Hero Carousel renders 
Then
 only editorial content with the "OBX Primary" tag applied is displayed in the carousel
AC2 — Carousel surfaces 6 articles across PERE brands
Given
 a user navigates to the PERE OBX homepage 
When
 the Hero Carousel renders 
Then
 the carousel displays 6 articles in total 
And
 2 articles are sourced from PERE News 
And
 2 articles are sourced from PERE Credit 
And
 2 articles are sourced from PERE Deals 
And
 within each brand source, articles are ordered by publication date (latest to oldest)
Rules:
If there are less then 2 articles per source
If we have 1 article - do we showcase only 1 - Yes
If we have no articles per source - do we showcase other sources with Obx_primary - Yes showcase whatever exists
AC3 — Card content renders correctly
Given
 the carousel is rendered with live data 
When
 the user views any card
Then
 the card always displays the following elements:
Category tag 
(optional?) - 

 to check on this
Article headline / title
Truncation max 2 lines
Standfirst / description
Truncation max 2 lines
Reading time
Publication timestamp
Featured image
 (or existing fallback image if none set) - 

 to get back on this
AC4 — Click-through to article detail
Given
 any card in the carousel is rendered 
When
 the user clicks on the card 
Then
 the user is navigated to the corresponding article detail page on the respective brand source
AC5— Fallback image displayed when featured image is missing
Given
 an article does not have a featured image set 
When
 the carousel renders the card for that article 
Then
 the existing fallback image is displayed in place of the featured image (existing II behaviour)
AC6 — Error and empty states
Test case 1— Progress bar reflects carousel position (Same as II)
Given
 the carousel is rendered with multiple cards
When
 the user views the carousel 
Then
 a progress bar is displayed above the carousel 
And
 the progress bar reflects the current card position out of the total
Test case 2— User can scroll through remaining content
Given
 the carousel is rendered with multiple cards 
When
 the user clicks the carousel navigation (left / right arrows) 
Then
 the user can scroll through the remaining articles in the carousel
Test case 3— Carousel state persistence across navigation
Given
 the user has advanced the carousel to a specific card 
When
 the user navigates away from the homepage and returns 
Then
 the carousel must remain at the 1st card’s position (same behavior as II)
Test case 4— Carousel renders for both user states
Given
 a user lands on the PERE OBX homepage 
When
 the user is logged-in OR logged-out 
Then
 the Hero Carousel renders identically for both states
Test case 5— No regression to existing II Hero Carousel
Given
 the existing II OBX homepage is rendered
When
 the Hero Carousel loads 
Then
 it continues to surface 4 articles tagged with OBX Primary from II WordPress with no regression
UX
AC1
 — Build to be responsive to mentioned resolutions 
AC2
 — Hover behaviour same as II (cursor changes to pointer on cards, progress bar transitions are smooth) - same II

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence > Equity-Investor Activity-Investor news > backend for latest investor news
description:
Background
The Latest Investor News component on the Equity > Investor Activity tab is currently hard-coded to fetch from the Infrastructure Investor WordPress source. This needs to be made configurable so the component can fetch from the correct brand-specific WordPress source dynamically.
UI is already built. This story covers backend changes only.
Pre-Requisite
Latest Investor News UI/layout is in place
USER STORY 
As a 
logged-in / logged-out user 
I want 
to be able to view the latest equity investor activity 
So that 
I can stay up to date with the latest investor activity in the equity market
Scope
Update the WordPress source for the Latest Investor News component to remove the hard-coded Infrastructure Investor reference. Data should be fetched dynamically by filtering on the WordPress category "investors", making the component reusable across different brands.
Business Rules
Remove the hard-coded Infrastructure Investor (
DATA_SOURCE_BRAND.II
) reference from the data fetch
Brand prop must be accepted and passed down to the data fetch layer
WordPress source must be mapped dynamically based on the brand prop (PERE → PERE WordPress source, II → II WordPress source)
Data fetch must filter on WordPress category = "investors"
Rules - Existing II Latest Investor News must continue to fetch correctly with no regression
Out of Scope
Frontend integration with the PERE homepage (covered in separate story)
UI changes — UI is already built
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike reference - 

 
UI: 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Open questions for Design:
UX logic is same as II — first card to have image, remaining 4 cards on right
Sort order — latest to oldest by publication date
Note from spike:
 Per spike findings, 
fetch-latest-investor-news.ts
 currently hardcodes 
DATA_SOURCE_BRAND.II
 on all three exported functions (
fetchLatestOBXPrimaryInvestorNews
, 
fetchLatestInvestorNews
, 
fetchLatestContentNews
). All three need the same brand → data source mapping fix as 
fetch-latest-fundraising-news.ts
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Latest Investor News backend accepts brand prop
 
GIVEN 
a developer triggers the Latest Investor News data fetch 
WHEN 
the fetch is called with a brand prop (e.g. PERE or II) 
THEN 
the data fetch maps to the correct WordPress source based on the brand
AC2 — Data fetch filters on "investors" category
 
GIVEN 
the data fetch is triggered for any brand 
WHEN 
the WordPress query runs 
THEN 
only articles tagged in the WordPress category "investors" are returned
AC3 — PERE Latest Investor News fetches from PERE source
 
GIVEN 
the PERE homepage is rendered 
WHEN 
the Latest Investor News component fetches data with brand = PERE 
THEN 
it returns "investors" content from the PERE WordPress source
Rules:
No breaking changes to existing II behaviour
Test case— II Latest Investor News continues to fetch correctly
 
GIVEN 
the existing II homepage is rendered 
WHEN 
the Latest Investor News component fetches data 
THEN 
it continues to return Infrastructure Investor "investors" content with no regression

title: PERE OBX > Homepage > Bento Box > to develop backend for Data Snapshot
description:
Background
The Data Snapshot component on the homepage is currently hard-coded to fetch from the Infrastructure Investor WordPress source. This needs to be made configurable so the component can fetch data dynamically based on the active brand (PERE and future brands).
UI is same as II and is already rendering on stage. This story covers backend changes only.
Pre-Requisite
"Data Snapshot" taxonomy created in WordPress Content Type and available in GraphQL on the 
PERE WordPress source
Data Snapshot UI/layout is in place on stage (same as II)
USER STORY 
As a
 developer
I want
 the Data Snapshot component to dynamically fetch from the correct WordPress source based on the active brand 
So that 
the component can be reused across PERE and future OBX brands without code changes
Scope
 
Update the WordPress source for the Data Snapshot component to make it configurable rather than hard-coded for Infrastructure Investor. The source should be dynamically driven so it can be reused across different brands without requiring code changes.
 
Business Rules
Remove the hard-coded Infrastructure Investor reference from the Data Snapshot data fetch
Brand prop must be accepted by the Data Snapshot component and passed down to the data fetch layer
WordPress source must be mapped dynamically based on the brand prop (PERE → PERE WordPress source, II → II WordPress source)
Only articles tagged with "Content Type: Data Snapshot" on the relevant WordPress source are eligible for display
The component displays exactly ONE article at a time (the most recent)
When a new Data Snapshot article is published on the relevant WordPress source, it automatically replaces the previous featured article

title: To configure/whitelist the articles (Rewrites)
description:
Background
Some PERE pages will continue to be served from the legacy WordPress site during the migration period. These pages should still present the new PERE top nav and footer so that users have a consistent experience across the site.
Pre-Requisite
Fusion nav bar component is implemented and available in the shared component library (reference: II nav bar implementation)
PEI PERE logo asset is available and signed off by design
PERE brand colour tokens are configured in the Fusion design system
Figma designs for the PERE nav bar structure are signed off
USER STORY 
As a product manager,

 I want legacy pages that are not yet migrated to OBX to be served with the correct nav and footer,

 so that users have a consistent experience across the site without being aware they are viewing legacy content.
Scope
Business Rules
IF the incoming URL matches an entry in the hardcoded rewrite list, THEN the request is rewritten to serve the legacy page content
IF the incoming URL does not match any entry in the hardcoded rewrite list, THEN the request proceeds with standard OBX rendering
IF a page is served via rewrite, THEN the OBX top nav and PERE footer are applied to the page
Out of Scope
Domain redirects -any work to change or redirect the domain a page is served from is not covered by this story
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

PERE (MVP v2)
 -UX prototype

LOGO SVG:

UX:
UI: (TO BE RECIEVED]

Pages Known
Data Pages
Network Pages
Landing Pages (Reports, Awards,etc)
Author Landing Page
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
Functionality
GIVEN a user navigates to a URL that exists in the rewrite list, WHEN the page loads, THEN the legacy page content is served under the new domain
GIVEN a user navigates to a URL that does not exist in the rewrite list, WHEN the page loads, THEN the page renders via standard OBX
GIVEN a page is served via rewrite, WHEN the page loads, THEN the OBX top nav and PERE footer are visible on the page
UX Behaviour
GIVEN a rewrite is triggered, WHEN the page loads, THEN there is no visible redirect or flash,  the content renders directly under the new URL
Whitelisting/n/n/n

WHITLISTED pages should also have the new domain, new url structure, and have the OBX nav anD footer 
 URL structure and implement domain/equity/ slug and domain/credit/slug format for all articles.
IF the requested URL is a WP post THEN serve on OBX/Fusion ELSE  any other content should be served from legacy.

title: Data Code changes, Integration with New Data source PERE news and Pere credit
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, article pages for PERE News and PERE Credit must render data panels where relevant. The data panel behaviour and logic mirrors the existing II implementation, with the key difference that the data source is a PERE-specific database shared across both PERE News and PERE Credit articles.
This ticket covers the implementation of the Investor data panel and Fund data panel on PERE article pages.
Pre-Requisite
II data panel implementation is complete and available as reference
PERE-specific database is available and accessible
Figma designs for the PERE data panel are signed off
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE News or PERE Credit article

 I WANT to see the relevant investor or fund data panel where applicable

 SO THAT I can access key entity information in context without leaving the article page
Scope
Implement the following data panels on PERE News and PERE Credit article pages, sourcing data from the shared PERE database. Behaviour and logic mirrors the II implementation.
Investor data panel Displays the following fields where data is available: Recent news, Investor allocation, AUM, Fund Commitments, Investor contacts.
Fund data panel Displays the following fields where data is available: Fund Name, Fund Focus, Investor Commitments, Launch Year, Key Contacts.
For all breakpoints.
Business Rules
BR1: Investor Data Panel
 

IF an investor is marked as Primary About on the article, 

THEN the Investor data panel must be displayed with data sourced from the PERE database. Where data is available, the following fields must be displayed: Recent News, Investor Allocation, AUM, Fund Commitments, Investor Contacts.
BR2: Fund Data Panel

 IF a fund is marked as Primary About on the article,

 THEN the Fund data panel must be displayed with data sourced from the PERE database. Where data is available, the following fields must be displayed: Fund Name, Fund Focus, Investor Commitments, Launch Year, Key Contacts.
BR3: Empty Fields

 IF data is not available for a specific field within a data panel, THEN that field must not be rendered.
BR4: Data Panel Not Received
 

IF data is not received for the data panel, THEN the data panel must not be rendered and all other components on the page must be unaffected.
BR5: Design

 IF a data panel is displayed, THEN it must render in the correct position and style per Figma.
BR6: Heap Tracking

 IF a data panel is rendered, THEN a Heap event must fire on every clickable element within the panel.
Technical Feasibility 
The existing II data panel component can be reused for PERE with the PERE database configured as the data source.
The data source is a PERE-specific database shared across both PERE News and PERE Credit articles.BRAND: PERE 
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: Investor data panel and Fund data panel must render per Figma.
UX: Data panel behaviour must be consistent with the II implementation across all supported article template types and across all breakpoints
NFR / CFR 
Analytics: Heap event IDs to be implemented on all clickable elements including primary category badge, tags, and CTAs
Performance: Article page render performance must be consistent with II article page benchmarks
Out of Scope
Article configuration chages
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

title: Article Code changes, Integration with New Data source PERE news and Pere credit [ALL WORK EXCEPT UPCOMING EVENTS]
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, PERE News and PERE Credit articles must render on the OBX platform. 

Articles across both publications must render in the same way as II articles on OBX, with PERE-specific configurations applied where the behaviour differs from II.

The key differences are that PERE News and PERE Credit articles are sourced from their respective WordPress instances.

This ticket covers all implementation work required for PERE News and PERE Credit articles to render correctly on OBX. QA and testing are covered in separate tickets.
Pre-Requisite
II article page implementation is complete and available as reference 

 
PERE News and PERE Credit WordPress instances are available and accessible
Figma designs for the PERE article page are signed off
OBX Article Layout to WP Admin mapping (Table 1) is confirmed
Fundraising and Investor CTA target URLs are confirmed by product
New URL structures for PERE News and PERE Credit are confirmed
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE News or PERE Credit article on OBX

 I WANT the article to render correctly with the right category, tags, related content, and CTAs

SO THAT I can read and navigate the content as expected
Scope
This ticket covers the configuration work required to render PERE News and PERE Credit articles on the OBX platform, matching the existing Infrastructure Investor (II) article page implementation.
When a PERE News or PERE Credit article is loaded, the data must be sourced from the respective PERE WordPress instance and mapped to the correct layout per its template.  (Refer Table 1)
The following are in scope:
Article page layout and component rendering (header, body, tags, breadcrumb, related news, RHS)
Primary Category, Exclusive, and general tag display
Investor and Fundraising CTA display, driven by article taxonomy terms
Key Mentions and Latest News RHS components
URL structure and 301 redirects from legacy PERE URLs
Fallback and error state handling (missing featured image, WP API failure, article not found, missing component data)
Business Rules
BR1: Data Sourcing and Layout
 IF a PERE News or PERE Credit article is loaded, THEN the data must be sourced from the respective PERE WordPress instance and mapped to the correct layout per its template, including all components listed below.
Article page layout and content:

 

 

 

 
Primary Category Tag display: WEB-25381
Exclusive Tag: WEB-25382
Display of tags: WEB-25962
BR2: URL Structure and Redirects
IF a user visits a PERE News article, THEN the URL must follow the structure domain/equity/[slug].
IF a user visits a PERE Credit article, THEN the URL must follow the structure domain/credit/[slug].
IF a user visits an existing legacy URL for a PERE News or PERE Credit article, THEN they must be redirected to the correct new URL via a 301 redirect.
BR3: Primary Category Tag
IF a primary category can be determined for the article, THEN the primary category badge must render above the headline following the defined hierarchy, per Figma.
IF no primary category can be determined, THEN no category tag is displayed.
IF the primary category badge is displayed, THEN it must be clickable and redirect the user to the primary taxonomy page on the legacy site in the same tab.
BR4: Exclusive Tags
 IF an article has exclusive tags, THEN they must render in the correct position and style per Figma.
BR5: Display of Tags
IF an article has tags assigned, THEN all tags must be displayed at the end of the article body per Figma.
IF no tags are present, THEN the tags section must not be rendered.
BR6: Investor and Fundraising CTAs
 
(Ref: WEB-25550. Use II links until actual PERE links are confirmed.)
IF the article taxonomy term includes Fundraising, THEN the Fundraising CTA must be displayed and open the correct target URL.
IF the article taxonomy term includes Investor, THEN the Investor CTA must be displayed and open the correct target URL.
IF both Fundraising and Investor taxonomy terms are present, THEN both CTAs must be displayed together.
IF an article falls under any of the four WordPress template types, THEN the CTAs must render in the correct position per OBX layout guidelines and Figma.
BR7: Related News
 Ref: WEB-25405
BR8: RHS — Latest News
 
(Ref: WEB-26396)
 IF a user visits a PERE News or PERE Credit article, THEN the RHS Latest News section must display the latest articles from News & Analysis sourced from the PERE News WordPress instance, and the currently viewed article must be excluded.
BR9: RHS — Key Mentions
 
(Ref: WEB-25551)
 IF any profiles have been marked as About or Mentions in the R&A Profile section, THEN they must be rendered per Figma and the II implementation.
BR10: Primary Category Carousel (Related News)
IF the Latest in [Primary Category] carousel is displayed, THEN it must render a maximum of 7 articles sorted newest to oldest, with a maximum of 4 full article cards visible at a time.
IF the Primary Category is News & Analysis, THEN the 3 articles displayed in the RHS Latest News section must also be excluded from the carousel.
The currently viewed article must always be excluded.
BR11: Breadcrumb
 
(Ref: WEB-25437)
IF a user is reading a PERE News or PERE Credit article, THEN the breadcrumb must render following the same logic as II.
IF the article's Primary Category belongs to the Network parent group, or no Primary Category is assigned, THEN no breadcrumb is displayed.
BR12: Exceptions
IF the featured image is missing, THEN the fallback image must be displayed. 
(WEB-25399) - Image iin supporting documentation
IF the WP API fails, THEN the error page must render in the same way as on II. 
(WEB-27990)
IF the article is not found, THEN the designed "not found" error page must be displayed. 
(WEB-27991)
IF data is not received for a component, THEN that component must not be rendered and all other components on the page must be unaffected.
Out of Scope
Code changes for Data panels

Testing on this ticket
IF a user visits an existing legacy URL for a PERE News or PERE Credit article, THEN they must be redirected to the correct new URL via a 301 redirect. [ST8]

title: to be able to read the PERE CREDIT articles as per the OBX fusion layouts [TESTING FOR PERE CREDIT ARTICLE]
description:
Background
PERE Credit articles must render on the OBX/Fusion platform following the same layout and component logic established for Infrastructure Investor, with PERE Credit-specific configurations applied. Content is sourced from the PERE Credit WordPress instance; data panels are driven by the shared PERE-specific database.
Pre-Requisite
II OBX article page implementation complete (ref: WEB-25400, WEB-26033)
PERE News article page ticket complete and available as reference
PERE Credit WordPress instance accessible in the target environment
PERE-specific database accessible
Figma designs for PERE Credit article page signed off
Article layout-to-WP template mapping (Table 1) confirmed for PERE Credit
URL structure confirmed: 
domain/credit/[slug]
Fundraising and Investor CTA target URLs confirmed (use II links until confirmed)
Fallback image asset available (ref: WEB-25399)
Error page designs signed off (ref: WEB-27990, WEB-27991)
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE Credit article on OBX,

 I WANT the article to render correctly with all expected components,

 SO THAT I can read and navigate the content as expected on the new platform.
Scope
Same as PERE News article page ticket, with the following difference:
Content sourced from the 
PERE Credit WordPress instance
URL structure: 
domain/credit/[slug]
301 redirects from legacy PERE Credit URLs
Business Rules
BR1 — Data Sourcing and Layout
 IF a PERE Credit article is loaded, THEN data must be sourced from the PERE Credit WP instance and mapped to the correct layout per its template type (Table 1). IF no template type is assigned, THEN the standard article layout applies as fallback.
BR2 — URL Structure and Redirects
 IF a user visits a PERE Credit article, THEN the URL must follow 
domain/credit/[slug]
. IF a user visits a legacy PERE Credit URL, THEN a 301 redirect must route to the correct new URL.
BR3 — Primary Category Tag
 IF a primary category is determined, THEN the badge renders above the headline per the defined four-level hierarchy and Figma, and links to the primary taxonomy page on the legacy site in the same tab. IF no primary category is determined, THEN no badge is displayed. (Ref: WEB-25381)
BR4 — Exclusive Tags
 IF an article has exclusive tags, THEN they render in the correct position and style per Figma. (Ref: WEB-25382)
BR5 — General Tags
 IF tags are present, THEN they render at the end of the article body per Figma. IF no tags are present, THEN the tags section is not rendered. (Ref: WEB-25962)
BR6 — Investor and Fundraising CTAs
 IF the article taxonomy includes Fundraising, THEN the Fundraising CTA renders and opens the correct target URL. IF the article taxonomy includes Investor, THEN the Investor CTA renders and opens the correct target URL. IF both are present, THEN both CTAs render together in the correct position per Figma. (Ref: WEB-25550)
BR7 — Related News
 Ref: WEB-25405
BR8 — RHS Latest News
 IF a user views a PERE Credit article, THEN the RHS Latest News section displays the latest News & Analysis articles from the PERE Credit WP instance, excluding the currently viewed article. (Ref: WEB-26396)
BR9 — RHS Key Mentions
 IF profiles are marked as About or Mentions in the R&A Profile section, THEN they render per Figma and the II implementation. (Ref: WEB-25551)
BR10 — Primary Category Carousel
 IF the Latest in [Primary Category] carousel is displayed, THEN it renders a maximum of 7 articles sorted newest to oldest, with a maximum of 4 full cards visible at a time. IF the Primary Category is News & Analysis, THEN the 3 RHS Latest News articles are also excluded. The currently viewed article is always excluded.
BR11 — Breadcrumb
 IF the article has an assigned Primary Category outside the Network parent group, THEN the breadcrumb renders following the same logic as II. IF the Primary Category belongs to the Network parent group or is absent, THEN no breadcrumb is displayed. (Ref: WEB-25437)
BR12 — Investor Data Panel
 IF an investor is marked as Primary About, THEN the Investor data panel renders with data from the PERE database. Available fields: Recent News, Investor Allocation, AUM, Fund Commitments, Investor Contacts. Unavailable fields are not rendered.
BR13 — Fund Data Panel
 IF a fund is marked as Primary About, THEN the Fund data panel renders with data from the PERE database. Available fields: Fund Name, Fund Focus, Investor Commitments, Launch Year, Key Contacts. Unavailable fields are not rendered.
BR14 — Error States
 IF the featured image is missing, THEN the fallback image renders. (Ref: WEB-25399) IF the WP API fails, THEN the error page renders per II behaviour. (Ref: WEB-27990) IF the article is not found, THEN the not-found error page renders. (Ref: WEB-27991) IF data is not received for any component, THEN that component is suppressed without affecting others.
Technical Feasibility 
II article page template config extended to cover PERE Credit template types
II data panel component reused with the PERE-specific database as the data source
PERE Credit implementation follows the same pattern as PERE News; data source and URL structure are the primary deltas
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: Investor data panel and Fund data panel must render per Figma.
UX: Data panel behaviour must be consistent with the II implementation across all supported article template types and across all breakpoints
NFR / CFR 
Heap event IDs on all clickable elements: primary category badge, tags, CTAs, breadcrumb links, data panel elements
Article page render performance consistent with II benchmarks
All components render correctly  across breakpoints (375px / 1024px / 1440px / 1920px)
Out of Scope
Paywall, Network Insights articles, data panel design changes from II pattern
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Confluence:

Sample test data:
UX:
UI:
Article page design shared with Marketing to add semantics- 

SEO requirements sheet
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Identify Assumptions, risks, and dependencies during requirements gathering for use cases and user stories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA 

AC1 — Article renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE Credit article on OBX WHEN the page loads THEN the headline, body, primary category badge, tags, breadcrumb, and byline render per Figma AND components with no data are suppressed without affecting others
AC2 — URL and redirects
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE Credit article WHEN the page loads THEN the URL follows 
domain/credit/[slug]
 AND legacy URLs 301-redirect to the correct new URL
AC3 — CTAs
 GIVEN an article with Fundraising and/or Investor taxonomy terms WHEN the page loads THEN the corresponding CTA(s) render in the correct position per Figma and open the correct target URL
AC4 — Data panels
 GIVEN an article with a Primary About set in WP WHEN the page loads THEN the correct Investor or Fund data panel renders with available fields only, sourced from the PERE database, per Figma
AC5 — RHS Latest News
 GIVEN a user is reading a PERE Credit article WHEN the page loads THEN the RHS Latest News displays the latest News & Analysis from the PERE Credit WP instance, excluding the current article
AC6 — Primary Category Carousel
 GIVEN the Latest in [Primary Category] carousel is displayed WHEN the page loads THEN a maximum of 7 articles render sorted newest to oldest, with the current article excluded AND if Primary Category is News & Analysis, the 3 RHS Latest News articles are also excluded
AC7 — Error states
 GIVEN the featured image is missing, THEN the fallback image renders GIVEN the WP API fails, THEN the error page renders per II behaviour GIVEN the article is not found, THEN the not-found page renders
AC8 — Meta tags render from WordPress
 GIVEN meta title, meta description, and canonical tag are set in WP for a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the OBX page renders the correct meta title, meta description, and canonical tag from the PERE News WP instance
AC9 — Meta tags auto-update from WordPress
 GIVEN the meta fields for a PERE News article are updated in WP and the article is republished WHEN the OBX article page is loaded THEN the updated meta title, meta description, and canonical tag are reflected without manual intervention
AC10 — Default meta tags when no WP values are set
 GIVEN a PERE News article uses any editorial template (A, B, C, or D) AND no meta title, meta description, or canonical tag has been manually set in WP WHEN the page is rendered THEN the meta title is set to the article title AND the meta description is left for Google to pull from the article body AND the canonical tag is self-referring
AC11 — Meta tags sourced from correct WP environment
 GIVEN the OBX platform is running in a staging environment WHEN a PERE News article page loads THEN metadata is fetched from the PERE News staging WP instance, not production
AC12 — JSON-LD structured data
 GIVEN a user navigates to a PERE News article page WHEN the page loads THEN the page HTML contains a JSON-LD script tag with schema metadata matching the data returned from the GraphQL endpoint
AC13 — Semantic heading structure
 GIVEN a PERE News article page is rendered WHEN the page markup is inspected THEN there is exactly one H1 per page AND heading tags H2–H6 follow a correct hierarchical structure with no levels skipped AND all heading semantics conform to the SEO team specifications (ref: SEO requirements sheet)
