title: to be able to read the PERE news articles as per the OBX fusion layouts [TESTING FOR PERE NEWS ARTICLE]
description:
Background
PERE News articles must render on the OBX/Fusion platform following the same layout and component logic established for Infrastructure Investor, with PERE News-specific configurations applied. Content is sourced from the PERE News WordPress instance; data panels are driven by the shared PERE-specific database.
Pre-Requisite
II OBX article page implementation complete (ref: WEB-25400, WEB-26033)
PERE News WordPress instance accessible in the target environment
PERE-specific database accessible
Figma designs for PERE News article page and data panels signed off
Article layout-to-WP template mapping (Table 1) confirmed
URL structure confirmed: domain/equity/[slug]
Fundraising and Investor CTA target URLs confirmed (use II links until confirmed)
Fallback image asset available (ref: WEB-25399)
Error page designs signed off (ref: WEB-27990, WEB-27991)
ST3 to expose multiple authors via GraphQL (ref: WEB-26406)
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE News article on OBX,

 I WANT the article to render correctly with all expected components,

SO THAT I can read and navigate the content as expected on the new platform.
Scope
Article layout and component rendering (header, body, tags, breadcrumb, RHS)
Primary Category, Exclusive, and general tag display
Investor and Fundraising CTA display driven by article taxonomy
Investor and Fund data panels sourced from the PERE-specific database
Key Mentions and Latest News RHS components
Follow and Share functionality
Fallback and error state handling
Business Rules
BR1: Data Sourcing and Layout
 IF a PERE News article is loaded, THEN data must be sourced from the PERE News WP instance and mapped to the correct layout per its template type per Table 1. IF no template type is assigned, THEN the standard article layout (Template A) applies as fallback.
BR2: URL Structure
 IF a user visits a PERE News article, THEN the URL must follow domain/equity/[slug].
BR3: Primary Category Tag
 IF a primary category is determined, THEN the badge renders above the headline per the defined four-level hierarchy and Figma, and links to the primary taxonomy page on the legacy site in the same tab. IF no primary category is determined, THEN no badge is displayed. (Ref: WEB-25381)
BR4: Exclusive Tags
 IF an article has exclusive tags, THEN they render in the correct position and style per Figma. (Ref: WEB-25382)
BR5: General Tags
 IF tags are present, THEN they render at the end of the article body per Figma. IF no tags are present, THEN the tags section is not rendered. (Ref: WEB-25962)
BR6: Investor and Fundraising CTAs
 IF the article taxonomy includes Fundraising, THEN the Fundraising CTA renders and opens the correct target URL. IF the article taxonomy includes Investor, THEN the Investor CTA renders and opens the correct target URL. IF both are present, THEN both CTAs render together in the correct position per Figma. (Ref: WEB-25550)
BR7: Related News
 Ref: WEB-25405
BR8: RHS Latest News
 IF a user views a PERE News article, THEN the RHS Latest News section displays the latest News & Analysis articles from the PERE News WP instance, excluding the currently viewed article. (Ref: WEB-26396)
BR9: RHS Key Mentions
 IF profiles are marked as About or Mentions in the R&A Profile section, THEN they render per Figma and the II implementation. (Ref: WEB-25551)
BR10: Primary Category Carousel
 IF the Latest in [Primary Category] carousel is displayed, THEN it renders a maximum of 7 articles sorted newest to oldest, with a maximum of 4 full cards visible at a time. IF the Primary Category is News & Analysis, THEN the 3 RHS Latest News articles are also excluded. The currently viewed article is always excluded.
BR11: Breadcrumb
 IF the article has an assigned Primary Category outside the Network parent group, THEN the breadcrumb renders following the same logic as II. IF the Primary Category belongs to the Network parent group or is absent, THEN no breadcrumb is displayed. (Ref: WEB-25437)
BR12: Investor Data Panel — Display Condition
 IF an editor has marked an Institution as Primary About on the article AND the partner status is [LP] or [LP, GP], THEN the Investor data panel must be displayed with the header "Know the investor." and the full institution name. IF the above condition is not met, THEN the Investor data panel must not be displayed. IF the institution name exceeds one line, THEN it must wrap per design. IF a user clicks the investor name, THEN they must be taken to the Investor's full profile page on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR13: Investor Data Panel — Current Allocation (Graphical)
 IF allocation data exists for the investor, THEN a graphical chart must be displayed showing distribution across asset classes with assigned colours, in descending order left to right, with "Other" always last and asset classes with no holdings excluded. IF no allocation data exists, THEN the entire Current Allocation section must not be displayed.
BR14: Investor Data Panel — Current Allocation (Tabular)
 IF allocation data exists, THEN a data table must display: asset class colour, name, current allocation %, and value in local currency with denomination, in descending order by allocation value with "Other" always last. IF a percentage is a whole number, THEN display without decimals (e.g. 45%). IF a percentage has decimals, THEN round to 2 decimal places. IF a value is a whole number, THEN display without decimals. IF a value has decimals, THEN round to 2 decimal places. Prefix with local currency symbol. Use denominations: m, bn, trn. IF there are 5 or fewer asset class legends, THEN display all by default. IF there are more than 5, THEN show top 5 with a "Show all [number]" button; clicking expands to show all with a "Show less [number]" button. IF a value is less than 1,000,000, THEN display with comma separator and up to 2 decimal places where applicable.
BR15: Investor Data Panel — Total AUM
 IF AUM data is available, THEN the Total AUM section must display the value in local currency using the format: Currency Symbol + Value + Denomination (e.g. €456bn). IF AUM data is not available, THEN the Total AUM section must not be displayed. IF a value is less than 1,000,000, THEN display with comma separator and up to 2 decimal places where applicable.
BR16: Investor Data Panel — Fund Commitments
 IF the investor has one or more fund commitments, THEN the Fund Commitments accordion must be displayed, collapsed by default. IF the investor has no fund commitments, THEN the Fund Commitments section must not be displayed. A maximum of 3 fund commitments must be displayed, sorted newest to oldest by commitment date. Each commitment must show: Fund Name, Fund Manager, Commitment Size (Currency Symbol + Value + Denomination), Commitment Date (Month, Year). IF a data point is not available, THEN display "-" in its place. IF a fund name or fund manager name is long, THEN it must wrap per design. IF the investor has more than 3 fund commitments, THEN a "View all" CTA must be displayed when the accordion is expanded; clicking must open the full fund commitments page on the legacy site in a new tab. IF the investor has 3 or fewer fund commitments, THEN the "View all" CTA must not be displayed. IF a user clicks a fund name, THEN they must be taken to the fund profile page on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR17: Investor Data Panel — Investor Contacts
 IF the Investor data panel is displayed, THEN an "Investor Contacts" CTA must be displayed per design. IF the Investor data panel is not displayed, THEN the "Investor Contacts" CTA must not be displayed. IF a user clicks "Investor Contacts", THEN they must be taken to the contact info page on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR18: Investor Data Panel — Latest News on Investor
 IF one or more articles exist where the investor is marked as About or Mention (excluding the current article), THEN the "Latest news on [Investor Name]" section must be displayed, collapsed by default. IF no such articles exist, THEN the section must not be displayed. When expanded, a maximum of 2 articles must be displayed, sorted newest to oldest by published date, with the current article excluded. Each article must show: Title (truncated after 2 lines with ellipsis), Published Date (DD Month YYYY), Read Time (x mins read). IF a user clicks an article, THEN they must be taken to the article on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR19: Key Mentions — Scroll Behaviour
 IF Key Mentions entries do not exceed the maximum container height, THEN the section must be static with no scroll. IF Key Mentions entries exceed the maximum container height, THEN the section must be vertically scrollable with a visual scroll indicator; scroll must be contained within the section.
BR20: Fund Data Panel — Display Condition
 IF an editor has marked a Fund as Primary About on the article, THEN the Fund data panel must be displayed with the header "Know the fund." and the full fund name. IF the above condition is not met, THEN the Fund data panel must not be displayed. IF the fund name exceeds one line, THEN it must wrap per design. IF a user clicks the fund name, THEN they must be taken to the Fund's full profile page on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR21: Fund Data Panel — Fund Manager
 IF the fund is marked as Primary About AND a Fund Manager name is available, THEN the Fund Manager name must be displayed per design with no hover or click interaction. IF no Fund Manager name is available, THEN the field must not be displayed.
BR22: Fund Data Panel — Launch Year
 IF the fund is marked as Primary About AND a launch year is available, THEN "Launch Year: YYYY" must be displayed per design with no hover or click interaction. IF no launch year is available, THEN the field must not be displayed.
BR23: Fund Data Panel — Fund Focus
 IF the fund is marked as Primary About AND Fund Focus data is available, THEN the Fund Focus section must display subsections in this order: Strategy, Sector, Region, Specific Focus. IF a subsection has no values, THEN it must not be displayed. Strategy and Sector values must be ordered alphabetically (A–Z). Region and Specific Focus values must be ordered West-to-East: North America → Latin America → Western Europe → Central/Eastern Europe → Sub-Saharan Africa → Middle East/North Africa → Asia-Pacific. IF a subsection has more than one value, THEN a "+N" badge must be shown; on hover a read-only tooltip must display all overflow values in the correct order. IF no Fund Focus data is available, THEN the section must not be displayed.
BR24: Fund Data Panel — Investor Commitments
 IF the fund is marked as Primary About AND the fund has one or more investor commitments where both Investor Name and Commitment Size are available, THEN the Investor Commitments accordion must be displayed, collapsed by default. IF the above condition is not met, THEN the Investor Commitments section must not be displayed. A maximum of 3 commitments must be displayed, sorted newest to oldest. Each commitment must show: Investor Name (truncated after 2 lines with ellipsis), Commitment Size (Currency Symbol + Value + Denomination). Commitment size formatting: whole numbers display without decimals; decimals rounded to 2 places; denominations: m, bn, trn; local currency prefix. IF there are more than 3 commitments, THEN a "View all" CTA must be displayed; clicking must open the full fund commitments page on the legacy site in a new tab. IF there are 3 or fewer commitments, THEN the "View all" CTA must not be displayed. IF a user clicks an investor commitment, THEN they must be taken to the fund profile on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR25: Fund Data Panel — View Performance Data
 IF the fund is marked as Primary About AND performance data is available, THEN a "View Performance Data" CTA must be displayed per design. IF the Fund data panel is not displayed, THEN the CTA must not be displayed. IF a user clicks "View Performance Data", THEN they must be taken to the fund performance page on the legacy site in a new tab.
BR26: Data Panel — Empty Fields
 IF data is not available for a specific field within a data panel, THEN that field must not be rendered unless otherwise specified (e.g. Fund Commitments data points show "-").
BR27: Data Panel — Data Not Received
 IF data is not received for the data panel, THEN the data panel must not be rendered and all other components on the page must be unaffected.
BR28: Data Panel — Design
 IF a data panel is displayed, THEN it must render in the correct position and style per Figma across all breakpoints (375px, 1024px, 1440px, 1920px) and all 4 article templates.
BR29: Data Panel — Heap Tracking
 IF a data panel is rendered, THEN a Heap event must fire on every clickable element within the panel.
BR30: Multiple Author Display IF an article has multiple authors, THEN all author names must be displayed in the order provided by WordPress. IF multiple authors are displayed, THEN names must be comma-separated with "and" appearing immediately before the final author's name (e.g. "John Smith, Jane Doe and Michael Brown"). IF an author name contains special characters (accents, hyphens, apostrophes), THEN it must be displayed as-is. IF a user clicks an author name, THEN they are taken to the corresponding author page on the legacy site in the same tab.
Technical Feasibility 
I article page template config extended to cover PERE News template types per Table 1
II data panel component reused with the PERE-specific database as the data source
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: Investor data panel and Fund data panel must render per Figma.
UX: Data panel behaviour must be consistent with the II implementation across all supported article template types and across all breakpoints
NFR / CFR 
Heap event IDs on all clickable elements: primary category badge, tags, CTAs, breadcrumb links, data panel elements
Article page render performance consistent with II benchmarks
All components render correctly at 375px (mobile), 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px
Out of Scope
Paywall, URL redirects, Network Insights articles, data panel design changes from II pattern
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Miro:
Confluence:
Glossary:
Sample test data:
UX:
UI:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Identify Assumptions, risks, and dependencies during requirements gathering for use cases and user stories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA 
AC1: Article renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article on OBX WHEN the page loads THEN the headline, body, primary category badge, tags, breadcrumb, and byline render per Figma AND components with no data are suppressed without affecting others
AC2: URL structure
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the URL follows domain/equity/[slug]
AC3: CTAs
 GIVEN an article with Fundraising and/or Investor taxonomy terms WHEN the page loads THEN the corresponding CTA(s) render in the correct position per Figma and open the correct target URL
AC4: Investor data panel — display condition
 GIVEN an article with an Institution marked as Primary About with partner status [LP] or [LP, GP] WHEN the page loads THEN the Investor data panel renders with the header "Know the investor." and the full institution name per Figma AND clicking the investor name opens the full profile on the legacy site in a new tab
AC5: Investor data panel — Current Allocation
 GIVEN the investor has allocation data WHEN the data panel loads THEN a graphical chart renders showing asset class distribution in descending order with "Other" last and empty classes excluded AND a data table renders with asset class colour, name, allocation %, and value in local currency with correct formatting and denomination AND legends collapse to top 5 by default with a "Show all [number]" button if more than 5 exist GIVEN no allocation data exists THEN the Current Allocation section is not displayed
AC6: Investor data panel — Total AUM
 GIVEN AUM data is available WHEN the data panel loads THEN Total AUM renders in the format Currency Symbol + Value + Denomination (e.g. €456bn) with comma separators for values under 1,000,000 GIVEN AUM data is not available THEN the Total AUM section is not displayed
AC7: Investor data panel — Fund Commitments
 GIVEN the investor has one or more fund commitments WHEN the data panel loads THEN the Fund Commitments accordion renders collapsed by default showing a maximum of 3 commitments sorted newest to oldest AND each commitment shows Fund Name, Fund Manager, Commitment Size, and Commitment Date AND missing data points display as "-" AND clicking a fund name opens the fund profile on the legacy site in a new tab GIVEN the investor has more than 3 commitments and the accordion is expanded THEN a "View all" CTA is visible and clicking opens the full commitments page in a new tab GIVEN the investor has no fund commitments THEN the Fund Commitments section is not displayed
AC8: Investor data panel — Investor Contacts
 GIVEN the Investor data panel is displayed WHEN the page loads THEN the "Investor Contacts" CTA renders per design AND clicking opens the contact info page on the legacy site in a new tab
AC9: Investor data panel — Latest News on Investor
 GIVEN one or more articles exist where the investor is About or Mention (excluding the current article) WHEN the data panel loads THEN the "Latest news on [Investor Name]" section renders collapsed by default showing a maximum of 2 articles sorted newest to oldest AND each article shows Title (truncated after 2 lines), Published Date (DD Month YYYY), and Read Time AND clicking an article opens it on the legacy site in a new tab GIVEN no such articles exist THEN the section is not displayed
AC10: Key Mentions — scroll behaviour
 GIVEN Key Mentions entries do not exceed the maximum container height WHEN the page loads THEN the section is static with no scroll GIVEN Key Mentions entries exceed the maximum container height WHEN the page loads THEN the section is vertically scrollable with a visual indicator and scroll is contained within the section
AC11: Fund data panel — display condition
 GIVEN an article with a Fund marked as Primary About WHEN the page loads THEN the Fund data panel renders with the header "Know the fund." and the full fund name per Figma AND clicking the fund name opens the full profile on the legacy site in a new tab
AC12: Fund data panel — Fund Manager
 GIVEN the fund has a Fund Manager name available WHEN the data panel loads THEN the Fund Manager name renders per design with no hover or click interaction GIVEN no Fund Manager name is available THEN the field is not displayed
AC13: Fund data panel — Launch Year
 GIVEN the fund has a launch year available WHEN the data panel loads THEN "Launch Year: YYYY" renders per design with no hover or click interaction GIVEN no launch year is available THEN the field is not displayed
AC14: Fund data panel — Fund Focus
 GIVEN the fund has Fund Focus data available WHEN the data panel loads THEN the Fund Focus section renders subsections in order: Strategy, Sector, Region, Specific Focus AND subsections with no values are not displayed AND Strategy and Sector values are alphabetical; Region and Specific Focus follow West-to-East ordering AND subsections with more than one value show a "+N" badge with a read-only hover tooltip GIVEN no Fund Focus data is available THEN the section is not displayed
AC15: Fund data panel — Investor Commitments
 GIVEN the fund has one or more investor commitments with both Investor Name and Commitment Size available WHEN the data panel loads THEN the Investor Commitments accordion renders collapsed by default showing a maximum of 3 commitments sorted newest to oldest AND each commitment shows Investor Name (truncated after 2 lines) and Commitment Size with correct currency formatting AND clicking a commitment opens the fund profile on the legacy site in a new tab GIVEN more than 3 commitments exist THEN a "View all" CTA is displayed and clicking opens the full commitments page in a new tab GIVEN the condition is not met THEN the Investor Commitments section is not displayed
AC16: Fund data panel — View Performance Data
 GIVEN the fund has performance data available WHEN the data panel loads THEN the "View Performance Data" CTA renders per design and clicking opens the fund performance page on the legacy site in a new tab GIVEN performance data is not available or the Fund data panel is not displayed THEN the CTA is not displayed
AC17: Data panel — empty fields and data not received
 GIVEN data is not available for a specific field WHEN the data panel loads THEN that field is not rendered and all other components are unaffected GIVEN data is not received for the entire data panel THEN the data panel is not rendered and all other page components are unaffected
AC18: Data panel — design and responsiveness
 GIVEN a data panel is displayed WHEN the page loads at any supported breakpoint (375px, 1024px, 1440px, 1920px) THEN the data panel renders in the correct position and style per Figma across all 4 article templates
AC19: Data panel — Heap tracking
 GIVEN a data panel is rendered WHEN a user interacts with any clickable element within the panel THEN a Heap event fires for that interaction
AC20: Meta tags render from WordPress
 GIVEN meta title, meta description, and canonical tag are set in WP for a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the OBX page renders the correct meta title, meta description, and canonical tag from the PERE News WP instance
AC21: Meta tags auto-update from WordPress
 GIVEN the meta fields for a PERE News article are updated in WP and the article is republished WHEN the OBX article page is loaded THEN the updated meta title, meta description, and canonical tag are reflected without manual intervention
AC22: Default meta tags when no WP values are set
 GIVEN a PERE News article uses any editorial template (A, B, C, or D) AND no meta title, meta description, or canonical tag has been manually set in WP WHEN the page is rendered THEN the meta title is set to the article title AND the meta description is left for Google to pull from the article body AND the canonical tag is self-referring
AC23: Meta tags sourced from correct WP environment
 GIVEN the OBX platform is running in a staging environment WHEN a PERE News article page loads THEN metadata is fetched from the PERE News staging WP instance, not production
AC24: JSON-LD structured data
 GIVEN a user navigates to a PERE News article page WHEN the page loads THEN the page HTML contains a JSON-LD script tag with schema metadata matching the data returned from the GraphQL endpoint
AC25: Semantic heading structure
 GIVEN a PERE News article page is rendered WHEN the page markup is inspected THEN there is exactly one H1 per page AND heading tags H2–H6 follow a correct hierarchical structure with no levels skipped AND all heading semantics conform to the SEO team specifications (ref: SEO requirements sheet)
AC26: Mobile rendering
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article on a mobile device WHEN the page loads THEN all components render correctly at 375px per Figma AND the Content tab and Data Panel tab are displayed with a sticky tab bar AND switching between tabs displays the correct content
AC27: Multiple author display
 GIVEN an article has multiple authors WHEN the page loads THEN all author names are displayed comma-separated with "and" before the final name AND names render in the order provided by WordPress AND styling matches Figma
AC28: Author name wrapping
 GIVEN an article has authors with long names WHEN the page loads THEN names wrap naturally without breaking the layout at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px
AC29: Author click through
 GIVEN a user is viewing a PERE News article WHEN they click an author name THEN they are taken to the corresponding author page on the legacy site in the same tab
AC30: Article title renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the full title renders matching WP including special characters AND maps to the correct OBX layout per Table 1 AND renders correctly at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px
AC31: Standfirst renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article with a Sub-title set in WP WHEN the page loads THEN the standfirst renders from the Sub-title field with no HTML tags or shortcodes visible GIVEN no Sub-title is set but an Excerpt exists THEN the standfirst falls back to the Excerpt GIVEN neither Sub-title nor Excerpt is set THEN no standfirst is displayed
AC32: Read time renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the read time renders based on the word count logic (0–250 = 1 min through 2250+ = 10 mins) AND maps to the correct OBX layout per Table 1 AND renders correctly at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px
AC33: Published date renders correctly
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the published date renders in the correct format (minutes if <1hr, hours if <24hrs, DD Month YYYY if ≥24hrs) in UTC AND published date appears before read time AND renders correctly at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px
AC34: Article body styling
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN the article body renders with correct OBX CSS covering subheadings, paragraphs, bullets, bold, italics, underline, fonts, hyperlinks, and special characters AND paragraphs have 16px spacing between them AND rich media elements, pull quotes, and block quotes have 40px spacing above and below AND heading spacing follows: H2 40px/16px, H3 32px/16px, H4 28px/16px, H5 24px/16px, H6 24px/16px
AC35: Embedded images
 GIVEN an article contains embedded images WHEN the page loads THEN images render at the correct position with the correct alignment (left, right, center, or none) and appropriate spacing
AC36: Blockquotes and pull quotes
 GIVEN an article contains blockquotes or pull quotes WHEN the page loads THEN they render in the correct OBX styled format per Figma with 40px spacing above and below
AC37: Infogram
 GIVEN an article contains an Infogram embed WHEN the page loads THEN the Infogram renders centre-aligned with default action buttons visible
AC38: Podcast (PodBean)
 GIVEN an article contains a PodBean embed WHEN the page loads THEN the podcast renders using the default PodBean player and controls at the editor-specified position AND the reader can play the podcast
AC39: Video
 GIVEN an article contains a video embed WHEN the page loads THEN the video renders at the editor-specified position with default third-party controls and correct aspect ratio GIVEN the video service returns an error THEN the default third-party error treatment is displayed
AC40: PDF (Flipsnack)
 GIVEN an article contains a Flipsnack embed WHEN the page loads THEN the PDF renders as an interactive embedded flipbook with Flipsnack settings (transitions, zoom, autoplay) preserved
AC41: WP Bakery Tabs
 GIVEN an article contains a Tabs shortcode WHEN the page loads THEN the Tabs component renders with correct tab labels and the first tab active by default AND switching tabs shows the correct content without a page reload AND the tab row wraps if it exceeds the article width
AC42: WP Bakery Button
 GIVEN an article contains a Button shortcode WHEN the page loads THEN the Button renders at the correct position with the title as the label AND clicking an anchor destination scrolls to that anchor AND clicking a URL destination navigates to that URL
AC43: Social sharing
 GIVEN a user is viewing a PERE News article WHEN they scroll the page THEN social share icons (LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Email, Copy Link) remain sticky on the left side GIVEN a user clicks Copy Link THEN the article URL is copied to clipboard and a "Link copied" confirmation is shown GIVEN a user clicks Twitter/X or LinkedIn while logged in THEN the respective post modal opens with prepopulated content GIVEN a user clicks Twitter/X or LinkedIn while not logged in THEN they are redirected to login before the post modal opens GIVEN a user clicks Email THEN the default mail app opens with a prepopulated subject and article link
AC44: Lazy loading
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article WHEN the page loads THEN RHS components (Latest News, data panels, Key Mentions, Investor/Fundraising tools) are lazy loaded AND Related News and Mentioned News are lazy loaded on scroll
AC45: Mobile layout (375px)
 GIVEN a user visits a PERE News article on a 375px viewport WHEN the page loads THEN all components render per the mobile-specific Figma design AND the layout differs from the desktop layout as per Figma

title: to create a Tags/Other category / taxonomy terms  listing page layout and components pagination
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX build, a second listing page variant is required for sections that do not use in-page filters. Unlike the primary listing page skeleton, this variant does not include the Equity / Credit filter or category pills filter bar. All other zones remain the same.
The page is made up of the following zones:
Breadcrumb slot
Page header
Listing grid
RHS sidebar
Pagination
Pre-Requisite
Top-level taxonomy categories are confirmed and applied for Equity content (Fundraising, Managers, Investors, Deals, People)
Credit taxonomy alignment is complete before Credit pills are activated
Article card component is built and supports a visible strategy label (Equity / Credit)
URL path structure is confirmed per Brief 2 (/news, /news/equity, /news/credit)
Mega-menu links are configured to route to this page
USER STORY 
As a developer, 

I need a static layout skeleton of the PERE listing page (no filter variant) so that all structural zones are in place, 

built to accommodate dynamic content, and ready to receive that content in subsequent stories.
Scope
Page layout for the no filter listing page route
Two-column grid: LHS listing grid column + RHS sidebar column
Static placeholder zones for: breadcrumb slot, page header, listing grid, RHS sidebar, and pagination
Breadcrumb slot must exist in the correct position on the page but will not render any content in this story
Layout must be built to handle: a dynamic page header, a variable RHS sidebar composition, and a variable number of articles in the listing grid
Responsive breakpoint structure (375px / 1024px / 1440px / 1920px)
No real data, no API calls — placeholder copy and lorem content only
Business Rules
IF the breakpoint is 1024px or above THEN the layout renders as two columns: LHS listing grid column and RHS sidebar column
IF the breakpoint is 375px THEN the RHS sidebar stacks below the LHS listing grid
IF the listing page (no filter variant) loads THEN the following zones are present in order from top to bottom: breadcrumb slot, page header, listing grid + RHS sidebar, pagination
IF the breadcrumb slot is present THEN it renders empty with no visible content until the breadcrumb story is built
IF the page header zone receives dynamic content THEN the layout must accommodate it without breaking or overflowing
IF the RHS sidebar receives a variable number of components THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing
IF the listing grid receives a variable number of articles THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing
Out of Scope
Breadcrumb content and rendering (separate story)
Page header content and rendering (separate story)
Equity / Credit filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Category pills filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Article card component rendering (separate story)

title: to view all the Upcoming Events on listing pages
description:
Background
Readers of PERE News and PERE Credit need quick access to upcoming PERE events to stay informed and engaged with the platform's activities. An upcoming events section on the RHS of article pages provides contextual, at-a-glance visibility of relevant events and drives traffic to the PEI Events site.
Pre-Requisite
Hardcoded events list (TABLE 1)
RHS panel component available on PERE News and PERE Credit article pages
Figma design sign-off
USER STORY 
AS A reader on PERE News or PERE Credit

I WANT TO see a list of upcoming PERE events displayed on the RHS of the article page

SO THAT I can click through to find out more details about each event or navigate to the full events calendar
Scope
Display a hardcoded list of upcoming PERE events in the RHS panel of article pages on PERE News and PERE Credit, which readers can view and click to access detailed information, and navigate to the full PERE events calendar.
Business Rules
IF a reader views an article page on PERE News or PERE Credit, THEN display the Upcoming Events section in the RHS panel as per design.
IF the Upcoming Events section is rendered, THEN display exactly the events listed in TABLE 1 in the order specified.
IF an event name exceeds 1 line, THEN truncate with an ellipsis (…).
IF a location exceeds 1 line, THEN truncate with an ellipsis (…).
IF a reader clicks on an event card, THEN navigate to the corresponding event details page in a new tab.
IF a reader clicks "View all", THEN navigate to 
https://www.peievents.com/en/pei-media-global-events-calendar/pere-events-calendar/
 in a new tab.
Technical Feasibility
Reuse the upcoming events list component built for Infrastructure Investor. The hardcoded data should be configurable per brand (II vs PERE). No dynamic data fetching required; the list will be manually updated as needed.
Usability / Design UX/UI
Reusable event list component, positioned in the RHS panel of PERE article pages
Each event card to display: month in abbreviated format (e.g., "Sep"), event name, location
Clear hover/click states per design
Consistent with II implementation; PERE-specific styling to follow Figma
Out of Scope
Dynamic/API-driven event data
Display order logic (hardcoded order from TABLE 1 applies)
Number-of-events display logic (show all 4 from TABLE 1)
Automatic expiry/removal of past events (manual update only)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Miro:

Risk:
 List is hardcoded; any event changes require a manual code/config update.

 The same reusable component from II can be extended for PERE with brand-specific data.
Dependency:
 RHS panel must be available and rendered on PERE News and PERE Credit article pages.

Month in abbreviated format (e.g., "Sep", "Nov", "Dec", "Mar")

title: Listing Page: Skeleton Layout for all Breakpoints
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX build, the listing page needs to be built in phases. This story covers building the static layout skeleton establishing the structural zones and slot placeholders , before any dynamic data rendering is introduced. 

Subsequent stories will handle the header, filters, article card rendering, RHS components, and pagination.
The skeleton must be built to accommodate dynamic content from the outset ,the page header, category pills, RHS sidebar composition, and article quantity in the listing grid will all vary depending on the section, filter state, and pagination and data returned. The layout must also be capable of switching between the Equity and Credit filters, and reflecting a different set of category pills per filter state. The layout must flex to handle all of this without breaking.
The page is made up of the following zones:
Breadcrumb slot
Page header
Equity / Credit filter
Category pills filter bar
Listing grid
RHS sidebar
Pagination
Pre-Requisite
Top-level taxonomy categories are confirmed and applied for Equity content (Fundraising, Managers, Investors, Deals, People)
Credit taxonomy alignment is complete before Credit pills are activated
Article card component is built and supports a visible strategy label (Equity / Credit)
URL path structure is confirmed per Brief 2 (/news, /news/equity, /news/credit)
Mega-menu links are configured to route to this page
USER STORY 
As a developer,

I need a static layout skeleton of the PERE listing page so that all structural zones are in place,

 built to accommodate dynamic content and filter switching, and ready to receive that content in subsequent stories.
Scope
Page layout for the listing page route
Two-column grid: LHS listing grid column + RHS sidebar column
Static placeholder zones for: breadcrumb slot, page header, Equity / Credit filter, category pills filter bar, listing grid, RHS sidebar, and pagination
Breadcrumb slot must exist in the correct position on the page but will not render any content in this story
Layout must be built to handle: a dynamic page header, switching between Equity and Credit filter states, a variable set of category pills per filter state, a variable RHS sidebar composition, and a variable number of articles in the listing grid
Responsive breakpoint structure (375px / 1024px / 1440px / 1920px)
No real data, no API calls, placeholder copy and lorem content only
Business Rules
IF the breakpoint is 1024px or above THEN the layout renders as two columns: LHS listing grid column and RHS sidebar column
IF the breakpoint is 375px THEN the RHS sidebar stacks below the LHS listing grid
IF the listing page loads THEN the following zones are present in order from top to bottom: breadcrumb slot, page header, Equity / Credit filter, category pills filter bar, listing grid + RHS sidebar, pagination
IF the breadcrumb slot is present THEN it renders empty with no visible content until the breadcrumb story is built
IF the Equity / Credit filter switches between states THEN the layout must accommodate the change without breaking or reflowing incorrectly
IF the category pills filter bar receives a different set of pills based on the active Equity / Credit filter state THEN the layout must accommodate the change without breaking or overflowing
IF the page header zone receives dynamic content THEN the layout must accommodate it without breaking or overflowing
IF the RHS sidebar receives a variable number of components THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing
IF the listing grid receives a variable number of articles THEN the layout must accommodate them without breaking or overflowing and reflect appropriate pagination.
Out of Scope
Breadcrumb content and rendering (separate story)
Page header content and rendering (separate story)
Equity / Credit filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Category pills filter logic and interactivity (separate story)
Article card component rendering (separate story)
RHS component content and data binding (separate stories per component)
Pagination logic (separate story)
Nav and footer (already built)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

RHS slot count and ordering may differ between Equity and Credit views, needs design clarification before build

The breadcrumb slot position is above the page header, as per design

IVEN I am viewing the listing page

 THEN the RHS sidebar stacks below the LHS listing grid in a single-column layout

title: the OBX footer to appear on content served from legacy WordPress sites
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, the site operates in a hybrid state where OBX pages are served by the Fusion Next.js front end and a proportion of pages continue to be served by the Classic WordPress codebase. While OBX pages carry the new Fusion footer, Classic pages require a separate footer implementation built within the Classic/WordPress codebase that is visually like-for-like with the Fusion footer.
This ticket covers the build of the Classic footer with the correct PERE branding, content, linking, and mobile rendering. The logic governing which pages the Classic footer conditionally renders on is addressed in a separate ticket.
Pre-Requisite
PERE OBX Fusion footer is implemented and available as reference
Figma designs for the PERE footer including mobile are signed off
Footer content and all link URLs are confirmed by product/editorial
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to a PERE Classic page 

I WANT to see a footer that is visually consistent with the OBX Fusion footer 

SO THAT my experience feels seamless whether I am on an OBX or Classic page
Scope
Build the Classic footer within the Classic/WordPress codebase, visually matching the OBX Fusion footer. This ticket covers the build, content, linking, and mobile rendering of the footer. Conditional rendering logic is out of scope and covered in a separate ticket.
Business Rules
BR1: Visibility
 IF a user visits any PERE Classic page, THEN the footer must be visible and visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer.
BR2: Sync with OBX Fusion Footer
 IF the OBX Fusion footer is updated, THEN the Classic footer must be updated to match.
BR3: Link Behaviour — External
 IF a footer link routes to an external URL, THEN it must open in a new tab.
BR4: Link Behaviour — Internal
 IF a footer link routes to an internal PERE page, THEN it must open in the same tab.
BR5: Mobile Rendering
 IF a user views the footer on a mobile viewport, THEN the footer must render per Figma mobile designs.
BR6: Missing Content
 IF content is not received for the footer, THEN the footer must not be rendered and all other page components must be unaffected.
Technical Feasibility
The Fusion footer component will be reused from II and adapted for the Classic/WordPress codebase.
Usability / Design
UI: The Classic footer must be visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer, matching layout, typography, colour tokens, and branding per Figma across desktop and mobile.
UX: Footer behaviour must be consistent with the OBX Fusion footer across all supported viewports:
XL: 1024px and 1440px
2XL: 1920px
Mobile
NFR / CFR
Analytics: Heap event IDs must be applied to all clickable footer elements.
Out of Scope
Fusion build
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma:  

 
UX:
UI:
MOBILE COLLAPSED VIEW
EXPANDED VIEW MOBILE
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Identify Assumptions, risks, and dependencies during requirements gathering for use cases and user stories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Footer renders on all PERE Classic pages
 

GIVEN a user visits any PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads THEN the footer is visible at the bottom of the page
AC2: Footer is visually like-for-like with the OBX Fusion footer

 GIVEN the footer is rendered on a PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads THEN the footer matches the OBX Fusion footer in layout, typography, colour tokens, and branding per Figma
AC3: Footer updates to match OBX Fusion footer changes
GIVEN the OBX Fusion footer has been updated WHEN the Classic footer is next rendered THEN it reflects the updated design to remain like-for-like
AC4: External links open in a new tab

 GIVEN a footer link routes to an external URL WHEN a user clicks the link THEN the destination opens in a new tab
AC5: Internal links open in the same tab

 GIVEN a footer link routes to an internal PERE page WHEN a user clicks the link THEN the destination opens in the same tab
AC6: Footer renders correctly on mobile

 GIVEN a user views a PERE Classic page on a mobile viewport WHEN the page loads THEN the footer renders per Figma mobile designs
AC8: Heap events fire on all clickable footer elements
 

GIVEN the footer is rendered WHEN a user clicks any clickable element within the footer THEN a Heap event fires for that interaction
AC9: Footer renders correctly across all supported breakpoints
 

GIVEN a user views a PERE Classic page WHEN the page loads at XL (1024px, 1440px), 2XL (1920px), or mobile viewports THEN the footer layout is correct and consistent with the OBX Fusion footer at each breakpoint
Classic Footer/nScope1.Build a legacy top nav for OBX PERE�built within the Classic/WordPress codebase.2.When any user requested content is displayed from the legacy PERE or PERE Credit WordPress site, the rendered page must show the OBX footer.Will we need to decide the list of post/Pages that need to be served from legacy?/n-/nST3 DEPENDENCY

title: to see PERE links on the classic top nav
description:
Background
The PERE OBX site (
pei-privaterealestate.com
) includes a top navigation bar with six primary nav items, each opening a mega menu. This ticket documents the linking behaviour for all nav items  which labels are clickable, where they route, and which URLs are yet to be confirmed.
When a user interacts with the top navigation, they need to be taken to the correct destination — or understand that a label is a grouping header only — so they can navigate the site without confusion or dead ends.
Pre-Requisite
Nav bar component built (OBX Fusion and/or Classic build tickets)
URL structure for PERE News and PERE Credit confirmed and live in the relevant environment
USER STORY 
As a site visitor, I want the top navigation links to take me to the correct pages, so that I can find the content I am looking for quickly and without errors.
Scope
This ticket covers the link destinations and click behaviour for all primary nav labels and mega menu items in the PERE OBX top navigation.
Business Rules
All link destinations and clickability rules are defined in the linking table below. The following rules apply globally across the nav:
IF a label is marked as not clickable in the table, THEN clicking it does not navigate — it opens or organises the mega menu only, and no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
IF a label has a confirmed URL in the table, THEN clicking it navigates the user to that URL within the same tab
IF a label is marked TBC in the table, THEN the destination must be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development
IF the URL is an external domain (

 ), THEN the link opens in a new tab
Out of Scope
Nav bar visual design and component build
Mega menu open/close behaviour
Mobile navigation behaviour
Footer links
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Risks:
 A number of URLs are marked TBC — if these are not confirmed before development begins, placeholder links or broken routes may go live.
Assumptions:
 All TBC items will be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development. The Deals item under News intentionally routes to an external domain (

 ) and opens in a new tab.
Issues:

/credit/

/equity/
 — this needs explicit sign-off.
Dependencies:

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
Functionality
GIVEN a user opens the mega menu under any primary nav item, WHEN they click a label marked as not clickable in the linking table, THEN no navigation occurs
GIVEN a user clicks any item with a confirmed URL in the linking table, WHEN the link is activated, THEN they are taken to the correct destination
GIVEN a user clicks Deals under News & Analysis, WHEN the link is activated, THEN 

  opens in a new tab
GIVEN all TBC URLs have been confirmed, WHEN the ticket is signed off, THEN the linking table is updated with final destinations before development begins
UX Behaviour
GIVEN a user hovers over a label marked as not clickable, THEN no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
GIVEN a user hovers over a clickable menu item, THEN a standard hover state is applied consistent with the nav design

title: to see a PERE logo and labels on the classic Top Nav
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX migration, the site operates in a hybrid state where OBX pages are served by the Fusion Next.js front end and a proportion of pages continue to be served by the Classic WordPress codebase. While OBX pages carry the new Fusion nav bar, Classic pages require a separate nav bar implementation built within the Classic/WordPress codebase that matches the Fusion nav bar exactly.
This ticket covers the build of the Classic nav bar with the correct PEI PERE logo, primary nav labels, and mega menu content. The logic governing which pages the Classic nav conditionally renders on is addressed in a separate ticket. Linking is also addressed in a separate ticket.
USER STORY 
As a visitor to a PERE Classic page, 

I want to see a nav bar with PERE-relevant navigation items and branded structure,

 So that I can navigate the site consistently whether I am on an OBX or Classic page.
Scope
Build the Classic nav bar within the Classic/WordPress codebase matching the Fusion nav bar exactly. This ticket covers the logo, primary nav labels, mega menu content, and utility bar only. Conditional rendering logic and linking are out of scope.
Business Rules
IF
 a page is served by the Classic WordPress codebase, 
THEN
 the Classic nav bar implementation must be available to render on that page.
IF
 the Classic nav bar renders, 
THEN
 its layout, visual appearance, and content structure must match the Fusion nav bar exactly.
IF
 a user hovers over a primary nav item, 
THEN
 its mega menu must open and any previously open mega menu must close.
IF
 a user clicks any clickable nav element, 
THEN
 a Heap event must fire for that interaction.
IF
 the conditional rendering logic governing which Classic pages the nav bar renders on is out of scope for this ticket, 
THEN
 it must be addressed in a separate ticket.
Technical Feasibility 
UI: The Classic nav bar must match the Fusion nav bar exactly, including the dark background, PERE brand typography, logo placement, and mega menu layout per Figma
UX: Mega menus open on hover and close on moving out, consistent with Fusion nav bar behaviour
Analytics: Heap event IDs to be implemented on the PEI PERE logo and all clickable primary nav items and mega menu links
Breakpoints: Desktop
Out of Scope
Conditional rendering logic governing which pages the Classic nav renders on (separate ticket)
URL linking for any nav item (separate Classic linking ticket)
Mobile hamburger menu
OBX Fusion nav bar (covered in separate tickets)
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Dependency:
 PERE Fusion nav bar labels ticket must be complete before this ticket begins to ensure label parity between OBX and Classic
Dependency:
 Product/editorial must confirm mega menu content for all six primary nav items before development can complete this ticket
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Classic nav bar renders with correct structure and branding
 

GIVEN the Classic nav bar has been built

 WHEN it renders on a page

 THEN it displays the PEI PERE logo in the top-left, a primary nav rail displaying News & analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions, and the utility bar on the right containing Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, and the user account icon, and it matches the Fusion nav bar exactly
AC2: All primary nav items render with a mega menu

 GIVEN the Classic nav bar has been built 

WHEN a user hovers over or activates any primary nav item 

THEN a mega menu opens for that item displaying the correct columns and sub-items as confirmed in Figma
AC3: Sub-items with descriptions render description text beneath the label
 

GIVEN a mega menu is open and conta

ins sub-items with descriptions

 WHEN the user views those sub-items THEN the description text renders directly below the sub-item label in a visually subordinate style
AC4: Sub-items without descriptions render label only

 GIVEN a mega menu is open and contains sub-items without descriptions

 WHEN the user views those sub-items THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text beneath it
AC5: Only one mega menu is open at a time
 GIVEN a user has one mega menu open WHEN they hover over or activate a different primary nav item THEN the previously open mega menu closes and the newly activated mega menu opens
AC6: Heap event IDs fire on all clickable nav elements
 GIVEN the Classic nav bar is rendered WHEN a user clicks the logo, any primary nav item, mega menu section heading, or mega menu sub-item THEN a Heap event fires for that interaction

title: to see PERE links on the top nav
description:
Background
The PERE OBX site (
pei-privaterealestate.com
) includes a top navigation bar with six primary nav items, each opening a mega menu. This ticket documents the linking behaviour for all nav items  which labels are clickable, where they route, and which URLs are yet to be confirmed.
When a user interacts with the top navigation, they need to be taken to the correct destination — or understand that a label is a grouping header only — so they can navigate the site without confusion or dead ends.
Pre-Requisite
Nav bar component built (OBX Fusion and/or Classic build tickets)
URL structure for PERE News and PERE Credit confirmed and live in the relevant environment
USER STORY 
As a site visitor, I want the top navigation links to take me to the correct pages, so that I can find the content I am looking for quickly and without errors.
Scope
This ticket covers the link destinations and click behaviour for all primary nav labels and mega menu items in the PERE OBX top navigation.
Business Rules
All link destinations and clickability rules are defined in the linking table below. The following rules apply globally across the nav:
IF a label is marked as not clickable in the table, THEN clicking it does not navigate — it opens or organises the mega menu only, and no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
IF a label has a confirmed URL in the table, THEN clicking it navigates the user to that URL within the same tab
IF a label is marked TBC in the table, THEN the destination must be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development
IF the URL is an external domain (

 ), THEN the link opens in a new tab
Out of Scope
Nav bar visual design and component build
Mega menu open/close behaviour
Mobile navigation behaviour
Footer links
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
Miro:
Confluence:
Glossary:
Sample test data:
UX:
UI:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Risks:
 A number of URLs are marked TBC — if these are not confirmed before development begins, placeholder links or broken routes may go live.
Assumptions:
 All TBC items will be confirmed by the product owner before the ticket moves to development. The Deals item under News intentionally routes to an external domain (

 ) and opens in a new tab.
Issues:
 Healthcare under Sectors routes to 
/credit/
 rather than 
/equity/
 — this needs explicit sign-off.
Dependencies:
 URL structure decisions for Data, Performance & Rankings, and Network & Events sections.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality
Functionality
GIVEN a user opens the mega menu under any primary nav item, WHEN they click a label marked as not clickable in the linking table, THEN no navigation occurs
GIVEN a user clicks any item with a confirmed URL in the linking table, WHEN the link is activated, THEN they are taken to the correct destination
GIVEN a user clicks Deals under News & Analysis, WHEN the link is activated, THEN 

  opens in a new tab
GIVEN all TBC URLs have been confirmed, WHEN the ticket is signed off, THEN the linking table is updated with final destinations before development begins
UX Behaviour
GIVEN a user hovers over a label marked as not clickable, THEN no pointer cursor or hover state is applied
GIVEN a user hovers over a clickable menu item, THEN a standard hover state is applied consistent with the nav design

title: To see PERE labels on the top nav [FUSION]
description:
Background
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, PERE is migrating from the legacy Classic site to the Fusion design system. 

Following the implementation of the nav bar shell and logo, this ticket populates the

PERE SPECIFIC Top uitility Icons

Primary nav rail and mega menu dropdowns with the correct PERE-specific labels, sub-items, and descriptions as shown in the Figma designs and target state screenshots.

The II nav bar provides a reference implementation; this ticket configures PERE-specific content in place of II content
Pre-Requisite

   is complete.
PERE Figma nav designs are signed off, including all mega menu column content and featured panel
Final nav label copy is confirmed by editorial/product.
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to the PERE site

I WANT to see a PERE Specific Nav Bar

SO THAT I can understand the PERE content offering and navigate to the right section
Scope
Populate the  nav bar with correct PERE specific labels and ensure the Nav bar matches the figma from both UI (Topograhy, Spacing, colors etc) and UX(On hover, On click etc)
Top utility bar
Primary nav items
Mega Menu and descriptions
Business Rules
Top Utility Bar:IF a user visits any PERE page, THEN the following Top utility Bar Icons must be visible
Ask PEI
Search
Sign In
Register
Watchlist
Memberships
Request Demo
Notifications
User Account
Primary Nav: 
IF a user visits any PERE page, THEN the primary Nav bar must be visible displaying six top-level items: News & analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions. (As per Primary Navigation Column in Table)
IF a primary nav item has a mega menu, THEN a chevron indicator renders next to the primary nav label to indicate it is expandable.
IF a primary nav item has a mega menu, THEN hovering over or activating that item opens the mega menu and the chevron points upwards
If a mega menu is opened, any other open mega menu must close automatically.
Mega Menu
IF a mega menu column has a section heading (e.g. News, Market analysis, Investor insight), THEN the heading renders with an arrow indicator if clickable. (
Mega Menu- Section Heading )
Mega menu option as per Table. 
Supporting description
IF a mega menu sub-item has a supporting description, THEN the description renders directly beneath the sub-item label in a lighter weight.(
As per description column in Table )
IF a mega menu sub-item does not have a supporting description (e.g. items under the News column), THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text beneath it.
Technical Feasibility 
.
The mega menu slot structure supports the column count and featured panel required by the PERE nav designs 
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: All labels, descriptions, and column headings must match Figma exactly in terms of typography weight, size, and colour.
UX:
 Mega menus open on hover and close on moving outside consistent with II behaviour.
Mega menu on hover behaviour to match Figma
Column layout must not break at any supported desktop breakpoint.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Heap event IDs to be implemented on all clickable primary nav items and mega menu links.
Out of Scope
URL linking for any nav item 
Mobile Implementation
Rendering on Classic
Changes to II nav bar labels or content
Top utility bar icons based on user type.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 
UX: 
PERE (MVP v2)
 
UI: 

 
Miro:
UX: 
PERE (MVP v2)
 
UI: 
PRIMARY NAV WITH TOP UTILITY BAR 
MEGA MENUS
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Mega menu column structure from Ticket 1 supports the number of columns required by the PERE News & analysis dropdown without further structural changes. 
There is a dependency on editorial/product confirming the final label copy for all six primary nav items and all mega menu sub-items before development can complete this ticket. It is assumed that sub-item descriptions are static copy and do not require CMS management.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: Top Utility Bar visibility
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN the page loads

 THEN the Top Utility Bar renders and displays the following icons in order: Ask PEI, Search, Sign In, Register, Watchlist, Memberships, Request Demo, Notifications, User Account
AC2: Primary nav renders with correct items

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN the page loads 

THEN the primary nav bar renders with exactly six top-level items in the following order: News & Analysis, Data, Performance & Rankings, Network & Events, Sectors, Regions
AC3: Chevron indicator on expandable nav items

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

 WHEN a primary nav item has an associated mega menu

THEN a chevron icon renders adjacent to that nav item's label
AC4: Mega menu opens on hover/activation
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

WHEN a user hovers over or activates a primary nav item that has an associated mega menu 

THEN the corresponding mega menu opens and the chevron points upwards.
AC5: Only one mega menu open at a time
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and one mega menu is already open

WHEN a user hovers over or activates a different primary nav item with a mega menu

 THEN the previously open mega menu closes and the new one opens
AC6: Section heading renders with arrow indicator when clickable
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN the mega menu column contains a clickable section heading (e.g. News, Market Analysis, Investor Insight)

 THEN the heading renders with an arrow indicator
AC7: Mega menu sub-item with supporting description

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open 

WHEN a sub-item has a supporting description 

THEN the description renders directly beneath the sub-item label in a lighter typographic weight
AC8: Mega menu sub-item without supporting description

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN a sub-item has no supporting description (e.g. items listed under the News column) 

THEN only the sub-item label renders with no description text below it
AC9: Mega menu content matches Figma
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open

 WHEN the user reviews the layout and content

 THEN all labels, descriptions, section headings, and column structure match the Figma designs exactly in terms of copy, typography weight, size, and colour

AND in terms of on hover, on click behaviour.
AC10: Mega menu closes when focus moves outside
 

GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page and a mega menu is open 

WHEN the user moves focus or cursor outside the nav and mega menu area 

THEN the mega menu closes
AC11: Layout integrity across breakpoints

 GIVEN a OBX PERE page loads

 WHEN the user views the page at 1024px, 1440px, or 1920px viewport width

THEN the Nav bar is responsive.
AC12: Heap event tracking on nav interactions

 GIVEN a user views an OBX PERE page

 WHEN the user clicks any primary nav item or mega menu link

 THEN the corresponding Heap event ID is triggered as per the tracking specification

title: to see a PERE logo on the Top Nav
description:
Background
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, PERE is migrating from the legacy Classic site to the new Fusion design system. The top navigation bar is the primary wayfinding element across all pages and must be implemented on PERE with the correct brand identity and structural scaffolding before labels and links are applied in subsequent tickets.
This ticket covers implementing the nav bar shell on PERE: the component structure and the PEI PERE logo, 

The following to replicate as per II,  the top utility bar (Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, user account), and the primary nav rail with the correct number of top-level items as placeholders. It does not include final labels or live URLs, which are addressed in separate tickets.
Pre-Requisite
Fusion nav bar component is implemented and available in the shared component library (reference: II nav bar implementation)
PEI PERE logo asset is available and signed off by design
PERE brand colour tokens are configured in the Fusion design system
Figma designs for the PERE nav bar structure are signed off
USER STORY 
AS A visitor to the PERE site

I WANT to see a correctly structured and branded nav bar on page load

SO THAT I can identify the publication and access utility features from any page on the site
Scope
Implement the Fusion nav bar component on PERE with:
PEI PERE logo in the top-left position
Dark background nav bar consistent with PERE brand.
Mega menu shell structure per top-level item (column layout and featured panel slot, to be populated in the labels ticket)
To remain same as II for this ticket
Top utility bar
Primary nav with six top-level item slots rendered as structural placeholders (labels and links to be added in subsequent tickets)
Business Rules
IF the user visits any OBX PERE page, THEN the nav bar must render at the top of the page across all page templates
IF the page is a PEREpage THEN the PEI PERE logo must appear in the top-left of the nav bar
IF the user clicks the PEI PERE logo THEN the user must be navigated to the PERE homepage 
(Temporary destination: 
https://pereportfolio-fusion.dev.pei-services.com/
)
IF the Fusion nav bar component is already in use on Infrastructure Investor THEN the same shared component must be reused on PERE and a separate component must not be created
The mega menu slot structure should support the column count required by the PERE nav designs 
The Fusion nav bar component used on II must be reused for PERE via a brand configuration prop or config file ,a separate nav bar component must not be created for PERE. 
Usability / Design UX/UI
UI: Nav bar renders with a dark background, PERE brand typography, and the PEI PERE logo at the correct size and position, per Figma. 
Utility icons render at correct size with appropriate spacing. 
UX: Nav bar is fixed/sticky at the top of the viewport. 
On hover, on click behaviour etc. to match design specifications
Responsive behaviour at desktop breakpoints  is consistent with II nav bar behaviour.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Analytics: Heap event ID to be implemented on the PEI PERE logo
Out of Scope
Navigation labels and dropdown content
Internal and external URL linking
Implementation for mobile
Rendering on Classic
Changes to II nav bar configuration
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 
PERE (MVP v2)
 -UX prototype
UI: 

 
LOGO SVG: 
Miro:
UX:
UI: (TO BE RECIEVED]
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
It is assumed that the Fusion nav bar component used on II is sufficiently configurable to support PERE brand. 
Assumption

 Utility bar icon set (Ask PEI, Search, Watchlist, Notifications, user account) is the same component set used on II with no structural changes required for PERE.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 – Nav Bar Renders Across All PERE Pages

 GIVEN a user visits any PERE page

 WHEN the page loads

THEN the nav bar renders at the top of the page across all page templates
AC2 – PERE Logo Displays and Links Correctly

 GIVEN the page is a PERE-branded page

WHEN the nav bar renders THEN the PEI PERE logo appears in the top-left of the nav bar

 AND clicking the logo navigates the user to the PERE homepage
AC3 – Fusion Nav Bar Component is Reused

GIVEN the Fusion nav bar component is already in use on Infrastructure Investor

 WHEN the nav bar is implemented on PERE

THEN the same shared component is reused and no separate component is created

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence | Equity-Fund Performance-10 largest Real estate funds

We're adding the "10 Largest Real Estate Funds" league table to help GP users conduct competitor analysis. This is the PERE equivalent of the existing II implementation delivered via 
WEB-24902
.

Across stage, dev and prod
Targeted Intelligence section + Equity pill + Fund Performance tab is in place
USER STORY 
As a
 GP user 
I want
 to be able to view the largest Real Estate funds 
So that
 I can see where most of the capital in the market is being invested
Scope
 
To introduce 10 largest funds in PERE OBX homepage to rank the largest Real Estate funds by size, under Equity > Fund Performance tab
Business Rules
The new ranking table component should be labelled as 
"10 Largest Real Estate Funds"
Positioned in the Fund Performance tab under the Equity pill
Displayed as the first component in the tab

"Performance of the largest funds, ranked by size"
Ensure the following column headers are displayed in the table:
Fund — fund's name

Vintage Year
Ensure only 10 funds are listed
Ensure each fund listed is hyperlinked:
IF 
user clicks the fund name
THEN 
they are navigated to the fund profile on the PERE Classic site 
AND 
land on the performance history tab
.
 The tab opens in the same page
Display the hyperlink 
"Compare funds"
 at the bottom of the component
IF 
user clicks the Compare Funds link
THEN 
they are navigated to the 
Benchmarks
 section on PERE Classic

Ensure the list of funds is filtered where fund status = Closed AND 
vehicle type = Closed Ended - 

 / 

 to get back
Note - To be checked with II (assumption - change the asset class only)
Ensure the funds listed are ordered by fund current size (largest to smallest). Ordering must be based on USD currency conversion (same logic as the Fund tab in R&A database)
For rendering it on front-end
Equity - All the non-Debt filters are applied
Same UI as II
Out of Scope
Adding filtering capability to the ranking table
Credit pill equivalent (covered in separate story if/when scoped)

title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence | Equity-Fund Performance-Recent fund performance
description:
Background
As part of the PERE OBX , we're building the Fund Performance tab under the Equity pill of the Targeted Intelligence section. We're adding the "Recent Fund Performance" component to help GP users gain insight into fund performance for Real Estate.
This is the PERE equivalent of the II implementation delivered via:
WEB-24113
 — List Recent Fund Performance
WEB-24116
 — Fund Profile Link
WEB-24117
 — See all funds with performance data (CTA)
WEB-24115
 — Data Source LP/GP Profile Link
This story covers all 4 aspects for PERE in a single ticket.
Pre-Requisite
Domain of PERE needs to be setup and it must be up and running
Across stage, dev and prod
Targeted Intelligence section + Equity pill + Fund Performance tab UI is in place
USER STORY 
As a
 GP user 
I want
 to view most recent Real Estate fund performance from the PERE homepage 
So that
 I can analyse fund performance and conduct competitor analysis
Scope
 
To build the "Recent Fund Performance" component on the PERE OBX homepage under Equity > Fund Performance tab, covering:
Listing the 10 most recent fund performance entries from the latest quarter
Hyperlink on fund name → PERE Classic fund profile (performance history tab)
Hyperlink on Data Source institution → PERE Classic GP or LP profile (relevant tab)
"See all funds with performance data" CTA → PERE Classic fund listing page (pre-filtered)
Business Rules
Component & columns
Component labelled as 
"Recent Fund Performance"
, positioned as the second component in the Fund Performance tab under the Equity pill
Column headers displayed:
Fund — fund name
DPI — multiple format (e.g. 1.6x)
TVPI — multiple format (e.g. 1.13x)
IRR — percentage format (e.g. 8.41%)
Vintage year - year format (e.g. 2017)
Data Source — institution name (only the institution that reported the latest performance data)
Order of display
 
Only list 
10 funds
 in the component 
Only funds with performance data from the 
latest quarter
 are listed 
Ordered alphabetically (A–Z)
 
Ordered by Latest reported
Fund criteria
 (only include funds matching all of below) 
Fund Status = Closed
Fund Stage = Final Close 
Fund Asset Class = 
Real Estate
 
(PERE equivalent of II's "Infrastructure")
 
Institution Partner Status = GP, GP Fund Focus = 
PERE / RE
, 
AND 
IsActive = True 
Fund's Reported Performance 
must
 have a Data Source. 
It should not be empty
Fund hyperlink
 
All funds listed are hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the fund name, 
THEN 
they are navigated to the fund profile on PERE Classic AND land on the 
Performance History tab
 
Fund profile opens in 
the same page
Data source hyperlink
 
All institutions listed in the Data Source column are hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the institution name 
AND 
the institution is a GP
THEN 
they are navigated to the GP profile on PERE Classic 
AND 
land on the 
Funds Managed tab
 
IF 
user clicks the institution name 
AND 
the institution is an LP
THEN 
they are navigated to the LP profile on PERE Classic AND land on the 
Fund Commitments tab
 
"See all funds with performance data" CTA
 
The text "See all funds with performance data" is displayed at the bottom of the component, hyperlinked 
IF 
user clicks the link, 
THEN 
they are navigated to the Fund tab on PERE Classic 
AND 
view a 
pre-filtered list of funds
:
"Only funds with performance data" filter applied
Results table ordered by "Size' (Desc order)
The pre-filter logic only applies when navigating from this CTA — not when arriving at the Fund tab through other routes
Rules:
The pre-filter is same as II, except for PERE-Equity, the strategy filter should be everything except Debt

 
CTA destination opens in the same page
No regression
 
No regression to existing II Recent Fund Performance behaviour (per WEB-24113, WEB-24115, WEB-24116, WEB-24117)
UI to be same as II
Out of Scope
Applying global filters to the Recent Fund Performance component
Persistent filters (strategy, region & sector) on the Fund Listing page
Adding a performance filter to the PERE Fund Listing page if not already present — handled by ST3 
(see RAID open question) - cannot be tested util rewrite is developed
Credit pill equivalent — covered in separate story
10 Largest Real Estate Funds — covered in 
WEB-28739
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma:

 
UI:
PERE classic site links
See all redirection - 
Funds | Database | PERE
Fund redirection (e.g. Blackstone) - Funds|Performance history
Data source redirection - 
Blackstone | GP | Manager Provided Overview
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Open question for Tech -
 
Does the PERE Classic Fund Listing page already have the "Only funds with performance data" filter (equivalent of II's WEB-24052), or does ST3 need a new ticket to add it on the PERE Classic side? The CTA in this story depends on landing on a pre-filtered fund list — if the filter doesn't exist on PERE Classic, this is a dependency
Assumption - 
Since the above cannot be found out until redirection is in place (ST11 task), we would make an assumption that there would be no ST3 work on this

Given
 that I am a GP user 
When
 I click the Equity pill and select the Fund Performance tab 
Then
 I should see the Recent Fund Performance component 
And
 the columns Fund, DPI, TVPI, IRR, and Data Source are displayed 
And
 the component lists only 10 funds with performance data from the latest quarter 
And
 funds are ordered Size(desc order)
AC2 — Fund criteria applied
Given
 AC1 
When
 the Recent Fund Performance component renders 
Then
 only funds matching all of the following criteria are listed: Fund Status = Closed, Fund Stage = Final Close, Fund Asset Class = Real Estate, Institution Partner Status = GP with GP Fund Focus = PERE / RE and IsActive = True 
And

AC3 — Navigate to Fund Profile (Performance History tab)
Given

When
 I click a fund's name in the table 
Then

And
 the page opens in the same page

Given
 AC1 
And
 the institution listed in the Data Source column is a GP

 I click the institution name in the table 
Then

And
 the page opens in the same page
 
AC5 — Navigate to LP Profile from Data Source
Given
 AC1 
And
 the institution listed in the Data Source column is an LP
When
 I click the institution name in the table 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Fund Commitments tab on the LP profile (PERE Classic) 

And
 the page opens in the same page 
AC6 — Navigate to "See all funds with performance data"
Given
 AC1 
When
 I click the "See all funds with performance data" link at the bottom of the component 
Then
 I should be navigated to the Fund tab on PERE Classic 
And
 the "Only funds with performance data" filter is pre-applied
And
 the results table is ordered by Size (Desc order)
And
 the page opens in the same page
Test case— No regression to existing II behaviour
Given
 the existing II homepage is rendered 
When
 the Recent Fund Performance component renders 
Then
 it continues to function as per WEB-24113 / WEB-24115 / WEB-24116 / WEB-24117 with no regression
UX
AC1
 — Build to be responsive to mentioned resolutions

 — Hover behaviour same as II
