title: PERE OBX > Targeted Intelligence > Equity-Investor Activity-Investor news > integration for latest investor news
description:
Background
The Latest Investor News component on the Equity > Investor Activity tab currently renders mock data. This story integrates the backend data layer (built in the separate backend story) with the existing frontend so the component surfaces live investor articles from the PERE WordPress source.
Pre-Requisite
Backend story for Latest Investor News must be complete
"
OBX Primary" admin tag must be available on PERE News WordPress
USER STORY 
As a
 logged-in or logged-out user 
I want 
the Investor Activity tab to display real news articles from the editorial content platform 
So that 
I can access the latest relevant investor news directly from the homepage
Scope
 
Integrate the backend data fetch with the Latest Investor News component on the Equity > Investor Activity tab so it surfaces live articles from PERE News WordPress.
 
Business Rules
Content sourcing
The section must fetch the latest articles from the WordPress Investors category
Each article must include all the data points needed to render the card (headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and any associated tags / categories)
Rendering
The section renders 1 primary news card and up to 5 secondary news cards (6 total)
Primary card displays: headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and tag badges
Secondary cards display: headline, publication date, reading time, and tag badges
When fewer than 6 articles are available, only the available articles are rendered with no empty placeholders or errors
Error & empty states
Empty states - surface same behavior as II
Error states-Have treatment same as II for now
Navigation
Each news card navigates to the correct article detail page
"See all" footer link navigates to the Investor news listing page (/news-analysis/investors)
Out of Scope
Backend data fetch layer (covered in separate backend story)
UI changes — UI is already built
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike ref: 

 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Dependency: "OBX Primary" admin tag must be deployed PERE News WordPress
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Articles fetched from correct source
 
GIVEN 
the Latest Investor News component is rendered on the PERE homepage 
WHEN 
the section loads 
THEN 
the latest Investor articles are fetched from the PERE News WordPress source 
AND 
each article includes the data points required to render the card (headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, tags) 
AND 
the same logic applies on the II homepage, with articles fetched from the II source
AC2 — Rendering
 
GIVEN 
articles are returned 
WHEN 
the section renders 
THEN 
1 primary card displays headline, summary, publication date, reading time, featured image, and tag badges 
AND 
up to 5 secondary cards display headline, publication date, reading time, and tag badges 
AND 
when fewer than 6 articles are returned, only the available articles render with no empty placeholders or errors
AC3 — Error and empty states
 
GIVEN 
the content source returns an error 
WHEN 
the section attempts to render 
THEN 
a graceful error state is shown with no unhandled exceptions 
AND 
GIVEN no articles are returned for the category, an appropriate empty state is rendered
Rules 
-Treatment for Empty state and error to be 
Same behavior as II
AC4 — Navigation
GIVEN 
any news card is rendered 
WHEN 
the user clicks the card 
THEN 
the user is navigated to the correct article detail page 
AND 
GIVEN the user clicks the "See all" footer link, the user is navigated to the equity- Investor news landing page
Note - ST11 is working on this and once the UI is buil click on this would render the section here
As a logged-in/out? user I want to view real-time�data growth metric So that I can quickly get insights into the PERE

description:
Background

So that

Integrate the backend data layer with the News & Analysis frontend component so that each column (Equity, Credit, Deals) renders live articles from its respective WordPress source.

title: PERE OBX > News & Analysis > backend for equity, credit and deals
description:
Background
The News & Analysis section on the PERE homepage needs to surface the latest articles for three asset class columns — Equity, Credit, and Deals — each from its own dedicated WordPress source. This follows the existing OBX pattern used for Podcasts, Roundtables, and Thought Leadership.
This story covers backend changes only. UI is already built. Integration with the homepage is covered in a separate story.
Pre-Requisite
News & Analysis section UI is already built
USER STORY 
As a
 developer 
I want 
the News & Analysis section to dynamically fetch articles for Equity, Credit, and Deals from their respective WordPress sources 
So that
 the component can surface the latest brand-specific news on the PERE homepage
Scope
 
Build the backend data layer for the News & Analysis section to fetch the latest articles for three asset class columns:
Equity — from PERE Equity WordPress source
Credit — from PERE Credit WordPress source
Deals — from PERE Deals WordPress source
The backend should be configurable and reusable, following the same pattern as existing content sections (Podcasts, Roundtables, Thought Leadership).
Business Rules
Backend must accept brand context and map dynamically to the correct WordPress source per column:
Equity → PERE Equity WordPress source
Credit → PERE Credit WordPress source
Deals → PERE Deals WordPress source
Each column fetches the latest articles independently, sorted by publication date (most recent first)
Number of articles fetched per column to match the design (4 cards visible + "See all" CTA per column)
Logic to exclude certain tags noted in -

 
Out of Scope
Frontend integration of the backend response with the News & Analysis component on the PERE homepage (covered in separate story)
UI changes — UI is already built
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike reference: 

 
UI: 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Assumption:
 All three WordPress sources (PERE Equity, PERE Credit, PERE Deals) are configured and reachable per the spike's fallbackConfig
Dependency:
 GraphQL plugin must be present and active on all three WordPress instances
Question for Design
 - For the first article shown in each of the three columns, is the image intended to remain static? - No, it should render the featured image as present in article | If no image exists - fall back image to be used
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Backend accepts brand context and maps to correct source
 
GIVEN 
a developer triggers the News & Analysis data fetch for a given column (Equity, Credit, or Deals) 
WHEN 
the fetch is called with the column's brand context 
THEN 
the data fetch maps to the correct WordPress source (PERE Equity / PERE Credit / PERE Deals)
AC2 — Each column returns the latest articles
 
GIVEN 
the data fetch is triggered for any column 
WHEN 
the WordPress query runs 
THEN 
the latest articles are returned, sorted by publication date (most recent first) 
AND
 the number of articles returned matches the design specification
Test case— No regression to existing II behaviour
 
GIVEN 
the existing II homepage is rendered 
WHEN 
the News & Analysis backend fetches data 
THEN 
existing II news fetching behaviour continues with no regression

title: PERE OBX > Homepage > Bento Box > integration for Data Snapshot
description:
Background
The Data Room bento box on the homepage currently renders mock data. This story integrates the backend data layer (built in the separate backend story) with the existing frontend so the component surfaces the latest Data Room article from the PERE WordPress source.
Pre-Requisite
Backend story for Data Room must be complete
USER STORY 
As a
 logged-in or logged-out user
I want 
the Data Room bento box to display the latest data visualization article from the editorial content platform 
So that 
I can easily discover and access proprietary data-driven insights from the homepage
Scope
 
Integrate the backend data fetch with the Data Room bento box component so it surfaces the most recent Data Room article from PERE WordPress.
Business Rules
Content sourcing
The Data Room bento box must fetch the latest article tagged as "Data Room" content from the active brand's WordPress source
Exactly ONE article is displayed — the most recently published Data Room article
Rule - When a new Data Room article is published, it automatically replaces the previous one
Rendering
 - Bento box renders the following elements per the existing II UI:
Component title: "Data Snapshot" 
Article headline
Featured image (chart icon)
Article tag?
If the article title is longer than 2 lines, it is truncated with ellipses and on hover a tooltip shows the full title
Error & empty states
If no Data Room article is available, the bento box renders an appropriate empty state -Similar to II
Navigation
 
Clicking anywhere on the Data snapshot section (title, headline, or image) navigates the user to the full article page in the same browser tab
Out of Scope
Backend data fetch layer (covered in separate backend story)
UI changes — UI is same as II and already on stage
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Spike ref: 

 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Dependency:
 "Content Type: Data Room" taxonomy must be configured on PERE WordPress and articles tagged before integration can be tested with real content
Open question for Product:
 Confirm PERE Data Room content is sourced from PERE News WordPress only
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality

GIVEN 
the Data Room bento box is rendered on the PERE homepage 
WHEN 
the bento box loads
THEN 
the most recently published Data Room article is fetched from the PERE News WordPress source 
AND 
only one article is displayed 
AC2 — Rendering
 
GIVEN 
an article is returned 
WHEN 
the bento box renders 
THEN 
it displays the component title "Data Snapshot", the article headline, and the featured image (chart icon)
AND 
if the title is longer than 2 lines, it is truncated with ellipses and a tooltip on hover shows the full title - Same as II
AC3 — Error and empty states
 
GIVEN 
the content source returns an error 
WHEN 
the bento box attempts to render
THEN 
a graceful error state is shown with no unhandled exceptions 
AND 
GIVEN no Data Room article is returned, an appropriate empty state is rendered (same as II)
AC4 — Navigation
 
GIVEN 
the Data Room bento box is rendered 
WHEN 
the user clicks anywhere on the component (title, headline, or image) 
THEN 
the user is navigated to the full article page in the same browser tab

title: PERE OBX > Homepage > Data Ribbon > backend + integration to show real-time counts (with growth metrics)
description:
Background
This story wires up the 
real-time counts
 for each card in the data ribbon — 
Network members (static for now) - 

 to check with ST10, Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, Service providers — sourced from the relevant APIs with brand context = 
PERE
.
This is the PERE equivalent of the
 II implementation 
delivered via:
WEB-24763
 — Investors card
WEB-24764
 — Fund Managers card
WEB-24765
 — Funds card
WEB-24766
 — Commitments card
WEB-24768
 — Deals card
WEB-26489
 — Service providers card
Pre-Requisite
Domain of PERE needs to be setup and it must be up and running
Across stage, dev and prod
Data Ribbon UI/layout is already built on the PERE OBX homepage (per 
WEB-28450
)
Fusion API endpoints for data ribbon data points already support brand context (per spike findings)
Click-through destinations on PERE Classic for each card (Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, Service Providers) must be available and reachable
USER STORY 
As a 
logged-in PERE user 
I want
 to see real-time counts in the data ribbon on the PERE OBX homepage 
So that 
I can quickly access PERE-specific data points
Scope
Wire up the backend + integration layer for the PERE Data Ribbon to display 
real-time counts
 for the following 7 cards, all sourced from the corresponding PERE data sources:
Network members
Funds
Commitments
Deals
Investors
Managers
Service providers
 (sum of Investment Consultants, Placement Agents, and Law Firms — same logic as II)
Business Rules:
Count display
Each card displays the live count for the PERE asset class sourced from the relevant API
Counts must be formatted with comma separators for readability (e.g. "4,368", "5,478", "11,125") - Same as II
Service providers card displays the sum of Investment Consultants + Placement Agents + Law Firms (same logic as II per WEB-26489)
Brand context
 
5. All API calls must pass brand context = 
PERE
 so the count returned is PERE-specific (not II) 
Click-through navigation
 
6. Each card must be clickable in its entirety (not just the chevron icon) and hyperlinked to the corresponding PERE Classic destination:
Funds
 → PERE Classic Fund database listing page (closed funds filter applied, same as II pattern)
Funds | Database | PERE
Commitments
 → PERE Classic Commitments tab on the investor database
Commitments | Database | PERE
Deals
 → PERE Classic Deals listing page
Private Real Estate Deal Data
Investors
 → PERE Classic LP database listing page
LP | Database | PERE
Managers
 → PERE Classic GP database listing page
GP | Database | PERE
Service providers
 → PERE Classic Investment Consultants tab
Investment Consultant | Database | PERE
Network members
 → 
Member directory of PERE
All click-through destinations open in the 
same tab
Loading state
 
9. While the count is being retrieved, the card should display a skeleton/placeholder state - same as II
Error state
 
10. If the API returns an error for any card, the card must remain visible (not hidden) and display an error message - same as II
Out of Scope
Data Ribbon UI changes
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Open question for Tech / Design:
 
What's the click destination for the 
Deals
 card on PERE —does it redirect to the PERE Deals brand site? - 
Private Real Estate Deal Data
Assumption: 
No change to the Salutation, in logged-out or logged-in view
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 — Data Ribbon displays live PERE counts
Given
 a user navigates to the PERE OBX homepage 
When
 the page loads 
Then
 the Data Ribbon displays the live counts for all 7 cards (
Network members
, Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, Service providers) 
And
 each count is sourced from the corresponding API with 
brand=pere
 
And
 each count is formatted with comma separators (e.g. "4,368") - same as II
AC2 — Service Providers count logic
Given
 the Service Providers card is rendered 
When
 the count is calculated 
Then
 the count equals the sum of Investment Consultants + Placement Agents + Law Firms for PERE 
And
 the label displays as "Service providers"
AC3 — Click-through navigation: Network members card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Network members
 card 
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Network members destination on PERE Classic  
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC3.1 — Click-through navigation: Funds card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Funds
 card
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Classic Fund database listing page with the closed funds filter applied 
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC3.2 — Click-through navigation: Commitments card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Commitments
 card (including the chevron icon) 
Then
 the user is navigated to the Commitments tab on the PERE Classic investor database 
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC3.3 — Click-through navigation: Deals card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Deals
 card
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Deals listing destination 
And
 the destination opens in the new tab - same as II
AC3.4 — Click-through navigation: Investors card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Investors
 card
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Classic LP database listing page 
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC3.5 — Click-through navigation: Managers card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Managers
 card
Then
 the user is navigated to the PERE Classic GP database listing page 
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC3.6 — Click-through navigation: Service providers card
Given
 a user views the PERE OBX homepage Data Ribbon 
When
 the user clicks anywhere on the 
Service providers
 card
Then
 the user is navigated to the Investment Consultants tab on PERE Classic 
And
 the destination opens in the same tab
AC4 — Loading state
Given
 the API call for any card is in progress 
When
 the data ribbon renders 
Then
 the card displays a skeleton/placeholder state 
And
 once the count is retrieved, the placeholder is replaced with the formatted count (same as II)

Replicate what error state we have II
AC6 — Data Ribbon renders for both user states
Given
 a user lands on the PERE OBX homepage

 the Data Ribbon renders for both states with live PERE counts
Rules:
The click on individual components will work as above only for logged-in user

Test case— No regression to existing II behaviour
Given
 the existing II OBX homepage is rendered 
When
 the Data Ribbon loads 
Then
 it continues to display live II counts with no regression
UX
AC1
 — Build to be responsive to mentioned resolutions

 — Hover behaviour same as II (visual feedback + cursor changes to pointer)

title: [QA] OBX PERE - Registration Confirmation Page - Verify PERE Logo, Page Header , Footer Links, H Tags  & Form submission flow
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story
As a  
product manager,
I want
 to ensure the Registration confirmation page on OBX PERE is correctly branded and all navigational elements are in place
So that 
the page is ready for users.
Test Case Scope 
To verify that the Registration Confirmation Page is implemented in the PERE brand
To verify the PERE logo, Page Header and Footer links are correctly rendered on the OBX PERE Registration confirmation  page.
To Verify that when an user successfully submits the registration form , they are navigated to the registration confirmation page.
To verify Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration confirmation form.
Out of scope
The CTA navigation in the page header. This will be verified once the CTAs are linked by the respective team during implementation.
The primary navigation dropdown menu items and linking. This will be verified once added by the respective team during implementation.
Footer menu items and linking. This will be verified once added by the respective team during implementation.
Business Rules:  
Verify that the Registration Confirmation Page is implemented in the PERE brand for OBX PERE site.
Verify that the PERE logo is displayed correctly in the page header
Verify that the page header is rendered correctly.
Verify that the primary navigation menu is displayed and accessible.
Verify that the footer and all associated elements are rendered correctly.
Verify that when an user successfully submits the registration form , they are navigated to the registration confirmation page.
Verify the  Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration Confirmation form.
H1
 We’ve created your account
P
 To activate your account please verify your email address.
p
 A link has been sent to 
<email address>.
UX/UI
OBX PERE > Registration confirmation page
OBX PERE > Registration confirmation 
Prototype > 
PERE (MVP v2) - Protoype
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Verify Header, Logo, and Footer on Registration Confirmation Page
GIVEN 
I am an anonymous user on the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I successfully submit my registration form
AND 
taken to the registration confirmation page
THEN 
I should see the page header displayed correctly
AND 
the PERE logo visible within the header
AND 
the footer displayed correctly at the bottom of the page
AND 
the H tags are added to the Registration confirmation page as defined by WAAC.

Scenario 2- Verify registration form submission flow
GIVEN 
I am an anonymous user on the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I successfully submit my registration form
THEN 
I should be taken to the registration confirmation page which displays correct messaging.

title: [QA] OBX PERE - Registration Page - Form Submission - Verify Loading State
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story
As a  
product manager,
I want
  to see a spinning loader after submitting the registration form,
So that 
users know their form submission is being processed and don’t attempt to resubmit the form.
Test Case Scope 
To verify the spinning loader within the “Create Account” CTA on the registration form.
Business Rules:
Verify that upon clicking the “Create Account” CTA after completing all mandatory fields, a spinning loader is displayed within the button and the label changes to “Loading”.
Verify that the loader appears immediately upon submission and remains visible until a response is received from the API.
Verify that once the loader is triggered, the “Create Account” button is disabled to prevent multiple submissions.
Verify that while the loader is in progress, the entire form is non-interactive, and users cannot edit fields, change dropdowns, or interact with any form elements.
Verify that on successful submission, the spinner is removed and the user is redirected to the confirmation page.
Verify that on submission failure, the spinner is removed, the button label reverts to “Create Account”, the CTA is re-enabled, and the form becomes editable again.
UX/UI
OBX PERE > Registration page > Loading Spinner
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Loading Spinner
GIVEN 
I am an anonymous user on OBX PERE
WHEN 
I navigate to the Registration page and interact with the form
AND 
I click on the “Create Account” CTA after completing all mandatory fields
THEN 
I should see a spinning loader with the label changed to “Loading”
AND 
the form should be disabled while the request is in progress
AND 
I should be redirected to the confirmation page upon successful submission.

title: [QA] OBX PERE - Registration Page - Verify Fields Display ,Validations and Header Tags
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story
As a  
product manager,
I want
 to ensure the registration page form fields, validations and error messages are working correctly on OBX PERE,
So that 
the page is ready for users.
Test Case Scope 
To verify the fields, field dropdown values, field validations and error messages are displayed correctly on the OBX PERE Registration form.
To verify Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration form.
Business Rules:  
Verify all fields are visible on the Registration form. Refer to table below.
Verify the user is able to enter their details into the input fields on the Registration form.
Verify the email field validation is triggered on focus out and all other field validations are triggered on form submission.
Verify the form fields retain the user's entered details when the data does not pass validation, so the user can update accordingly.
Verify the "Protected by reCAPTCHA v3" label is removed and no longer displayed below the Create Account CTA on the form.
Verify the  Semantic H tags are added to the  OBX PERE Registration form.
Field and Form Validations + Error Messages
Verify that if the user enters special characters into the First Name and Last Name fields, the system allows the defined special characters to be entered. 
Accepted format: 
^[a-zA-Z\u0080-\uFFFF\-'
., ]{1,50}$`
Verify that if the user attempts to enter a character outside the Zephr-supported set in the First Name or Last Name fields, the character is blocked and not accepted.
Verify the phone number field is not validated for 11 digits.
Verify inline validation for the email address field is displayed immediately after the validation check is performed (on focus out / blur).
Verify that if the user enters an email address that already exists in the system, the inline validation message 
“This email address is already registered. 
Sign in
 or use another email address”. 
is displayed next to the email field.
Verify that if the user enters an email address with a personal / blacklisted domain, the inline validation message 
“Please provide your work email address”. 
is displayed next to the email field.
The form field should retain previously entered values when the email validation is displayed
Verify the relevant error message is displayed on the form when the user enters data that does not pass field and form validation.
Verify that if the user attempts to submit the form without entering data for all mandatory fields, the validation message 
“Required field” 
is displayed next to the relevant field(s).
Verify that if the user attempts to submit the form without entering data for all mandatory fields, the message 
“Please complete all fields” 
is displayed at the top of the form.
Verify that if the user enters an email address that already exists and attempts to submit the form, the message 
“This email address is already registered. 
Sign in
 or use another email address” 
is displayed.
Verify the Sign in text is hyperlinked and navigates the user to the existing sign in modal when clicked.
Verify that if the user enters an email address in an invalid format, the inline validation message 
“Email format not valid” 
is displayed next to the field.
Verify that if the user enters a value that does not match the expected format for a field, the inline validation message  
“Only numeric values and special characters are allowed”
 is displayed next to the field.
Verify that if the user attempts to submit the form without accepting the Terms & Conditions and privacy notice, the inline validation message 
“Agree to the terms and conditions to continue” i
s displayed.
Verify the T&Cs and Privacy Notice in the Registration form are hyperlinked and navigate the user to the T&C or privacy notice page for the relevant brand site when clicked.
Verify that when all input values are valid and the submission is successful, no validation or error messages are displayed when the user clicks the 
“Create account ” 
button.
Verify H1 and H2 tags are added to the OBX PERE Demo form . Refer to attached screenshot.
Field Type
Placeholder Text / Messaging
Validation Type
Rules
First Name
Free Text Box
/
Mandatory Field
Allow only characters supported by Zephr as per the regex: 
^[a-zA-Z\u0080-\uFFFF\-'., ]{1,50}$
Free text fields must be limited to 256 characters.
Last Name
Free Text Box
/
Mandatory Field
 Allow only characters supported by Zephr as per the regex: 
^[a-zA-Z\u0080-\uFFFF\-'., ]{1,50}$
Free text fields must be limited to 256 characters.
Organisation
Free Text Box
/
Mandatory Field
 Allow alphanumeric, special characters and  numeric values. 
Free text fields must be limited to 256 characters.
Work Email
Free Text Box
/
Mandatory Field
Allow alphanumeric and special characters. Email must include @ symbol.
Free text fields must be limited to 256 characters.
Phone Number
Free Text Box
/
Mandatory Field
Allow numeric values and special characters only. 
Country
Drop-down List
Select country
For  Country = United States ; Select state
Mandatory Field
Use existing country taxonomy. Use taxonomy source that’s currently used on existing registration form.  Single select only
The state drop-down menu should be display only when United States is selected 
Business Category
Drop-down List
 
Select business category
Mandatory Field
Use existing business category taxonomy. Use taxonomy source that’s currently used on existing registration form. Single select only
Job Title
Drop-down List
 
Select job title
Mandatory Field
Use existing job title taxonomy. Use taxonomy source that’s currently used on existing registration form. Single select only
Job Function
Drop-Down List
Select job function
Mandatory Field
Use existing job function taxonomy. Use taxonomy source that’s currently used on existing registration form.  Single select only 
T&Cs and Privacy Notice
Tick Box and 
Hyperlinks 
 
 
Mandatory Field
Display message alongside the tick-box.

[tick-box] 
I agree to Infrastructure Investor’s terms and conditions and privacy notice
Already have an account? 
Sign in
Hyperlink 
Optional Field
Create account
Clickable Button
 
Optional Field
 
UX/UI
OBX PERE > Registration page
Registration page> Semantic markup  > Header Tags
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Registration Form Fields, Validations & Error Messages
GIVEN
 I am an anonymous user on OBX PERE,
WHEN
 I navigate to the Registration page and interact with the form,
THEN
 all fields, dropdown values, field validations and error messages should be displayed and function correctly as mentioned in the business rules above.
AND 
the H tags are added to the Registration form as defined by WAAC.

title: OBX PERE - Marketing Landing Page - Hero Section - Product Video/Image, Existing Subscribers, Client Endorsements Sections & Hero CTAs
description:
Background 
As part of the One Brand Experience (OBX) initiative, we are introducing a marketing landing page for the OBX PERE site, with its own dedicated URL to showcase PEI's value proposition, product range and available subscriptions. To build this marketing page, we will be using a tool called 
Builder.io
.
User Story 
As a
 PEI Marketer, 
I want 
the Hero Section to be displayed on the marketing landing page, 
So that 
customers can can instantly grasp the main message and engage with content.
Scope 
To import the following assets into 
Builder.io
 for the OBX PERE marketing landing page.
Hero Section - Heading and description , Request a Demo CTA with CTA linking , Explore the Product CTA with CTA linking and Option to upload a video or static image with an embedded video player
Customer Base Section - Text, count and avatars
Client Endorsement Section - Text and client logos
To provide the marketing team the ability to edit or update all assets and copy within the Hero, Customer Base and Client Endorsement sections directly in 
Builder.io
.
Out of Scope 
All other sections of the marketing landing page will be handled in separate tickets.
Business Rules
Hero Section
Ensure the hero section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the hero section heading is implemented with a H1 Tag and with the text: "
Smarter, faster private real estate investment decision making"
 
Ensure the description in the hero section reads as "
Whether you’re a manager, investor or service provider, PEI Private Real Estate delivers unique private market insights to guide better, more impactful business decisions.
"
Ensure the Request a Demo CTA renders on the marketing landing page and is linked to the OBX PERE /request-demo/ page.
Ensure the Explore the Product CTA renders on the marketing landing page and navigates users to the OBX PERE Homepage when clicked.
Hero Section - Media Upload
Ensure 
Builder.io
 provides an option to upload either a video file or a static image to the hero section, with the ability to change the selected media at any time.
Ensure only one media type (video or static image) is displayed in the hero section at any given time, retaining the correct dimensions, quality and formatting as defined in Figma when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure that if a video is uploaded, a video player is rendered in the hero section; if a static image is uploaded, it displays at the correct dimensions and styling as defined in Figma.
Note: Adding a video or image to the hero section is optional. The video will be recorded and uploaded by the marketing team.
Customer Base Section
Ensure the customer base section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the text in this section reads 
"Join 16,000+ private real estate professionals".
Client Endorsement Section
Ensure the client endorsement section is imported and matches the style in the Figma file when rendered on the frontend.
Ensure the text in this section reads
 "Trusted by".
Ensure the following client logos are displayed in this section :
BGO
GIC
Brookfield
KKR
Blackstone

https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
Ensure the marketing team is able to edit and update all assets and copy within the Hero, Customer Base and Client Endorsement sections directly in 
Builder.io
.
Ensure data test ids are added to the Hero Section, Media upload, Customer base and Client endorsement sections
UX / UI 
OBX PERE Marketing Landing Page > Hero Section, Embedded Media, Customer Base & Client Endorsements
Assets - 

 
Figma Link >
https://www.figma.com/design/e1cB67795LuBcH9ALfsuhc/PERE-Marketing-experience-%E2%80%93-UI?node-id=125-2720&t=TSPzRehGS9y2GNQi-1
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations 
None

GIVEN
 I am a PEI marketer,
AND
 the hero section has been successfully imported from Figma into 
Builder.io
,
WHEN
 I navigate to the OBX PERE marketing landing page,
THEN
 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND
 the heading and description should be displayed as mentioned 
AND 
the heading should be implemented with a 
<h1>
 Tag

 the Request a Demo and Explore the Product CTAs should be displayed and linked correctly

AND

 I am a PEI marketer,
AND
 the customer base section has been successfully imported from Figma into 
Builder.io
,
WHEN
 I navigate to the OBX PERE marketing landing page,
THEN
 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND
 the text, count and avatars should be displayed as mentioned.

Scenario3 - Client Endorsement Section Rendered Correctly

 I am a PEI marketer,
AND
 the client endorsement section has been successfully imported from Figma into 
Builder.io
,
WHEN
 I navigate to the OBX PERE marketing landing page,
THEN
 the styling should match the Figma design when rendered on the frontend,
AND
 the text and client logos should be displayed as mentioned

 I am a PEI marketer with access to 
Builder.io
,

Builder.io
,

title: OBX PERE & OBX II - Registration Page (Anonymous Users) - Add Text Input Function to Country, Job Title & Job Tile Field & Country
description:
Background
We are introducing a new registration page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit registration request and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX PERE Fusion site.
User Story 
As a
n Marketer
I want
 certain field in the registration form to have a type function  on OBX II and OBX PERE
So that
 prospects can quickly find the correct value in the list
Scope 
To add text input functionality to the Country, Job Title and Job Function dropdown fields on the regsitration form for OBX PERE & OBX II.
Out of Scope 
Creating a new configuration for the existing registration page to be available on OBX PERE. This is handled separately.
Copy change of ' I Agree' and Hyperlinks of T&C and privacy notice. This is handled separately.
Adding Testimonial and Image to the registration  form. This is handled separately.
Zephr Linking to PERE Web Activities / Hooks. This is handled separately.
Business Rules 
Ensure the Country, Job Title and Job Function fields accept free-text input from the user on the registration form for OBX PERE & OBX II
Ensure the dropdown filters and displays only matching values as the user types in the Country , Job Title or Job Function field based on the existing values already available in the dropdown list.
Ensure matching is triggered if any character entered appears anywhere within the full dropdown value
Ensure a "No matching job titles" & "No matching job functions" &  "No matching countries" message is displayed in the dropdown if no matches are found for the value entered in the Job Title or Job Function field or Country
Ensure the final value saved is a selection from the pre-existing dropdown list for both Job Title and Job Function fields; free-text only entries must not be accepted.
Ensure data test-id are added to all sections of the registration form.
Introduce a split if needed (Optional - To be decided  by the development team)
UX / UI 
OBX PERE > Registration Page > Testimonial and Image Displayed 
Job Title - Text input function
Job Function - Text input function
Prototype Link > 
https://share-format-55727347.figma.site/request-demo
  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 - Add free text input to Country, Job Title & Job Function fields on OBX II & OBX PERE  Registration form
GIVEN
 I am an anonymous user on OBX II & OBX PERE ,
WHEN
 I view the registration from,
THEN
 I should see the Country , Job Title and Job Function fields accept free-text input,
AND
 I should see the dropdown immediately filter and display matching values from the existing dropdown list as I type
AND
 matching should be triggered from the first character entered
AND
 matching should work if the entered character(s) appear anywhere within the value the dropdown list
AND
 I should see only relevant suggestions based on the entered input
Scenario 2 - No matching results message is displayed on OBX II & OBX PERE  Registration form
GIVEN
 I am an anonymous user on OBX II & OBX PERE,
WHEN
 I view the registration from,
AND 
I type a value in the Job Title field that does not match any existing dropdown value,
THEN
 I should see a "No matching job titles" & "No matching job functions" & “No matching countries” message displayed in the dropdown,
Scenario 3 - Final value must be selected from the pre-existing dropdown list on OBX II & OBX PERE  Registration form
GIVEN
 I am an anonymous user on OBX II & OBX PERE
WHEN
 I view the registration from,
AND 
I type in the Country, Job Title or Job Function field and select a value from the filtered dropdown list,
THEN
 I should see the selected value populate the field,
AND
 I should not be able to save or submit a value that is not from the pre-existing dropdown list.

Scenario 4 -Add data test IDs to all sections of the Registration form
GIVEN
 I am viewing the OBX II & OBX PERE registration form
WHEN
 the page is loaded
THEN
 I should see data test IDs added to all sections of the registration  form
AND
 each section should have a unique and identifiable data test ID for testing purposes

title: OBX PERE & OBX II- Request a Demo Submission Confirmation Page (Registered Base & Lite Users) - Update Copy
description:
Background
We’ve introduced a new ‘Request a Demo’ experience on the Fusion pages across OBX II and OBX PERE, with an improved user interface to enable prospects to submit demo requests and allow the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. As part of this, new submission confirmation pages have also been introduced. For Lite and Base registered users who click the ‘Request a Demo’ CTA, the new fusion pages will see the new OBX Request a Demo submission registered users confirmation page, ensuring a more consistent and seamless user experience across both brands.
Scope 
To display updated confirmation messaging for registered users (free lite and paid base/base trial) on the Request a Demo submission confirmation page across OBX PERE and OBX II.
This applies only to “Request a Demo” CTAs clicked on the new Fusion pages including the Demo CTA on network components and editorial pages
Out of scope
Replace classic submission confirmation banner with new fusion confirmation page. This is handled 

 
Business Rules:
 
Ensure this messaging is updated to both OBX PERE and OBX II brand demo confirmation pages.
Ensure this update is applied only to CTAs from the new Fusion pages including the Demo CTA on network components and editorial pages
IF a user is a registered free lite user OR a registered paid base/base trial user, AND they click the “Request a Demo” CTA on Fusion pages, THEN the user should be navigated directly to the submission confirmation page without displaying the form.
Submission confirmation page - 
https://www.infrastructureinvestor.com/request-demo/confirmation/
 
This page should open in the same tab.
Ensure existing Zephr web activities/hooks are triggered to send the demo request to the CRM end.
No changes should be made to the existing OBX Fusion submission confirmation message for anonymous users. This has already been implemented. 
Please Note: The request a demo form does not be displayed for already registered users as their details already exist in our system. This is as per existing existing behaviour. 
OBX Fusion Request a Demo Confirmation Submission Page (Lite & Base / Base Trial Users)
Heading: display the text 
‘Request received’ 
Description: display the text 
'A member of our team will be in touch shortly to schedule your consultation.'
Refer to design below. 
UX/UI
OBX II > Demo confirmation page for registered users( free lite and paid base/base trial)
OBX PERE >  Demo confirmation page for registered users (free lite and paid base/base trial)
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 - OBX II: Display Request a Demo Confirmation Page for Signed-in Users (Registered - Lite, Base & Base Trial)
GIVEN 
I am a registered lite, base, or base trial user
AND 
I am logged into the OBX Infrastructure Investor (OBX II) site
WHEN 
I click on the “Request a Demo” CTA on a Fusion pages
THEN 
I should be navigated to the OBX Fusion request a demo submission confirmation page in the same tab
AND 
the registered user messaging should be displayed
AND 
the Request a Demo form should not be displayed
AND 
the existing Zephr web activities/hooks should trigger the downstream demo request process
Scenario 2 - OBX PERE: Display Request a Demo Confirmation Page for Signed-in Users (Registered - Lite, Base & Base Trial)
GIVEN 
I am a registered lite, base, or base trial user
AND 
I am logged into the OBX PERE site
WHEN 
I click on the “Request a Demo” CTA on a Fusion pages
THEN 
I should be navigated to the OBX Fusion request a demo submission confirmation page in the same tab
AND 
the registered user messaging should be displayed
AND 
the Request a Demo form should not be displayed
AND 
the existing Zephr web activities/hooks should trigger the downstream demo request process

title: OBX PERE - Request a Demo Page (Anonymous Users)- Zephr Linking to PERE Web Activities / Hooks
description:
Background
We are introducing a new Request a Demo page for OBX PERE with an improved user interface, enabling prospects to submit demo requests and allowing the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. This page will be implemented on the new OBX Fusion infrastructure site.
As a
 Sales Manager
I want 
the request a demo form to be configured for OBX PERE
So that
 it can be integrated in Zephr with PERE specific web activities. 
Scope 
To integrate the new request a demo form with Zephr for OBX PERE site.
Note: the request a demo form is only required for 
anonymous users
 only. The request a demo form is not required for 
registered users
 as their details will already be stored in our system. Registered users will just see the confirmation message when they click any request a demo CTA. This is as per existing behavior on OBX II site.
Out of Scope
Creating a new configuration for the existing Request a Demo page/form to be available on OBX PERE. This is handled separately.
Business Rules & Web Hooks
Ensure that IF  a prospect successfully submits the request a demo form, AND the contact does not exist in the CRM under the email address provided, THEN a new account is created in Zephr for the user.
Ensure the Zephr Registration endpoint is used for account creation as per the Zephr documentation (
/blaize/register
).
Ensure the web activity brand
 
=
 
“PEIPRE” ( Dev & Stage)  (This is set in AWS Parameter store and is not part of the ticket)
The Web Activity Sub-Type =
 This is set in the Zephr webhook.
Ensure that for an anonymous user requesting a demo, the user is created in Zephr only if the
 “pere_demo_request_at_registration”
 
checkbox is checked/true.
Ensure that the web activity is associated only with the PERE product and does not map to any other brand.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
User Attribute added in dev/stage/prod: pere_demo_request_at_registration  

 
Zephr Feature updated and live for PERE (stage): 

  
https://console.zephr.com/products/features/sdk/obx-demo-request
 
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1  - Successfully Submit Request a Demo Form - Create Web Activity
GIVEN
 that I am Anonymous user, 
WHEN
 I enter all mandatory fields on the request a demo form on OBX PERE
AND
 I successfully submit a demo request,
THEN
 a new account should be created in Zephr for the user,
AND
 a contact should be created in CRM, 
AND
 the Boolean 
pere_demo_request_at_registration
 should be populated in the user attribute in Zephr
AND 
the web activity is associated only with the PERE product

title: PERE OBX Ask PEI Homescreen UI
description:
Background
The Ask PEI interface on PERE OBX displays a subheader beneath the Ask PEI logo. As PERE OBX is a private equity real estate focused platform, the subheader copy must reflect the correct brand context rather than using the II OBX copy.
User Story
AS A
 user accessing Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 the sub-header beneath the Ask PEI logo to accurately describe the feature in the context of PERE OBX,

SO THAT
 I have a clear understanding of what Ask PEI is when I open it on the PERE OBX platform.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to update the subheader copy beneath the Ask PEI logo on PERE OBX to read "AI assistant for private real estate" and to add the link to the updated privacy policy <link TBC>.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the Ask PEI subheader on II OBX
Any changes to the Ask PEI logo
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic
Any other copy changes within Ask PEI beyond the subheader
Business Rules:
The subheader beneath the Ask PEI logo on PERE OBX must read "AI assistant for private real estate".
NFRs:
Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
UX/UI

 Screenshot provided as design reference. The subheader sits directly beneath the Ask PEI logo and must retain its existing styling with the copy updated only.

 a user opens Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

 the interface renders,

title: Ask PEI - Implement PERE OBX replicated as in II OBX
description:
Background
Ask PEI has been enabled on PERE OBX. To ensure users receive responses relevant to PERE OBX content, the correct brand value must be sent in the Fusion API call. Without this, Ask PEI cannot differentiate between II OBX and PERE OBX and may return responses from the wrong asset class. The brand value 
pere
 must be passed in the API call when Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX.
User Story
AS A
 user interacting with Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 my queries to return results relevant to private real estate,

SO THAT
 I receive accurate and contextually correct responses from Ask PEI on the PERE OBX platform.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to ensure the correct brand value is sent in the Fusion API call when Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX, so that responses are scoped to the PERE asset class.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the Fusion API itself
Any changes to Ask PEI on II OBX
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic beyond passing the correct brand value
Business Rules:
When Ask PEI is accessed from PERE OBX, the brand value 
pere
 or 
pereportfolio
 must be sent in the Fusion API call, resolving to the Private Equity Real Estate asset class.
When Ask PEI is accessed from II OBX, the brand value 
ii
 or 
infraportfolio
 must continue to be sent in the Fusion API call, resolving to the Infrastructure Investor asset class.
The brand value sent must correctly differentiate between II OBX and PERE OBX to ensure users receive contextually relevant responses.
NFRs:
No regression to existing Ask PEI behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
The correct brand value must be sent consistently across all Ask PEI interactions on PERE OBX.
UX/UI
Not applicable. This is a backend integration change with no user-visible impact beyond receiving contextually correct responses.
Screenshot:
Figma Link:  
Input
Resolves to
Asset class
ii, infraportfolio
ii
Infrastructure Investor
pere, pereportfolio
pere
Private Equity Real Estate
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: PERE Brand Value Sent in Fusion API Call
GIVEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the Fusion API call is made,

THEN
 the brand value 
pere
 or 
pereportfolio
 is sent in the API call.
AC2: Ask PEI Returns PERE-Relevant Responses
GIVEN
 the correct brand value is being sent in the Fusion API call,

WHEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on PERE OBX,

THEN
 the response returned is scoped to the Private Equity Real Estate asset class and is relevant to PERE OBX content.

GIVEN
 the PERE brand value has been configured for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 a user submits a query via Ask PEI on II OBX,

 the brand value 
ii
 or 
infraportfolio
 continues to be sent in the Fusion API call and II OBX responses are unaffected.

 the brand value change has been implemented for PERE OBX,

 a user interacts with Ask PEI on II OBX,

title: SEO (on-live) - crawler to view brand logo as an alt image and not as a h1 tag
description:
Background
 Identified via post-GA Screaming Frog crawl (text-only) supplied by PEI. Single-component fix; high-leverage. Cross-reference: see Multiple H1 row (this single fix resolves the bulk of that finding). Open Brain entry created 13 May 2026.
Issue: 
On OBX-templated article pages, the H1 element contains the site brand name 'PEI Infrastructure Investor' (emitted from a site-logo wrapper component), not the article headline. The article headline is rendered as H2 instead. Affects 21,051 articles — essentially the entire OBX article corpus. SEO impact: H1 is a primary topical signal. Identical brand-name H1s across 21k articles flatten topical differentiation site-wide and make article-level keyword relevance harder for Google to identify. This is the root cause of most of the 'Multiple H1 per page' finding (see related row) — the brand-name H1 from the logo wrapper plus the article headline (which should be the H1) produces two H1s per page.
Solution:
Single templating change at the site-logo wrapper component in the OBX article template. The brand-name element should not be wrapped in an H1 tag (use a span, div, or aria-label-only logo image instead). The article headline element should be the H1. One component fix resolves this finding and the related Multiple H1 finding simultaneously
Pre-Requisite
 OBX-II is released
USER STORY 
As a crawler viewing the OBX II site pages

I want to be able to see page brand logo as an alt image and not as a h1 tag 

So that Google's initial crawl sees the structured data and doesn’t create duplicate H1 on the page
Scope
Business Rules
To ensure the brand-name to not have H1 tag
To ensure the brand logo is updated to Alt image HTML tag
Out of Scope
Updating Article pages to have a single H1 tag (after removing the H1 on the logo) - this has a dependency from design and will be handled in a separate ticket 
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
UI 
SEO Audit 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
None
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: OBX II header logo remove H1 tag
Given I am on the OBX II page
WHEN I view the content
THEN the Header logo HTML should be alt image only

 the user is navigated to the corresponding article page in
 a new tab
.
AC6: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN
 a user views the Deal News tab at >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports,

WHEN

title: View PERE News and Analysis in a dedicated tab in search results page
description:
Background
The PERE OBX search results page layout has been implemented [

 ]. This ticket covers the News & Analysis tab specifically, detailing how results are displayed, grouped, and presented to the user within that tab.
User Story
AS A
 user searching for content on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 to see News & Analysis results displayed in a clear and structured layout with the ability to filter by category,

SO THAT
 I can quickly scan and identify relevant articles from my search.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement the results display, category filtering, and card design for the News & Analysis tab on the PERE OBX search results page.
Out of Scope 
Search bar, tab structure, and pagination (covered in the search results page layout ticket [

]).
Any other search results tabs (covered in separate tickets).
Any changes to individual article pages.
Business Rules:
Ensure “News & Analysis” is the first tab on the search results page.
Ensure the II News & Analysis results are rendered based on the search term entered by the user
Search will use Algolia.
IF
 user enters a search term 
 Then 
finds matching news on the 
“Article Title”
 or 
“Article Body” against the RE WP admin
The search keyword can match 
anywhere in the article
A match is valid if the keyword appears in the 
article title
.
A match is also valid if the keyword appears in the 
article body
.
Display order sorted in descending order based on the published date, with the most recent articles shown first.
The News & Analysis tab must display category filter pills at the top of the results, each showing the category name and result count (e.g. Equity (13), Credit (15)).
Equity - PERE WP
Credit - PERE Credit WP
Results must be displayed in a single column layout.
 Maximum upto 10 results per page.
 Display total number of results on the bottom left of the grid a 1 -10 of N ( when N is the total no of results).
Each result card must display the following:
Article title with the search term highlighted within the title
Read time
Publication date
Article thumbnail image on the right side of the card
IF no image Then use the fallback image for an article  (Pending thumbnail size /square image from design)
Read time and publication date must be displayed on the same line, separated by a bullet point.
Each result card must be clickable and navigate the user to the corresponding article page.  - NOT IN SCOPE OF THIS TICKET.
Display the hand cursor when user hovers on the article title
IF
 user clicks the article title 
THEN
 user is navigated to the article page on the legacy site (PERE News) in the same tab.
Need 
not
 show the count when displaying “Search results for ‘search term’ in News & Analysis” as per image b. Also replicate for II OBX.
NFRs:
Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
Add data test IDs to the result card.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
image a:
image b:
Figma Link:  

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
This ticket is dependent on the search results page layout ticket [

 ] being completed first.
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Category Filter Pills Displayed
GIVEN
 a user performs a search and selects the News & Analysis tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 category filter pills are shown at the top of the results, each displaying the category name and result count,

AND
 Maximum up to 10 results per page,

AND
 Display total number of results on the bottom left of the grid a 1 -10 of N ( when N is the total no of results),

AND 
Display order sorted in descending order based on the published date, with the most recent articles shown first.
AC2: Single Column Layout
GIVEN
 a user views the News & Analysis tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 results are presented in a single column layout as per the design reference.
AC3: Contextual Label Displayed
GIVEN
 a user has performed a search and is viewing the News & Analysis tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 a contextual label is shown reading "Search results for '[query]' in News & Analysis".
AC4: Result Card Displays Correct Content
GIVEN
 results are displayed in the News & Analysis tab,

WHEN
 a user views a result card,

THEN
 the card displays: 

- the article title with the search term highlighted

- read time and publication date on the same line separated by a bullet point

- thumbnail image on the right side of the card.
AC7: Result Card is Clickable - 
NOT IN SCOPE OF THIS TICKET
GIVEN
 a user views a result card in the News & Analysis tab,

WHEN
 the user clicks on the card,

THEN
 a hand cursor is displayed when user hovers on the article card,

AND
 the user is navigated to the corresponding article page in same tab.
AC8: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN
 a user views the News & Analysis tab at >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 the result cards and category filter pills are correctly displayed across all supported viewports.
AC9: Contextual Label Displays Without Count
GIVEN
 a user has performed a search and is viewing any search results tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 the contextual label reads "Search results for '[query]' in [Tab Name]" with no result count included in the label,

AND
 this behaviour is consistent across both PERE OBX and II OBX

title: SEO (On-live) JSON-LD Schema to remove Admin in the URLs
description:
Observation 
Issue - JSON-LD article schema: hasPart.cssSelector references .td-post-content, a class that does not exist in OBX HTML
Owner - PEI dev team
Actual behavior  - OBX article schema declares hasPart.cssSelector: ".td-post-content" — a legacy tagDiv Newspaper-theme class. OBX HTML uses [data-testid="article-body"] for the body container. The declared selector resolves to nothing on the rendered page. Under Google's flexible sampling guidance, declaring a paywalled section but giving an invalid selector is structurally equivalent to not declaring it. Combined with the post-GA UA-based differential serving (Googlebot gets full body, default UA gets paywall stub), the pattern carries cloaking risk.
Expected behavior - Change hasPart.cssSelector value from .td-post-content to [data-testid="article-body"] (or the appropriate stable selector for the OBX article body container).
Stage url - 

 
Remove : Admin in the URLs

description:
Background

Loans

Real Estate

 the Credit pill is active and Investor Activity sub-tab is selected

 the user views the sub-tab

 the following components render with mock data per the design:

AC4 — Fund Performance sub-tab renders with mock data

 the Credit pill is active and Fund Performance sub-tab is selected

 the user views the sub-tab 
Then
 the following components render with mock data per the design:

AC5 — Loans sub-tab renders with mock data

 the Credit pill is active and Loans sub-tab is selected 
When
 the user views the sub-tab

 the following components render with mock data per the design:

 any sub-tab is active under the Credit pill

And

 — Hover behavior to be same as II

title: Create and Implement Magic Link Sign In Email Templates for PERE OBX and PERE Deals
description:
Background
SSO has been integrated on PERE OBX and PERE Deals. As part of the SSO authentication flow, an email is triggered to users to enable them to sign-in. 
Currently no dedicated email templates exist for either site, meaning users may receive emails that do not reflect the correct brand.
Branded email templates must be created and implemented for both PERE OBX and PERE Deals so that users receive a clear and consistent sign in experience.
User Story
AS A
 user attempting to sign in to PERE OBX or PERE Deals via SSO,

I WANT
 to receive a branded authentication email that clearly identifies the site I am signing in to,

SO THAT
 I can sign in with confidence and am not confused by a generic or incorrectly branded email.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to design and implement SSO authentication email templates for PERE OBX and PERE Deals.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the SSO authentication flow or logic.
Email templates for any sites other than PERE OBX and PERE Deals.
Any changes to existing II OBX email templates.
Business Rules:
A dedicated SSO authentication email template must be created and implemented for PERE OBX.
A dedicated SSO authentication email template must be created and implemented for PERE Deals.
Each template must clearly identify the site the user is signing in to.
The sign in link within each email must navigate the user to the correct destination for the respective site.
NFRs:
Email templates must render correctly across major email clients.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
No design available at this stage. Email template designs for PERE OBX and PERE Deals must be completed and signed off before development begins. This is a blocker for implementation.
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Dependency - Both templates must be designed and signed off by design team before development begins.
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: PERE OBX SSO Authentication Email Triggered
 
GIVEN
 a user initiates sign in on PERE OBX via SSO,

WHEN
 the authentication email is triggered,

THEN
 the user receives the PERE OBX branded SSO authentication email.
AC2: PERE Deals SSO Authentication Email Triggered
GIVEN
 a user initiates sign in on PERE Deals via SSO,

WHEN
 the authentication email is triggered,

THEN
 the user receives the PERE Deals branded SSO authentication email.
AC3: Sign In Link Navigates to Correct Destination
GIVEN
 a user receives an SSO authentication email for PERE OBX or PERE Deals,

WHEN
 the user clicks the sign in link,

THEN
 the user is navigated to the correct sign in destination for the respective site.
AC4: Templates Correctly Branded
GIVEN
 a user receives an SSO authentication email,

WHEN

GIVEN

WHEN

THEN

title: Add structured, user-correlated logging to Zephr retry wrapper
description:
Component:
 packages/auth
The Zephr retry wrapper added in 
fix/WEB-0000/zephr-createV4Session-retry
 emits retry attempts via 
console.warn
 with no user or request context. This makes it impossible to:
Correlate a retry log to the specific user / session it affected
Filter retry telemetry by 
email
, 
host
, 
tenant
, or 
module
 in DataDog
Cross-reference with the surrounding pino-structured logs in 
session.helper.ts
 (e.g. 
"Creating Zephr session..."
, 
"Zephr session created successfully"
)
Build dashboards/alerts off retry rate per user/tenant
Today, the only way to associate a retry to a user is to match timestamps manually between the unstructured 
[zephr]
 console line and the structured 
module:blaize-session
 pino log entries. That's not viable at scale.
Current log on first retry (no context):
[zephr] createV4Session attempt 1/3 failed (status=401), retrying in 247ms
Acceptance criteria:
Replace 
console.warn
 calls inside 
withZephrRetry
 in packages/auth/src/helpers/zephr/zephr-admin.helper.ts with the pino logger.
Accept an optional logger/context argument when calling 
withZephrRetry
, so callers can pass through 
email
, 
host
, 
tenant
, 
module: "blaize-session"
 (and any other scoped fields).
Wire 
createV4Session
 and 
getUserInfoByEmail
 to forward the caller's scoped logger (e.g. the 
scopedLog
 already built in 
createBlaizeSession
) into the retry wrapper.
Retry-attempt and giving-up logs must be structured JSON with at minimum these fields:
email
host
tenant
label
 (e.g. 
createV4Session
, 
getUserInfoByEmail
)
attempt
, 
maxAttempts
status
 (numeric HTTP code or 
"network"
)
delayMs
 (for retry-pending logs)
err.message
, 
err.stack
 (for terminal failure log)
module: "blaize-session"
 (so existing dashboards/filters still apply)
Log levels should match severity:
warn
 for retryable failure on attempt < maxAttempts
error
 for terminal failure (giving up)
Add a single DataDog query example to the PR description showing how to filter retries by user, e.g.:
module:blaize-session @label:createV4Session @email:"<user>"
No behavioral change to retry policy (same backoff, same status classifier, same max attempts).
Non-goals:
Changing which errors are retryable (
isRetryableZephrError
 stays as is)
Changing backoff timing
Replacing the 
ZephrHttpError
 type
Why now:
We recently shipped (and reverted) a fix for the silent "PH avatar / Welcome to Infrastructure Investor" degraded state caused by 
createV4Session
 swallowing 401s. The retry layer is the right long-term mitigation, but without user-correlated retry telemetry we can't tell whether retries are succeeding for real users, only that they happened somewhere in the fleet.
Links / context:
Originating fix branch: 
fix/WEB-0000/zephr-createV4Session-retry
 (commit 
1ebeed9e76
)
Related (reverted) fix: 
fix/WEB-0000/PH-resolution
 / 
feature/WEB-0000/PH-issue
Files: packages/auth/src/helpers/zephr/zephr-admin.helper.ts, apps/app-portfolio/lib/helpers/zephr/session.helper.ts, apps/app-main/lib/helpers/zephr/session.helper.ts

title: Ask PEI - FE URL Configuration
description:
Background
AskPEI surfaces different entities - articles, institutions, and funds - that the frontend needs to link out to. As the OBX rollout progresses, brand-specific domains are being consolidated into unified URLs (e.g. 
perenews.com
, 
perecredit.com
, and 
peredeals.com
 all consolidating to a single PERE domain).
Currently, both the frontend and AskPEI are independently setting URLs. This worked for the II OBX cutover, but creates two sources of truth that will visibly break when PERE OBX goes live - because the domain consolidation means hardcoded legacy domains will immediately produce incorrect links.
Two parallel problems need solving:
Frontend rendering
 - the FE must resolve the correct canonical URL to send users to, based on stable identifiers provided by AskPEI, not hardcoded domains.
WordPress canonical URLs
 - the URL stored against each article in WordPress must reflect the post-OBX domain structure, as this data is used for downstream data exports.
The PERE OBX cutover is the forcing function. The agreed model must be in place before that migration.
User Story
AS A
 user of AskPEI,

I WANT
 to be able to click on a link to an article, institution, or fund surfaced in a response,

SO THAT
 I should taken to the correct canonical URL on the current OBX domain - regardless of which brand I am on or whether the underlying domain has recently changed.
Scope 
Requirement 1 — Frontend URL Resolver
AskPEI must pass stable identifiers (not hardcoded URLs) to the frontend. The FE (or a shared resolver layer) owns the mapping from entity reference → current canonical URL, and resolves it at render time.
The resolver must handle five entity reference types as per table 1.
Institution URLs must carry three pieces of context: institution identity, role (LP, GP, LF, PA, IC), and section. Role is not optional — section names like "Contact information" and "Related news" appear across multiple roles, so role is part of the destination identity.
When a domain changes (e.g. 
perenews.com
 → new OBX URL), the FE must render the new URL without any changes required on the AskPEI side.
Requirement 2 — WordPress Canonical URLs
Article records in WordPress must have their URL field updated as part of the OBX migration to reflect new canonical URLs. Data exports used for sales must reflect these new URLs, not legacy ones. This ensures the FE resolver and the data sales export stay aligned on a single source of truth.
Out of Scope 
Legacy URL redirect strategy during and after OBX cutover (related but separate)
Defining the exact API contract between AskPEI and the FE (open question — see below)
Business Rules:
AskPEI must never return a hardcoded legacy domain URL to the frontend
The FE resolver is the single authority for translating entity references into canonical URLs
The static reference allowlist must be bounded and reviewable — AskPEI should not be able to cite arbitrary WordPress paths outside this list
WordPress canonical URLs must be updated as part of each OBX brand migration, not after
NFRs:
The resolver must degrade gracefully if an entity reference cannot be mapped — no broken links surfaced to users
Resolver logic must be brand-agnostic and extensible to new brands without code changes per brand
The institution role/section taxonomy is expected to grow — the resolver must not rely on per-type special cases
Table 1:
Type
Stable anchor
Notes
Article
slug
URL changes when hosting domain changes under OBX
Institution
data.peimedia.com/institutions/{id}
Resolver maps id + role + section → correct branded URL
Fund
data.peimedia.com/funds/{id}
Resolver maps id → 
/database/fund-profile/id/{fund_id}/{slug}/
Static reference
Named key (e.g. 
rankings
, 
fund-performance-methodology
)
Curated allowlist of WordPress pages AskPEI may cite
Deep breakdown
{institution} + {role} + {section} + {item_id}
Extends institution pattern one level deeper
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Open question:
 What is the agreed contract between AskPEI and the FE? Specifically — does AskPEI pass a 
data.peimedia.com
 stable ID, a structured object 
{type, id, role, section}
, or something else? This must be agreed before implementation begins.
Open question:
 How are legacy URLs redirected during and after the OBX cutover? (Related but tracked separately.)
Dependency:
 WordPress canonical URL updates are assumed to be part of each brand's OBX migration runbook — needs confirmation with the content/migration team.
Assumption:
 
data.peimedia.com/institutions/{id}
 and 
data.peimedia.com/funds/{id}
 remain stable anchors through and after the OBX migration.
Acceptance Criteria
AC1:
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns an article reference in a response,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to the correct canonical article URL on the current OBX domain, not a legacy domain.
AC2: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns an institution reference with role and section context,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to the correct institution profile URL with the correct role and section path for the current brand domain.
AC3: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns a fund reference,

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the rendered link points to 
/database/fund-profile/id/{fund_id}/{slug}/
 on the correct branded domain.
AC4: 
GIVEN
 AskPEI returns a static reference (e.g. 
rankings
),

WHEN
 the frontend resolver processes it,

THEN
 the link resolves to the correct brand-site path, and references outside the approved allowlist are not rendered as links.
AC5: 
GIVEN
 a brand domain changes as part of an OBX cutover,

WHEN
 the resolver mapping is updated for that brand,

THEN
 all AskPEI links for that brand immediately reflect the new domain — with no changes required to AskPEI itself.
AC6: 
GIVEN
 article records are migrated as part of an OBX brand cutover 
WHEN
 a data sales export is generated post-migration 
THEN
 all article canonical URLs in the export reflect the new OBX domain, not legacy brand domains
When we implemented II we hardcoded a lot of URLs in AskPEI however for real estate we will need to shift the URL generation to being controlled by the FE, particularly as URLs will be changing.
I've prepared some documentation for you. I'd appreciate your help with prioritising this work so we can get it in for PERE.
