title: Search Engine Optimization - Marketing Landing Page - Body SSR
description:
Background
SEO validation identified that the Marketing Landing page is not rendering its body content in the initial HTML response.
Currently, the initial HTML mainly contains navigation and footer content, while the page-specific marketing content is delivered through RSC streaming payloads and client-side hydration. As a result, key commercial and indexable copy is not reliably available to search engines before JavaScript execution.
This is causing the Marketing Landing page to fail SEO expectations for server-rendered content. To improve indexability and ensure SEO-critical content is discoverable by search engines, the page body content needs to be rendered directly in the initial HTML response.


SEO Feedback 
- The page may be technically server-routed by Next.js, but the body content is hydrated client-side via RSC streaming. For SEO purposes i.e. what Googlebot reliably indexes that's not server rendering
Scope 
New indexable marketing pages need their body content to be SSR rendered in the initial HTML response to rank.
Business rules
Total page size 606KB. After stripping all script blocks, 42KB remains. 93% of the page is RSC streaming payloads inside self.__next_f.push() calls.
Initial HTML contains 0 h1 elements and 0 h2 elements. The 8 h3 elements are sister-brand nav links (Infrastructure, Private Equity, Private Real Estate, Private Debt, Secondaries).
Value-prop content from the page (4,000+ fund profiles, $2.5trn tracked deals, the FAQ block, "Depending on the package..." subscription detail, etc.) is present in the source but only inside RSC payloads. None of it appears in the static HTML.
What Google receives in the initial response: header nav, brand sitemap, footer. No page-specific content.
Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 - Validate Marketing Landing Page Body Content is Server Rendered in Initial HTML Response
GIVEN 
the Marketing Landing page is loaded and the initial HTML response is inspected before JavaScript execution
WHEN 
the page source is validated for server-rendered content
THEN 
the initial HTML response should contain page-specific marketing content
AND 
the initial HTML should contain valid 
<h1>
 and 
<h2>
 elements
AND 
value-prop content such as “4,000+ fund profiles”, “$2.5trn tracked deals”, FAQ content, and subscription/package details should be present in the static HTML response
AND 
the page content should not be rendered only through RSC streaming payloads inside 
self.__next_f.push()
 calls
AND 
the initial HTML response should not contain only header navigation, brand sitemap links, and footer content
AND 
the page should expose SEO-critical content in the initial HTML response received by Googlebot.

title: OBX PERE - Search results page - layout set up
description:
Background
The PERE OBX platform requires a search results page that allows users to search across content types and view results in a structured, paginated layout. This ticket covers the implementation of the search results page layout as per the provided UX/UI reference screens.
User Story
AS A
 user searching for content on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 to see search results displayed in a clear, organized layout with pagination,

SO THAT
 I can efficiently browse and navigate through relevant content across all content types.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement the PERE OBX search results page layout including the search bar, content type tabs, and pagination.
Out of Scope 
Detailed results display and card design for individual tabs (covered in separate tickets).
Any changes to individual article, fund, firm, or deal pages.
Business Rules:
The search results page must display a search bar at the top with a clear option to reset the query.
Results must be organised across five tabs: News & Analysis, Deal News, Firms, Funds, and Deals, each displaying a result count.
A contextual label must be displayed below the tabs indicating the search term and the active tab (e.g. "Search results for '[query]' in News & Analysis").
Pagination must be displayed at the bottom of the results, showing the current page number, total pages, and previous and next navigation controls.
The search term (if any) entered by the user must be pre-populated in the search bar when the results page renders.
NFRs:
Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
Add data test IDs to the search bar, tabs, and pagination components.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link:  

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Search Bar Displayed
GIVEN
 a user lands on the PERE OBX search results page,

WHEN
 the page renders,

THEN
 a search bar is displayed at the top of the page with the current query pre-populated and a clear option available.
AC2: Content Type Tabs Displayed
GIVEN
 a user lands on the PERE OBX search results page,

WHEN
 the page renders,

THEN
 five tabs are displayed - News & Analysis, Deal News, Firms, Funds, and Deals - each showing a result count.
AC3: Contextual Label Displayed
GIVEN
 a user has performed a search and selected a tab,

WHEN
 results are displayed,

THEN
 a contextual label is shown indicating the search term and the currently active tab.
AC4: Pagination Displayed
GIVEN
 a search query returns more results than fit on a single page,

WHEN
 the user scrolls to the bottom of the results,

THEN
 pagination controls are displayed showing the current page number, total pages, and previous and next navigation options.
AC5: Pagination Navigation Functions Correctly
GIVEN
 a user clicks the next or previous pagination control,

WHEN
 the navigation is triggered,

THEN
 the results update to display the correct page of results.
AC6: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN
 a user views the PERE OBX search results page at >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports,

WHEN
 the page renders,

THEN
 the search bar, tabs, and pagination are correctly displayed across all supported viewports.

title: OBX PERE - Search modal on homepage
description:
Background
The PERE OBX header includes a Search button in the top navigation. When clicked, a search modal must appear overlaying the page, allowing users to enter a search query. This provides a quick and accessible search entry point from anywhere on the site without navigating away from the current page.
User Story
AS A
 user browsing PERE OBX,

I WANT
 a search modal to appear when I click the Search button in the header,

SO THAT
 I can quickly enter a search query from anywhere on the site without leaving the current page.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement the search modal that is triggered when a user clicks the Search button in the PERE OBX header, including the modal overlay, search input, and dismiss behaviour.
Out of Scope 
Search results page layout and functionality (covered in a separate ticket)
Any changes to the Search button styling in the header
Business Rules:
Clicking the Search button in the PERE OBX header must trigger the search modal.
The search modal must overlay the current page with a darkened background, keeping the page content visible but not interactive behind it.
The search modal must display a search input with placeholder text "Search news, funds, firms and deals".
The search modal must include a dismiss (X) button in the top right corner.
Clicking the dismiss button or clicking outside the modal must close the modal and return the user to the current page.
When a user clicks on the search input, a blinking cursor must appear indicating the input is active and ready for text entry.
When a user begins typing in the search input, the placeholder text must disappear.
Submitting a search query must be triggered by the user pressing the Enter or Return key, which must navigate the user to the search results page with the query applied. Users can also navigate to results page without entering any query.
NFRs:
Responsive across >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports.
Add data test IDs to the search modal, search input, and dismiss button components.
UX/UI
Screenshot: 
Screenshot provided as design reference. The modal appears as a dark overlay at the top of the page with a full-width search input and a dismiss button on the right.
Figma Link:  

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Search Modal Opens on Click
GIVEN
 a user clicks the Search button in the PERE OBX header,

WHEN
 the click is registered,

THEN
 the search modal is displayed overlaying the current page with a darkened background.
AC2: Search Input Displayed
GIVEN
 the search modal is open,

WHEN
 the modal renders,

THEN
 a search input bar is displayed with the placeholder text "Search news, funds, firms and deals"
AC3: Modal Dismissed via X Button
GIVEN
 the search modal is open,

WHEN
 a user clicks the dismiss (X) button,

THEN
 the modal closes and the user is returned to the current page.
AC4: Modal Dismissed by Clicking Outside
GIVEN
 the search modal is open,

WHEN
 a user clicks outside the modal,

THEN
 the modal closes and the user is returned to the current page.
AC5: Blinking Cursor on Input Focus
GIVEN
 the search modal is open,

WHEN
 a user clicks on the search input,

THEN
 a blinking cursor appears in the search input indicating it is active and ready for text entry.
AC6: Placeholder Text Disappears on Typing
GIVEN
 a user has clicked on the search input,

WHEN
 the user begins typing,

THEN
 the placeholder text disappears and the user's input is displayed in its place.
AC7: Search Query Submitted via Enter or Return Key
GIVEN
 a user has entered a search query in the modal,

WHEN
 the user presses the Enter or Return key,

THEN
 the user is navigated to the search results page with the query applied.
AC8: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN
 a user views PERE OBX at >=375px, >=1024px, >=1280px, and >=1536px viewports,

WHEN
 the search modal is triggered,

THEN
 the modal is correctly displayed and functional across all supported viewports.
AC9: Empty Search Query Submitted via Enter or Return Key
GIVEN
 a user has not entered a search query in the modal,

WHEN
 the user presses the Enter or Return key,

THEN
 the user is navigated to the search results page with the blank query.

 — all selectors in element file, all assertions in component, zero Playwright API calls in spec
API-backed assertions
 — card data verified against live Fusion API response, not hardcoded expected values
Parallel execution
 — all card-level assertions and all link navigations run in Promise.all; new tabs opened simultaneously
Index-drift safe
 — ticket size validation uses cardsWithTickets mapping to correctly align API indices with UI element indices when some cards have no ticket size
Resource-safe
 — all new-tab operations wrapped in try/finally to guarantee page.close() on failure
No for loops
 — all iteration via Array.from + Promise.all
Aggregated component methods
 — spec tests delegate to single methods (verifyBentoCardDataMatchesApi, verifyInvestorActivityCardDataMatchesApi); no data management in the spec layer

title: Search results page — spaces in search terms produce double-encoded URL (%2520) and corrupt the search input
description:
Steps to reproduce :
Navigate to the search page  
https://stage.infrastructureinvestor.com/search/
 try a multi-word search term (e.g. 
black rock
)
Observe that the search input field displays 
black%20rock
 instead of 
black rock
Notice that the URL gets double-encoded and becomes 
?k=black%2520rock
Expected behavior:
 
The search input should always display the decoded search term (
black rock
), and submitting a search should produce a clean single-encoded URL (
?k=black%20rock
)
Actual Result:

When a user enters a multi-word search term such as 
black rock
, the search input automatically converts the space into encoded special characters and displays 
black%20rock
 instead of the readable text. Upon submitting the search again, the query parameter becomes double-encoded (
?k=black%2520rock
), causing the malformed URL issue to persist on every subsequent search.

title: First 50 Words Preview for Paywalled Articles
description: Background A spike confirmed that surfacing the first 50 words of a paywalled article is technically feasible via client-side truncation using DOMParser . The full article HTML is already delivered to the browser and the Zephr paywall decision is made client-side, so no payload changes are required. This story covers the front-end implementation. Pre-Requisite NA USER STORY As a reader who lands on a paywalled article, I want to read the first 50 words of the article body before the paywall gate, so that I can evaluate whether the content is relevant before being asked to subscribe. Scope Client-side truncation of article body content to the first 50 words using DOMParser Truncated content renders as plain text only-no images, rich media, or embeds Truncated preview displays above the existing paywall gate component Applies to paywalled articles only Business Rules IF the article is paywalled AND THEN the first 50 words of the article body should be displayed above the paywall gate, regardless of referral source (search, newsletter, direct, social, etc.) IF the article body contains fewer than 50 words, THEN the full body text is displayed IF the first 50 words contain images, embeds, rich media, infograms, podcasts, or block quotes, THEN those elements are stripped and not counted toward the word limit, only plain text is rendered. WE CURRENTLY RENDER FEATURED IMAGES should we continue to do that? If a hyperlink falls at the 50-word cut-off point, the preview extends to the end of the full linked phrase rather than cutting mid-link. eg: Word 50 lands on "latest" but " latest acquisition " is a single hyperlink. Rather than cutting after "latest" and leaving a broken link, the preview extends to include "acquisition" so the full link is intact. …the fund announced its latest acquisition … HTML tags (, ) are not counted as words , only the visible text counts toward the 50. IF the truncation point falls mid-sentence, THEN an ellipsis is appended at the end of the preview eg: "Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetuer adipiscing elit. Donec odio. Quisque volutpat mattis eros. Nullam malesuada erat ut turpis. Suspendisse urna nibh, viverra non, semper suscipit, posuere a, pede. Lorem..." Out of Scope Define what is included/ and not included in the scope for the User story SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION Figma: RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks Assumption: Surfacing the first 50 words of a paywalled article is technically feasible via client-side truncation using DOMParser. Risk: The full article HTML remains in the browser and is accessible via dev tools or view source. This is an accepted risk of the client-side approach Googlebot is handled separately and already fixed , it will always receive the full article. This is independent of the user-facing paywall behaviour.. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality AC1: Preview displays above paywall gate GIVEN a paywalled article is displayed with the paywall gate WHEN the page loads THEN the first 50 words of visible article body text are displayed as plain text above the paywall gate AC2: Non-text elements are excluded GIVEN the article body contains any non-text elements (images, embeds, rich media, infograms, podcasts, block quotes) WHEN the preview is rendered THEN those elements are excluded from the preview and do not contribute to the word count AC3: Preview does not cut mid-sentence or mid-link GIVEN a paywalled article is displayed with the paywall gate WHEN the preview is rendered THEN the preview cuts off at 50 words with an ellipsis appended, unless the 50th word falls mid-link, in which case the preview extends to the end of the closing anchor tag before truncating AC4: Short articles display in full GIVEN the article body contains fewer than 50 words WHEN the preview is rendered THEN the full body text is displayed with no ellipsis

title: AskPEI - UI - Update Starter Questions - Phillip Feedback: update starter questions and categories
description:
BACKGROUND
The AskPEI / Phillip copilot landing screen shows starter question categories to help users begin a query. The current 4 categories (News & Analysis, Rankings, Institutions, Funds) no longer reflect reader workflows. Editorial and R&A have provided 3 replacement categories (Market trends, Fundraising, LPs) with updated question strings.
The change is a text-only data swap in a single static TypeScript file (`apps/app-portfolio/components/copilot/copilot-suggestions.ts`). All downstream components consume the exports unchanged.
USER STORY
AS A
 Phillip / AskPEI user landing on the copilot

I WANT
 starter questions grouped under categories that match how I actually research the market

SO THAT
 I can pick a useful prompt without having to invent one from scratch
SCOPE
In Scope
Replacing category data in apps/app-portfolio/components/copilot/copilot-suggestions.ts
Unit tests covering all 4 acceptance criteria
MR raised against main with clean CI
Out of Scope
Template responder in the copilot Python repo
Changes to any other component, nav config, or analytics wiring
E2E / Playwright tests for the copilot UI
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
[AC-1] Only new categories shown
GIVEN
 a user opens AskPEI
WHEN
 the page renders
THEN
 the only categories shown are Market trends, Fundraising and LPs, in that order
[AC-2] Retired categories absent
GIVEN
 a user opens AskPEI
WHEN
 the user looks at the starter questions
THEN
 none of the retired categories (News and Analysis, Rankings, Institutions, Funds) or their questions appear anywhere on the screen
[AC-3] Correct questions per category
GIVEN
 a user clicks one of the starter question category buttons
WHEN
 the question list renders
THEN
 the questions shown match exactly those specified for that category (3 for Market trends, 4 for Fundraising, 3 for LPs)
[AC-4] Questions invoke copilot with exact text
GIVEN
 a user clicks any starter question button
WHEN
 the click is handled
THEN
 the copilot is invoked with that question's exact text as the prompt
[AC-5] Lint passes
GIVEN
 the updated code is in place
WHEN
 npm run lint is executed for app-portfolio
THEN
 it exits 0 with no errors
[AC-6] All tests pass
GIVEN
 the spec file covers all ACs
WHEN
 npm run test is executed for app-portfolio
THEN
 all 9 tests pass and coverage is reported
[AC-7] MR raised with no conflicts
GIVEN
 the branch is pushed from the poc clone at HEAD of main
WHEN
 a merge request is raised against main
THEN
 it shows no conflicts and references

title: PERE OBX > Investigate and document data flow through II/PERE components in app-portfolio
description:
Goal
Investigate and document how data flows through II/PERE components in the 
app-portfolio
 application to build a shared understanding before any API integration work begins.
Background
The 
app-portfolio
 app supports multiple brands including 
pereportfolio
 and 
infraportfolio
. The PERE components rely on shared packages (
packages/shared-portfolio
) and external data sources. The current data flow — from config, API calls, and state management down to rendered components — needs to be investigated and documented so that it provides a clear path during API integration with components.
Scope of Investigation
How brand-specific configuration (PERE vs II) is loaded and consumed in 
app-portfolio
How 
packages/shared-portfolio
 is used by PERE components and how it interacts with brand-specific code
How API calls are initiated, where endpoints are configured, and how request/response handling is structured
How state management is implemented across shared and brand-specific layers
How data flows from config → API → state → component rendering
Any external data sources used by PERE components and how they are accessed
Differences (if any) between how II and PERE components consume data
Expected Outcomes
By the end of this spike, the team should have:
A documented data-flow diagram
 showing how data moves from config → API call → state management → component rendering for both II and PERE components in 
app-portfolio
A list of shared vs brand-specific touchpoints
 — clearly identifying what lives in 
packages/shared-portfolio
 vs what is brand-specific
An inventory of external data sources
 consumed by PERE components, including endpoints (where known), authentication mechanism (if any), and which components consume them
A list of identified risks, gaps, or unknowns
 that could affect future API integration work

title: OBX Homepage States - Access and Package Changes - Render Homepage State on JWT Token refresh
description:
Background
OBX II offers multiple subscription packages with different levels of access and permissions. The homepage should always reflect the correct state based on the user’s current package and access level.
User Story
As a
 Product Manager
I want
 the OBX homepage to reflect the correct state when a user’s package changes,
So that
 users see content and access aligned with their current subscription.
Scope
To render the correct OBX homepage state for users when their package changes, based on the latest JWT token.
Out of scope
Homepage state for Registered (Paid) users on Base Trial and Unlimited Trial packages will be handled separately
Business Rules
The OBX Homepage must use the JWT token to determine what content and access to show the user.
When the JWT token refreshes (every ~15 minutes), the homepage must update to reflect the user's current package, update permissions accordingly, and display the appropriate homepage state:
Anonymous / Lite 
Base / Unlimited
IF a user upgrades their package AND the JWT token is refreshed, THEN paywalls/restrictions should be updated and the homepage displays the Base/Unlimited state (as applicable).
IF a user downgrades their package AND the JWT token is refreshed, THEN paywalls/restrictions should be updated and the homepage displays the Anonymous/Lite state (as applicable).
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Documentation - Miro

title: [Stopgap] SEO Audit -  Serve full article body to verified search crawlers (Googlebot/Bingbot) for GA
description:
Background
For GA, we need search crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot) to be able to index the full body of paywalled articles, while end users continue to see the paywalled experience. This ticket captures a short-term stopgap approach until the proper SSR fix lands post-GA.
This is the outcome of a discussion between Gaurabh and Richard.
Proposed approach (stopgap)
Add a duplicate article page on a hidden route that renders the full article body with no paywall logic.
Use the existing CloudFront / Lambda@Edge function (already used for the home page variants) to route verified search crawlers to that hidden route.
End users continue to hit the standard article route with paywall logic unchanged.
Estimated effort (app code only): ~2 days, since we already have a similar mechanism in place for the home page variants. DevOps work is on top of this — see below.
Same content is served — we are simply choosing whether to render the paywall version or the full page based on the requester. The page-level meta tags / schema (e.g. 
isAccessibleForFree
) are a separate question to be addressed later.
Teams involved
App / Frontend (ST11 dev)
 — hidden route, page rendering without paywall, sitemap/robots/internal-link hygiene, feature flag.
DevOps / Platform
 — Lambda@Edge code review + deployment, CloudFront behavior + cache key changes, IP-range refresh job, IAM, WAF, CloudWatch alarms, staging parity.
SEO / DMO (Kevin Burgum)
 — sign-off on the cloaking risk, Search Console monitoring after rollout.
QA
 — test plan covering verified-bot, spoofed-UA, and normal-user paths in staging.
Scope — App / Frontend work
New hidden article route (e.g. 
/_crawler/article/{slug}
 — final path TBD with DevOps) that renders the full article body with no paywall, no subscribe CTA, no Zephr/paywall scripts.
Confirm the hidden route is 
not
 present in 
sitemap.xml
, breadcrumbs, internal links, or 
<link rel="canonical">
. Canonical on the hidden route should point back to the public article URL.
robots.txt
 should NOT advertise the hidden route. Bots reach it only via the edge rewrite.
Feature flag to enable/disable the routing behavior without a redeploy (so we can roll back fast if Search Console flags issues).
Logging/metric hook so we can count how many requests get rewritten and confirm origin response is the no-paywall variant.
Scope — DevOps / Infrastructure work
CloudFront & Lambda@Edge
Update the existing Lambda@Edge function (the same one used for home page variants) to add the article-route rewrite for verified crawlers. Confirm whether this lives at viewer-request or origin-request:
viewer-request
: 128 MB / 5 s limit, no env vars, no VPC — fine for a CIDR lookup against a baked-in or S3-fetched list.
origin-request
: 10 GB / 30 s limit, env vars allowed — needed if we plan to call out to DNS or DynamoDB.
Recommend 
origin-request
 so the rewrite output is part of the cache key and we get caching benefit without leaking content.
Lambda@Edge must be deployed in 
us-east-1
 and replicated to all edge locations — propagation typically takes 5–10 min.
IAM: execution role for the edge function needs 
lambda.amazonaws.com
 + 
edgelambda.amazonaws.com
 trust, plus 
s3:GetObject
 on the IP-ranges bucket if we go with that approach.
CloudFront distribution: add the new behavior / update the existing one to invoke the edge function on the article path pattern.
Cache key must separate bot-routed responses from user responses
 so the no-paywall HTML is never served to a normal user from cache. Two clean options:
Rewrite the URI path to a different origin path on the bot branch (so the cache key naturally differs), or
Add a custom header (e.g. 
x-crawler-verified: 1
) and include it in the CloudFront cache key policy.
Set sensible TTLs on the bot-routed response — long enough to be efficient, short enough to recover quickly if we have to disable.
Crawler verification
Do NOT trust the User-Agent alone.
 Verify by either:
Reverse-DNS lookup
 on the client IP (resolve PTR → forward-confirm A/AAAA), per Google's official guidance, OR
CIDR match
 against the published IP-range JSONs (preferred for Lambda@Edge: zero external calls in the hot path).
Source files:
Google: https://developers.google.com/static/crawling/ipranges/common-crawlers.json (also 
googlebot.json
, 
special-crawlers.json
)
Bing: https://www.bing.com/toolbox/bingbot.json
Refresh mechanism: scheduled Lambda (EventBridge cron, daily) that fetches both JSON files, validates them, and writes to an S3 bucket. The edge function loads from S3 at cold start and caches in memory for the life of the container.
Alarm if the refresh job fails 2 days in a row.
Alarm if the loaded list size drops by >X% vs previous (sanity check against a bad upstream file).
If we go with reverse-DNS instead/as well, note: Lambda@Edge DNS adds latency on every uncached request, and there's no VPC, so we rely on the public resolver. CIDR match is materially simpler.
WAF & abuse protection
Confirm the existing AWS WAF web ACL on the CloudFront distribution still applies to the rewritten path.
Add a rate-limit rule on the bot route keyed by source IP, even though the IP is supposedly verified — defense in depth.
Monitoring & alerting (CloudWatch)
Lambda@Edge: invocation count, error rate, throttles, duration p95. Alarm on error rate > threshold (failures here can break the whole CDN behavior).
Custom metrics published from the edge function: 
verified_bot_hits
, 
unverified_bot_ua_attempts
 (UA looked like a bot but IP did not verify), 
ip_list_load_failure
.
Refresh-job Lambda: success/failure metric + alarm.
Dashboard summarising the above for the first 2 weeks post-launch.
Staging / non-prod parity
Mirror the Lambda@Edge + CloudFront behavior in staging so QA can reproduce the routing.
Provide a way to test from a non-Googlebot IP (e.g. a staging-only allowlist or header-based override gated by a shared secret) — this override MUST be disabled in production.
Rollout & rollback
Phased rollout behind the feature flag: enable for a small % first, watch metrics + Search Console, then ramp.
Rollback path: flip the feature flag → edge function falls through to today's behavior. Document the runbook.
Considerations / risks
Google's guidance is that paywalled content should use the 
isAccessibleForFree
 schema markup; serving different content to bots vs users can be considered cloaking.
Many sites do crawler-based routing without issue, but it's not guaranteed — we need to keep a close watch on indexing and Search Console signals after rollout.
Crawlers MUST be verified by reverse-DNS on the IP and/or matching against the published Googlebot/Bingbot IP ranges, NOT just the User-Agent string. Otherwise anyone spoofing the UA gets free articles.
This is explicitly a stopgap — must be replaced by the proper SSR fix before PERE GA. All stakeholders need to be aware of that.
References
Google verified bot IP ranges: https://developers.google.com/static/crawling/ipranges/common-crawlers.json
Google bot verification guidance: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/verifying-googlebot
Bingbot verification: https://www.bing.com/toolbox/bingbot.json
Bingbot verification guidance: https://www.bing.com/webmasters/help/how-to-verify-bingbot-3905dc26
Zuora docs (precedent / approach reference): https://docs.zuora.com — Configure HTML features in the developer interface
Acceptance criteria
A hidden article route exists that renders the full article body without paywall logic.
CloudFront / Lambda@Edge routes only verified Googlebot and Bingbot requests to that route.
Crawler verification uses CIDR match on Google/Bing's published IP ranges and/or reverse-DNS — UA alone is NOT sufficient.
The IP-range list is refreshed daily from upstream and the refresh has its own monitoring/alerting.
CloudFront cache key cleanly separates bot-routed responses from user-facing responses (no risk of a cached no-paywall page being served to a normal user).
Spoofed UA requests (a normal browser claiming to be Googlebot) do NOT bypass the paywall. Verified by automated test in staging.
Standard end-user requests continue to hit the existing paywalled article route, behavior unchanged.
Feature flag exists to disable the rewrite without a redeploy; rollback procedure documented.
CloudWatch alarms exist for: Lambda@Edge errors, IP-range refresh failure, anomalously low/high verified-bot traffic.
Hidden route is excluded from sitemap.xml and internal links; canonical points back to the public URL.
Indexing and Search Console are monitored for a defined period (≥2 weeks) after rollout for any cloaking signals.
Notes / follow-ups
This is a stopgap arrangement and it will be replaced by the proper SSR fix before PERE GA.
Page-level meta tags / 
isAccessibleForFree
 schema to be revisited as part of the proper SSR fix.
Consider creating subtasks for: (1) App route + rendering, (2) Lambda@Edge code change, (3) IP-range refresh job + S3 bucket, (4) CloudFront cache-key + WAF config, (5) CloudWatch alarms + dashboard, (6) Staging parity + test plan.

title: OBX PERE - API baseline configuration
description:
Background 
The PERE OBX domain has been set up as a bare-bones skeleton [

 ]. To ensure further integrations can be connected efficiently and consistently, a baseline configuration must be established for each required service.
This ticket covers the API baseline configuration for PERE OBX so that data and service integrations can be built on top of it in subsequent tickets.
User Story 
AS A
 developer building integrations on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 an API baseline configuration to be in place,

SO THAT
 data and services can be connected to PERE OBX consistently and without friction in future tickets.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to establish an API baseline configuration for PERE OBX. Full API integration and functional validation are out of scope and will be addressed in subsequent tickets.
Out of Scope
Full API integration or functional data testing on PERE OBX.
Any changes to the existing API configuration on II OBX.
Any PERE OBX features or components beyond API baseline configuration.
Business Rules
An API baseline configuration must be established for PERE OBX, consistent with the II OBX configuration where applicable.
The configuration must be structured to make further API integrations on PERE OBX straightforward to implement.
NFRs
No degradation to existing API behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION

Miro:
Confluence:

UI: Not applicable.

GIVEN
 the API baseline configuration has been completed,

WHEN
 the configuration is reviewed,

THEN
 all required APIs are configured for PERE OBX at a baseline level and are ready for further integration work.
AC2: No Regression on II OBX
GIVEN
 the API baseline configuration has been completed for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the II OBX platform is accessed,

THEN
 the existing API configuration on II OBX is unaffected.

title: Ask PEI - Request a demo - call web activity
description:
Background
The Ask PEI paywall will include a Request a Demo CTA alongside the existing Find Out More CTA. When a user clicks Request a Demo, a web activity must be fired to the CRM and the user must be shown a confirmation page directly — without a form — since the user is already logged in and their details are known. The CRM web activity itself will be implemented by the CRM team; this ticket covers the frontend integration of calling that activity and displaying the confirmation page upon success.
User Story
AS A
 logged-in user viewing the Ask PEI paywall,

I WANT
 to be able to request a demo with a single click and see a confirmation immediately,

SO THAT
 I can express my interest in upgrading without needing to fill in a form.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to add a Request a Demo CTA to the Ask PEI paywall, call the CRM web activity when the CTA is clicked, and display a confirmation page to the user upon success.
Out of Scope
Implementation of the CRM web activity itself (owned by the CRM team)
Any form-based data collection from the user
Any changes to the paywall beyond the addition of the Request a Demo CTA
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality or logic
Business Rules
A Request a Demo CTA must be visible on the Ask PEI paywall.
IF
 a registered user clicks on the ‘Request a Demo’ CTA from the prompt counter or paywall 
THEN
 a web activity must be triggered in the CRM using the defined endpoint confirmed by the CRM team 
AND
 upon a successful CRM web activity call, the user must be shown the confirmation page directly 
AND
 the user must not be presented with a form.
The confirmation page must be displayed without requiring the user to input any details, as the user is already logged in.
The rule to be used as configured by Jason and CRM team is - 
https://console.zephr.com/products/features/sdk/obx-ask-pei-demo-request/
. (this is on stage but not yet configured) 
NFRs:
Responsive across the 375, 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
The CRM web activity call must not block or delay the rendering of the confirmation page beyond what is necessary.
Add data test ID to the Request a Demo CTA and confirmation page components.
UX/UI
Figma Link: 

 
Connect your Figma account
UI:
DO NOT 
display the ready to start section
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
The CRM web activity endpoint and the expected request payload must be confirmed with the CRM team before development begins. This is a blocker for implementation.
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1: Request a Demo CTA Visible on Paywall
GIVEN 
a logged-in user has reached their usage limit and the paywall is displayed,

WHEN 
the user views the paywall,

THEN 
a Request a Demo CTA is visible on the paywall.
AC2: CRM Web Activity Called on Click
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA on the paywall,

WHEN 
the click is registered,

THEN 
a web activity is called to the CRM using the confirmed endpoint.
AC3: Confirmation Page Displayed on Success
GIVEN 
the CRM web activity call has completed successfully,

WHEN 
the response is received,

THEN 
the user is shown a confirmation page directly without being presented with a form.
AC4: No Form Presented to User
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA,

WHEN 
the CRM web activity is called,

THEN 
no form or data input is presented to the user at any point in the flow.
AC5: Error Handling
GIVEN 
a logged-in user clicks the Request a Demo CTA,

WHEN 
the CRM web activity call fails,

THEN 
an appropriate error state or fallback message is displayed to the user and the page does not break.
AC6: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user views the Ask PEI paywall and confirmation page at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN 
the user interacts with the Request a Demo CTA,

THEN
 the CTA, web activity call, and confirmation page function correctly and display consistently across all supported breakpoints.

title: OBX PERE - Integrate Shared SSO
description:
Background
The PERE OBX platform has been established as a bare-bones skeleton [

 ]. As other teams begin building out the homepage and components, SSO must be integrated to enable authenticated access. This ticket captures the high-level requirement for SSO implementation on PERE OBX, mirroring the approach used on II OBX. Individual technical subtasks will be added by the dev team.
The domain being used is - 
pereportfolio-fusion.dev.pei-services.com
User Story
AS A
 user accessing the PERE OBX platform,

I WANT
 to be authenticated via SSO,

SO THAT
 I have a seamless and consistent login experience in line with other PEI OBX platforms.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement SSO on the PERE OBX platform, mirroring the existing SSO implementation on II OBX. Individual technical tasks required to complete this work will be defined and added by the dev team as subtasks.
Out of Scope
Any changes to the existing SSO implementation on II OBX
Business Rules
The SSO implementation on PERE OBX must mirror the existing II OBX SSO implementation.
Authenticated users must be able to sign in and out of PERE OBX via SSO.
The implementation must not affect or regress the existing SSO behaviour on II OBX.
NFRs:
SSO behaviour on PERE OBX must be consistent with the II OBX implementation.
No degradation to existing authentication behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
UX/UI
Figma Link:
UI:
No design changes expected. Authentication behaviour must match II OBX.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
The II OBX SSO implementation will serve as the reference for this work.
Individual technical subtasks will be defined and added by the dev team to break down the implementation.
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1: SSO Functional on PERE OBX
GIVEN
 a user attempts to log in to PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the authentication flow is triggered,

THEN
 the user is authenticated via SSO in a manner consistent with the II OBX implementation.
AC2: Successful Redirection Post-Authentication
GIVEN
 a user has successfully authenticated via SSO on PERE OBX,

WHEN
 authentication is complete,

THEN
 the user is redirected to the correct destination consistent with II OBX behaviour.
AC3: Failed Authentication Handled Correctly
GIVEN
 a user fails to authenticate via SSO on PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the authentication failure occurs,

THEN
 the user is redirected to the appropriate fallback consistent with II OBX behaviour.
AC4: No Regression on II OBX
GIVEN

WHEN

THEN

title: OBX PERE - Open search configuration
description:
Background
The PERE OBX platform is being built out and OpenSearch must be configured to enable data to be fetched across the platform. This involves adding the necessary connection configurations and ensuring all required indexes are in place and ready to serve data to PERE OBX.
User Story
AS A
 developer building on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 OpenSearch to be fully configured with the correct connection settings and indexes,

SO THAT
 data can be fetched reliably across the PERE OBX platform.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to add the OpenSearch connection configuration for PERE OBX and ensure all required indexes are in place and ready to fetch data from.
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the existing OpenSearch configuration on II OBX.
Any feature-level data integration beyond confirming indexes are ready.
Business Rules:
The necessary configuration to connect PERE OBX to OpenSearch must be added.
All required indexes must be created and confirmed as ready to fetch data from before this ticket is closed.
The following indexes are required and should be validated are available in Opensearch:
pei-ra-institutions
pei-ra-funds
Pei-ra-transactions

NFRs:
No degradation to existing OpenSearch behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Not applicable.
Figma Link:  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: OpenSearch Connection Configured
GIVEN
 the configuration has been completed,

WHEN
 the PERE OBX platform attempts to connect to OpenSearch,

THEN
 the connection is established successfully using the configured credentials and settings.
AC2: All Required Indexes in Place
GIVEN
 the configuration has been completed,

WHEN
 the indexes are reviewed,

THEN
 all required indexes for PERE OBX are present and ready to fetch data from.
AC3: Data Fetchable from Indexes
GIVEN
 all indexes are in place,

WHEN
 a data fetch is triggered from PERE OBX,

THEN
 data is returned correctly from the relevant OpenSearch indexes.
AC4: Credentials Stored Securely
GIVEN
 the configuration has been completed,

WHEN
 the connection configuration is reviewed,

THEN
 OpenSearch credentials and connection details are stored securely and are not hardcoded in the codebase.
AC5: No Regression on II OBX
GIVEN
 the OpenSearch configuration has been completed for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the II OBX platform is accessed,

THEN
 the existing II OBX OpenSearch configuration and data fetching behaviour are unaffected.

title: Homepage UI Upgrade - Data Ribbon Growth Metric - Include Today's  Additions & Updates (excluding network member)
description:
Background 
As part of OBX V1, we released the first iteration of the new homepage internally. We now plan to enhance it by adding growth metrics to the data ribbon.
User Story
 
As a
 PEI user, 
I want
 to see the growth metric in the data ribbon, 
So that
 I can track the increase in PEI database coverage.
Scope 
To include today’s additions and updates in the growth metric calculation for the last 30 days in the data ribbon on the OBX homepage.
Out of Scope
Creating APIs to retrieve the count. This will be handled in WEB- 25937 
Creating an API for new networks members added. This will be handled in a separate ticket by the network team.
Mobile responsiveness of the text above the data ribbon component on the right-hand side. This will be handled in a separate ticket
Business Rules:
 The growth metric should be calculated for the 
last 30 days, including today
.
IF a new deal has been added in the last 30 days 
including today
 , THEN display count in the growth metric on the relevant card using the uploaded date in the deals database. 
Example: 
Deals 11, 230 x added 
The asset class associated to the deals should be Infrastructure. The deal status must be tagged as completed. Do not deduct any deleted deals
IF a new commitment or fund has been added in the last 30 days 
including today,
 THEN display count in the growth metric on the relevant card using the published date in R&A database. 
Example: 
Funds 1, 230 x added
Example: 
Commitments 3,487 x  added 
The asset class associated to the fund and commitment should be Infrastructure. 
IF a date has been entered for an Investors, Fund Managers, Law Firm, Placement Agent, Investment Consultant in last 30 days 
including today
 using the new OBX data ribbon profile update date field in R&A database, THEN display the count in the growth metric on the relevant card. 
Example: 
Investors 4,687 x updated
Example: 
Managers 6,797 x updated
Example: 
Service Providers 12,797 x updated
The growth metric for the service provider card should always be the sum of the Law Firm, Placement Agent and Investment Consultant profile updates as a whole. 
No other changes should be made to the existing implementation, except including today in the calculation logic.
UI/UX
OBX Infrastructure Investor Site > Data Ribbon > Growth Metric
Figma Link > 
https://www.figma.com/design/dlydKp27DTaWEpiYTJAOiU/Homepage-%E2%80%93-Logged-In-%E2%80%93UI?node-id=16391-11332&t=YFHb3oRo52UvtzcD-1
  
Assumptions / Open Questions / Dependencies / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Include Today in Growth Metrics
GIVEN
 I am a user on the OBX Homepage
WHEN
 I view the data ribbon growth metrics
THEN
 the growth metrics for Funds, Commitments, Deals, Investors, Managers, and Service Providers should be calculated for the last 30 days, 
including today’s additions and updates
AND
 no other changes should be made to the existing data ribbon implementation.

title: Paywall bypass – Full post content visible in DOM for anonymous users
description:
h2. Problem

Content is viewable in the DOM by anonymous users. Content should 
NOT
 be in the DOM for these users — hiding it in the front end is not sufficient.
h2. Affected URL (STAGE)

https://stage.infrastructureinvestor.com/the-enduring-legacy-of-sw/
h2. Evidence
In the DOM, the full content of the post is visible to anonymous users.
Screenshot 1 is from a platinum user.
Screenshot 2 (page source) is from an anonymous user.
The body content visible in screenshot 1 is present in the DOM in screenshot 2.
h2. Steps to reproduce
Open the STAGE URL in an incognito/private window (not logged in).
View page source / inspect the DOM.
Search for the article body/content.
h2. Expected result

For anonymous users, restricted article body content is 
not present
 in the delivered HTML/DOM (server response), and cannot be retrieved without authentication/entitlement.
h2. Actual result

For anonymous users, the full article body appears in the HTML/DOM/page source.
h2. Environment
Stage: 
https://stage.infrastructureinvestor.com/the-enduring-legacy-of-sw/
Prod: Also reproducible in PROD on the app (per report).
h2. Impact

Serious issue if content is accessible despite the paywall.
h2. Question for investigation

Is this just a STAGE issue that will be resolved in PROD, or is this a broader problem affecting PROD as well?
h2. Security/Severity note

Potential paywall bypass / content exposure risk. Please treat as 
high severity
 and 
security-sensitive
 until confirmed otherwise.

title: Request a Demo - (Registered Users Only) - Integrate with Zephr
description:
Background
For registered users that hit a paywall and click a “Request a Demo” CTA, we want to make this process as seamless as possible. Given these users are already logged in we simply need to trigger the downstream process to send the demo request into the Sales teams' queue.
As a
 Registered user, 

I want 
to request a demo

So that
 the sales team can call me to show me through the product
Scope 
To call the Zephr feature to initiate the downstream processes for a demo request
Out of Scope
The following features will be handled in separate tickets:
Setting the context of which CTA was clicked (network, marketing, data, content)
Business Rules & Web Hooks
The demo request should be triggered by calling the appropriate Zephr SDK feature rule.
The rule will run in the “Registered” Zephr canvas and will call the 
https://console.zephr.com/products/features/sdk/demo-request
 SDK feature rule.
No details are needed to be passed to Zephr when calling the SDK feature rule
The Webhook sent from Zephr will be the 
PEIPI - CRM - Create Web Activity - Content Demo Request
For a Logged In user
User should be navigated to the submission confirmation page 
https://www.infrastructureinvestor.com/request
-demo/confirmation
 on the same tab after the user clicks the 
“Request your demo”
 button AND a successful response is received back
The confirmation page should display the following for a registered user.
Page heading
 “Request received”
Page description 
“A member of our team will be in touch shortly to schedule your consultation”
Body Text 
“What to expect?”

A specialist will demonstrate the specific tools and insights relevant to your strategy.
Expertly curated updates to keep you ahead of the market

Rapid data-backed decision-making powered by our AI Co-pilot, ‘Ask PEI’
Direct access to investors at events and online
Complete visibility of investor strategy and competitor performance
REQUEST DEMO -Confirmation Page for a logged in user: 

 
DO not display the ready to start section
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 
 - Successfully Submit Demo Request
GIVEN
 that I am a signed in “Registered” ONLY user in Zephr (should have a session but no II products)

WHEN
 I click on any CTA title “demo request” 

THEN
 Zephr should send a Web Activity for a Content Demo request to CRM

AND
 I should see some confirmation message that informing me of the next steps PEI will take to deliver the demo
Scenario 2 
 - Anon user
GIVEN
 that I am NOT a signed in user

WHEN
 I land on the path to request a demo

THEN
 I should see the demo request register form
Scenario 3 
 - Subscriber users
GIVEN
 that I am a subscriber user (any II product)

WHEN
 I land on the path to request a demo

THEN
 I should NOT see any demo information and the request for a demo should NOT be triggered
AC4:"Request a demo" CTA – Logged In user
 GIVEN a logged in user lands on the confirmation page THEN the page displays:
Heading: "Request received"
Description: "A member of our team will be in touch shortly to schedule your consultation"
Body heading: "What to expect?"
Body content:
A specialist will demonstrate the specific tools and insights relevant to your strategy
Expertly curated updates to keep you ahead of the market

title: Registration & Request a Demo - First & Last Name Fields - Restrict Input to Only Special Characters Allowed by Zephr
description:
Background
As part of the OBX initiative, we are enhancing the OBX Infrastructure portfolio site by implementing a complete end-to-end registration and Request a Demo flow. This will enable prospects to submit their details and create a free account as part of the process.
User Story
As a
 Product Manager, 
I want 
the First Name and Last Name fields to accept only characters supported by Zephr,
So that 
user details pass validation on form submission and free account can be created successfully.
Scope 
To restrict input in the First Name and Last Name fields on Registration and Request a Demo forms to only characters supported by Zephr.
Business Rules: 
Ensure that IF the user enters a value into the First Name or Last Name fields on the registration or demo form, THEN only characters supported by Zephr are allowed as per the regex: 
^[a-zA-Z\u0080-\uFFFF\-'., ]{1,50}$
Ensure that IF the user attempts to enter a character outside the Zephr-supported set in the First Name or Last Name fields, THEN the character is blocked and not accepted in the fields.
Ensure no changes are made to other field validation messages at both form level and field level on form submission.
Breakdown of characters supported by Zephr
^
 / 
$
 — full string match, start to end
{1,50}
 — 1 to 50 characters long.
Character class allows:
a-zA-Z
 — standard ASCII letters
\u0080-\uFFFF
 — the entire extended Unicode range from the start of Latin Extended characters all the way up to U+FFFF, which covers:
Accented/diacritic Latin letters (é, ñ, ü, ø, etc.)
Greek, Cyrillic, Hebrew, Arabic, and many other scripts
Curly quotes, em dashes, and a large swathe of typographic symbols
\-
 — hyphen
'
 — straight apostrophe
`
 — backtick
.
 — literal dot
,
 — comma
— space
UX/UI
Registration Form > First & Last name field
Request a demo Form > First & Last name field
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 - Valid Character Input 
GIVEN
 I am a user on the registration or demo form
WHEN
 I enter a value into the First Name or Last Name fields
THEN
 I should be allowed to input characters that match the Zephr regex 
^[a-zA-Z\u0080-\uFFFF\-'., ]{1,50}$

Scenario 2 - Invalid Character Restriction 
GIVEN
 I am a user entering a value into the First Name or Last Name fields
WHEN
 I attempt to input a character that is not supported by Zephr
THEN
 that character is blocked and not accepted in the field

title: SEO Audit - To Implement SSR for Article Sidebar and links
description:
Background
As per the SEO Audit on stage before go-live, identified critical issues affecting the Google crawling
Issue: Structured data not in server-rendered HTML. 
The Next.js article template defers all content to client-side hydration. Article body, sidebar modules, and internal article-to-article links exist only in RSC streaming payloads (self.__next_f.push()). Google must execute JS to see any article content. 
Confirmed on live OBX site 14 Apr 2026. 
R
isk/Impact
  Google's initial crawl pass sees an empty page: no article text, no internal links, no sidebar content. Google's renderer can execute JS, but this adds dependency on a rendering queue that operates on a delay with no coverage guarantee. For a site where articles are the primary organic traffic driver, this puts the entire content corpus at risk. 
Pre-Requisite
 
USER STORY 
As a Google  viewing the article page

I want to be able to see the article content on initial crawl

So that Google's need not execute JS to see any artcile content to optimize indexing
Scope
Business Rules
Out of Scope
None
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
None
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1:

title: SEO Audit - JSON-LD SSR from Article Body to  Header to enable schema visibility without a JS execution
description:
Background
As per the SEO Audit on stage before go-live, identified critical issues affecting the Google crawling
Structured data not in server-rendered HTML. 
JSON-LD only renders after client-side hydration. Same RSC-only pattern as the article body. The schema should be delivered in the SSR payload. 
Given that the article body and sidebar are moving to SSR, including JSON-LD in the same work should not add meaningful complexity. 
R
isk/Impact
 RSC-only delivery means Google's initial crawl sees no structured data at all. Same rendering risk as the article body: dependent on Google's rendering queue with no coverage guarantee. Loss of rich result eligibility (article carousels, Top Stories) typically results in a 20–40% lower CTR from existing rankings. 
Pre-Requisite
 JSON-LD configured but in the Article body
USER STORY 
As a crawler viewing the article page

I want to be able to see the article structure server-rendered

So that Google's initial crawl sees the structured data
Scope
To ensure JSON-LD is available for Google’s initial crawl
Business Rules
JSON-LD should be present in the header and not the article body 

Includes:

Article schema
Paywall schema (
isAccessibleForFree
, 
hasPart
, 
cssSelector
)
Out of Scope
None
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
None
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1: JSON-LD seen in header
Given I am on the article page
WHEN I inspect the page
THEN JSON-LD should be present in the article header 
AND this will ensure google \ sees the schema on initial crawl and will not need a JS execution to render the schema

title: Ask PEI - Simplified UI for Paywall - GA
description:
Background
Ask PEI usage limits are enforced via Zephr based on a user's membership tier. For GA, the full tiered paywall UI designs are not feasible to implement. A single simplified paywall must be displayed to all users when they reach their limit, regardless of tier. The full tiered designs and differentiated messaging are deferred to post-GA.
User Story
AS A
 user who has reached my Ask PEI usage limit,

I WANT
 to see a clear message indicating I have no prompts remaining,

SO THAT
 I understand I cannot submit further queries and am aware of my options.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement a single simplified paywall displayed to all users when their Ask PEI usage limit is reached, showing "0 prompts remaining." and have a simple CTA - Find out more that navigates the user to the memberships page so that the users can still proceed.
Out of Scope
Tiered paywall UI designs per membership type (post-GA)
Prompt count indicator / countdown messaging (post-GA)
Request Demo CTA wiring (post-GA)
Differentiated paywall messaging per tier (post-GA)
Prerequisites
Zephr rules configured for user memberships (

 )
Zephyr feature slug is connected to Ask PEI (see Zephyr hook-up ticket [

 ])
Simplified paywall design is confirmed/agreed with UX/UI 
Business Rules
When any user hits their usage limit, a single paywall must be displayed with the message "0 prompts remaining"
The same paywall applies to all tiers for GA — no differentiation between Lite, Base, or Unlimited users
The paywall must be displayed in response to the Zephr enforcement signal
A "Find out more" CTA must be visible on the paywall. Clicking it navigates the user to 
www.infrastructureinvestor.com/memberships
 in the same browser tab.
The ‘Find out more’ CTA should be given the same treatment as ‘Request a demo’ button, i.e- light background.
Package
Limits
Anonymous user
-
LIte/Registered (Formerly Bronze) - This doesn’t exist in zephr- only registered user is considered as lite user. (No product assigned in zephr)
LTL [
4
 lifetime questions/queries]
Base Membership
 (
pei-pi-base-membership
)
Monthly limit [
10
 questions/queries per month]
Unlimited Membership
 (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
)
Unlimited / Fair use limit [1000 questions per day]
Base Membership 
- TRIAL (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
)
Monthly limit [
10
 questions/queries per month]
Unlimited Membership 
- TRIAL (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
)
Unlimited / Fair use limit [1000 questions per day]
UX/UI
Figma Link: 

 
UI:
A single simplified paywall is shown to all users upon hitting their limit, displaying "0 PROMPTS REMAINING"
The full tiered paywall designs (as per Figma) are not in scope for GA
Refer to Figma for paywall component placement and styling
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1 - Paywall displayed on limit reached
GIVEN 
any user has reached their Ask PEI usage limit,

WHEN 
they attempt to submit a query,

THEN 
the simplified paywall is displayed with the message "0 prompts remaining",

AND
 the ‘Find out more’ CTA with a light background.
AC2 - Paywall displays without full page reload
GIVEN 
the paywall is triggered,

THEN 
it displays without a full page reload.
AC3 - Paywall not displayed within limit
GIVEN 
a user has not reached their limit,

WHEN 
they submit a query,

THEN 
no paywall is displayed.
AC4 - Find out more CTA navigation
GIVEN 
the paywall is displayed,

WHEN 
the user clicks "Find out more",

THEN 
they are navigated to 
www.infrastructureinvestor.com/memberships
 in the same browser tab.

title: Ask PEI - Enforce paywall with prompt counter
description:
Background
Ask PEI usage must be restricted based on a user's membership tier. A previous decision was made to implement a single simplified paywall UI across all tiers without a prompt counter. This ticket supersedes that approach. The paywall must now be enforced per subscription tier with tier-specific behaviour, and a prompt count indicator must be displayed when a user has fewer than 5 prompts remaining, giving users visibility of their usage before the paywall is triggered.
User Story
AS A
 user accessing Ask PEI,

I WANT
 my access and usage to reflect my membership tier and to be informed when I am approaching my limit,

SO THAT
 I can use Ask PEI within the limits of my subscription,

AND 
I am aware of my remaining prompts before I run out, 

AND
 I am prompted to upgrade when I reach my limit.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to enforce tier-specific paywall rules within Ask PEI and to display a remaining prompts count indicator when a user has fewer than 5 prompts remaining, across all applicable membership tiers.
Out of Scope
Any changes to membership or subscription management
Any changes to the underlying Ask PEI functionality or logic
Any changes to membership tiers or their associated limits
Prerequisites
Zephr rules configured for user memberships (

 )
Zephyr feature slug is connected to Ask PEI (see Zephyr hook-up ticket [

 ])
Business Rules
IF
 a user has a Lite membership (
pei-pi-lite
) 
AND
 the number of lifetime questions remaining is fewer than 5 
THEN
 the prompt count indicator must be displayed showing the number of lifetime questions remaining and a ‘Request a demo’ CTA should be visible 
ELSE
 the prompt count indicator must not be displayed.  (
Refer to UI 1a
)
IF
 a user has a Base Membership (
pei-pi-base-membership
) or Base Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
) 
AND
 the number of monthly questions remaining is fewer than 5 
THEN
 the prompt count indicator must be displayed showing the number of monthly questions remaining and a ‘Request a demo’ CTA should be visible 
ELSE
 the prompt count indicator must not be displayed. (
Refer to UI 1b
)
IF
 a user has an Unlimited Membership (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
) or Unlimited Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
) 
AND
 the number of daily questions remaining is fewer than 5 
THEN
 the prompt count indicator must be displayed showing the number of daily questions remaining 
ELSE
 the prompt count indicator must not be displayed. - NOT REQUIRED SINCE UNLIMITED USERS CAN ASK 1000 PROMPTS PER DAY.
IF
 a user has a Lite membership (
pei-pi-lite
) 
THEN
 they can ask a lifetime total of 4 questions 
ELSE IF
 the user has reached their 4 question lifetime limit 
THEN
 the paywall must be displayed. (
Refer to UI 2a
)
IF
 a user has a Base Membership (
pei-pi-base-membership
) or Base Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
) 
THEN
 they can ask up to 10 questions per month 
ELSE IF
 the user has reached their 10 question monthly limit 
THEN
 the paywall must be displayed. (
Refer to UI 2b
)
IF
 a user has an Unlimited Membership (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
) or Unlimited Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
) 
THEN 
they can ask up to 200 questions per day under the fair use limit 
ELSE IF
 the user has reached their 200 question daily limit 
THEN
 the paywall must be displayed. - NOT REQUIRED SINCE UNLIMITED USERS CAN ASK 1000 PROMPTS PER DAY.
The prompt count must accurately reflect the user's remaining usage based on their membership tier - lifetime remaining for Lite, monthly remaining for Base and Base Trial, and daily remaining for Unlimited and Unlimited Trial.
A "Find out more" CTA must be visible on the paywall. Clicking it navigates the user to 
www.infrastructureinvestor.com/memberships
 in the same browser tab. (
Refer to UI 3a
)
NFRs:
Responsive across the 375, 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Usage limits must be enforced accurately and consistently — no user must be able to exceed their defined limit.
The prompt count must update accurately in real time as the user submits questions.
Add data test IDs to the paywall and prompt count indicator components.
Package
Limits
Anonymous user
-
LIte/Registered (Formerly Bronze) - This doesn’t exist in zephr- only registered user is considered as lite user. (No product assigned in zephr)
LTL [
4
 lifetime questions/queries]
Base Membership
 (
pei-pi-base-membership
)
Monthly limit [
10
 questions/queries per month]
Unlimited Membership
 (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
)
Unlimited / Fair use limit [200 questions per day]
Base Membership 
- TRIAL (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
)
Monthly limit [
10
 questions/queries per month]
Unlimited Membership 
- TRIAL (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
)
Unlimited / Fair use limit [200 questions per day]
UX/UI
Figma Link: 

 
UI:
Refer to Figma for paywall component placement and styling
1 a. Prompt countdown indicator for Lite users 
1 b. 
Prompt countdown for Base and Base trial users 
2 a.
 
Paywall shown for Lite users post rate limit 
2 b. Paywall shown for Base and Base Trial users post rate limit 
3 a. “Find Out more should anchor to this section
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1: Lite Membership Limit Enforced
GIVEN a user has a Lite membership (
pei-pi-lite
),

WHEN the user has asked fewer than 4 lifetime questions,

THEN the user can continue to use Ask PEI.
GIVEN a user has a Lite membership (
pei-pi-lite
),

WHEN the user has reached their 4 lifetime question limit,

THEN the paywall is displayed and the user cannot submit further questions.
AC2: Base Membership Limit Enforced
GIVEN a user has a Base Membership (
pei-pi-base-membership
) or Base Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
),

WHEN the user has asked fewer than 10 questions in the current month,

THEN the user can continue to use Ask PEI.
GIVEN a user has a Base Membership (
pei-pi-base-membership
) or Base Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
),

WHEN the user has reached their 10 question monthly limit,

THEN the paywall is displayed and the user cannot submit further questions.
AC3: Unlimited Membership Fair Use Limit Enforced
GIVEN a user has an Unlimited Membership (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
) or Unlimited Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
),

WHEN the user has asked fewer than 200 questions on the current day,

THEN the user can continue to use Ask PEI.
GIVEN a user has an Unlimited Membership (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership
) or Unlimited Membership - Trial (
pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
),

WHEN the user has reached their 200 question daily fair use limit,

THEN the paywall is displayed and the user cannot submit further questions.
AC4: Prompt Count Displayed When Below 5 - Lite
GIVEN a user has a Lite membership (
pei-pi-lite
),

AND fewer than 5 lifetime questions remaining,

WHEN the Ask PEI interface renders THEN the prompt count indicator displays the accurate number of lifetime questions remaining.
AC5: Prompt Count Displayed When Below 5 - Base and Base Trial
GIVEN a user has a Base Membership or Base Membership - Trial,

AND fewer than 5 monthly questions remaining,

WHEN the Ask PEI interface renders,

THEN the prompt count indicator displays the accurate number of monthly questions remaining.
AC6: Prompt Count Displayed When Below 5 - Unlimited and Unlimited Trial
GIVEN a user has an Unlimited Membership or Unlimited Membership - Trial,

AND fewer than 5 daily questions remaining,

WHEN the Ask PEI interface renders,

THEN the prompt count indicator displays the accurate number of daily questions remaining.
AC7: Prompt Count Not Displayed When 5 or More Remaining
GIVEN a user has 5 or more prompts remaining within their membership limit,

WHEN the Ask PEI interface renders,

THEN no prompt count indicator is displayed.
AC8: Prompt Count Updates in Real Time
GIVEN a user has fewer than 5 prompts remaining and submits a question,

WHEN the question is submitted and a response is received,

THEN the prompt count indicator updates to reflect the new remaining count accurately.
AC9: Paywall Displayed as per Figma
GIVEN a user has reached their usage limit for their membership tier,

WHEN the paywall is displayed,

THEN it is styled and presented as per the Figma design.
AC10: Find Out More CTA on Paywall
GIVEN a user has reached their usage limit and the paywall is displayed,

WHEN the user views the paywall,

THEN a "Find out more" CTA is visible on the paywall.
GIVEN a user clicks the "Find out more" CTA on the paywall,

WHEN the click is registered,

THEN the user is navigated to 
www.infrastructureinvestor.com/memberships
 in the same browser tab.
AC11: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN a user views Ask PEI at 375px, 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN the paywall or prompt count indicator is displayed,

THEN both are correctly displayed and functional across all supported breakpoints.
