title: [DevOps] - Setup OBX-PERE Domain
description:
Background
As part of expanding the OBX platform to additional PEI brands, the existing II OBX platform needs to be replicated as a bare-bones skeleton for the PERE OBX platform. This skeleton will serve as the foundation for future PERE OBX development. Since PERE OBX will share a codebase with II OBX, care must be taken throughout implementation to ensure no changes break the existing II OBX site. A temporary domain will be used until the business confirms the final domain.
USER STORY 
AS A
 developer building the PERE OBX platform,

I WANT
 a bare-bones skeleton of the II OBX platform to be available for PERE OBX,

SO THAT 
PERE OBX development can begin from a stable and consistent foundation without impacting the existing II OBX site.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to replicate the II OBX platform as a bare-bones skeleton for PERE OBX, including the header, footer, and basic page structure. The skeleton must be accessible via a temporary domain until the final domain is confirmed by the business.
Outcomes: 
Devops support will be needed for the 
Domain Setup & Deployment Pipeline
 
The site will be deployed on a temporary domain until the final domain is confirmed by the business.
Basic auth will be applied to the temporary domain to prevent unintended public access prior to launch.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: Temporary Domain Accessible
GIVEN 
the skeleton has been deployed,

WHEN 
the temporary domain is accessed by an authorised user,

THEN 
the PERE OBX skeleton loads correctly on the temporary domain.
AC2: Basic Auth on Temporary Domain
GIVEN 
the skeleton has been deployed to the temporary domain,

WHEN 
an unauthorized user attempts to access it,

THEN 
basic authentication is enforced and access is restricted to authorized users only.

title: OBX PERE - Zephr baseline configuration
description:
Background 
The PERE OBX domain has been set up as a bare-bones skeleton [

 ]. To ensure further integrations can be connected efficiently and consistently, a baseline configuration must be established for each required service.
This ticket covers the Zephr baseline configuration for PERE OBX so that subscription and access control integrations can be built on top of it in subsequent tickets.
User Story 
AS A
 developer building integrations on PERE OBX,

I WANT
 a Zephr baseline configuration to be in place,

SO THAT
 subscription and access control functionality can be integrated consistently and without friction in future tickets.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to establish a Zephr baseline configuration for PERE OBX. Full integration and functional validation are out of scope and will be addressed in subsequent tickets.
Out of Scope
Full Zephr integration or functional testing on PERE OBX.
Any changes to the existing Zephr configuration on II OBX.
Any PERE OBX features or components beyond Zephr baseline configuration.
Business Rules
A Zephr baseline configuration must be established for PERE OBX, consistent with the II OBX configuration where applicable.
The configuration must be structured to make further Zephr integrations on PERE OBX straightforward to implement.
NFRs
No degradation to existing Zephr behaviour on II OBX as a result of this change.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 
Miro:
Confluence:
UX:
UI: Not applicable.
Assumptions / Open Questions / Dependency
Acceptance Criteria
 
AC1: Zephr Baseline Configuration in Place
GIVEN
 the Zephr baseline configuration has been completed,

WHEN
 the configuration is reviewed,

THEN
 Zephr is configured for PERE OBX at a baseline level and is ready for further integration work.
AC2: No Regression on II OBX
GIVEN
 the Zephr baseline configuration has been completed for PERE OBX,

WHEN
 the II OBX platform is accessed,

THEN
 the existing Zephr configuration on II OBX is unaffected.

title: [TECH] Logging - Authentication events are not logged (High)
description:
Problem
The following pages and components have no logging at all:
app/auth/signin/page.tsx
app/auth/signout/page.tsx
app/[brand]/registration/page.tsx
components/session/session-sync-all.tsx
 — session state changes (authenticated / unauthenticated / loading) are never logged
app/api/register/route.ts
 only logs on failure — successful registration is silent.
What needs to be done
Log login event: timestamp, auth method, user ID
Log logout event: user ID, session duration
Log registration success: user ID, brand
Log session state changes in 
session-sync-all.tsx
: previous state → new state, user ID
Ensure all auth events carry 
user.sessionId
 for journey correlation
Acceptance criteria
Login, logout, registration, and session state transitions all produce structured log entries in DataDog. Auth-related user journeys are traceable by 
@user.id
.

title: [TECH] Logging - Page view tracking is not implemented (High)
description:
Problem
There is no logging of route changes or page views. 
app/layout.tsx
 and 
app/[brand]/layout.tsx
 have no page view events. It is not possible to trace which pages a user visited, in what order, or how they navigated through the app.
DataDog RUM is initialised with 
trackUserInteractions: true
 but this only captures raw DOM events — not structured page view events tied to the app's routing.
Acceptance criteria
Every page navigation produces a structured 
page_view
 log in DataDog containing the route path and brand. A user's page sequence is reconstructable by filtering on 
@user.sessionId
.

title: [TECH] Logging - Global error boundary does not log to DataDog (High)
description:
Problem
app/global-error.tsx
 renders an 
<ErrorFallback>
 component when an uncaught runtime error occurs but never calls the logger. Uncaught errors are completely silent in DataDog — there is no alert or log entry when the app crashes for a user.
Acceptance criteria
When an uncaught runtime error occurs, a structured error log appears in DataDog with error type, message, stack trace, and affected user context.

title: [TECH] Logging - REST API calls have no structured logging (High)
description:
Scope
Structured DataDog logging must cover the 
entire request path
, not just the caller side:
[browser/SSR] → app-portfolio → /api/proxy or direct → api-fusion → upstream (OpenSearch / DynamoDB / Algolia / GraphDB / 3rd-party) → response
                    ↑                                       ↑
              caller-side logs                        server-side logs
              (currently partial)                     (partial — middleware only)
A request must produce 
correlated
 structured log entries at every hop, queryable in DataDog by a single shared trace/correlation ID.
In scope
apps/app-portfolio
 (caller side)
Three originally-flagged offenders with no logging at all:
lib/services/fetch-view-counts.ts
lib/services/fetch-dataribbon.ts
lib/services/server/zephr-decision.service.ts
Every other outbound HTTP call
 in 
app-portfolio
 (callers of 
fetchFusionApi
, 
fetchFusionAPI
, 
fetchGraphQLBaseAPI
, 
fetchBaseAPI
, raw 
fetch()
, route handlers under 
app/api/*/route.ts
).
Outbound calls must inject DataDog/OpenTelemetry trace headers so 
api-fusion
 can join the same trace.
apps/api-fusion
 (server side)
Current state:
common/middlewares/logger.middleware.ts logs every incoming request + response — exists.
common/logger/custom-logger.service.ts — pino-based exists.
common/tracing/datadog-tracer.config.ts — exists.
Gaps to close:
Field names 
don't follow DataDog standard attributes
 — current middleware emits 
method
, 
url
, 
statusCode
, 
duration: "123ms"
 (string!). Rename to 
http.method
, 
http.url
, 
http.status_code
, 
duration
 (numeric ns) + 
http.url_details.path
 etc. (full schema below). Without this, DataDog can't auto-facet, build log-based metrics, or correlate with APM.
No correlation ID accepted from upstream callers
 — middleware doesn't read 
x-request-id
 / 
x-datadog-trace-id
 / 
traceparent
 from incoming headers and doesn't bind them to the per-request log context. Without this, an 
app-portfolio
 log and the corresponding 
api-fusion
 log can't be linked.
Per-controller / per-service operation logging is inconsistent
 — middleware only captures request boundaries. Business operations inside controllers/services (e.g. transaction aggregation, dataribbon assembly, OpenSearch query dispatch) frequently log via 
console.*
 or not at all. Each meaningful operation should emit 
start
 / 
end
 logs sharing the request's trace ID.
Outbound calls from api-fusion
 (to OpenSearch, DynamoDB, Algolia, GraphDB, any 3rd-party API) lack structured per-call logs.
Any 
console.log
 / 
console.error
 in controllers, services, or interceptors must be replaced with 
CustomLoggerService
.
Cross-cutting
Both apps must emit logs using the 
same field schema
 (DataDog standard attributes) so a single Logs Explorer query works across both services.
Both apps must participate in the 
same DataDog APM trace
 for any given request.
Out of scope
Other apps (
app-main
, 
app-networks
, 
app-deals-dataentry
, 
api-networks
, 
api-dataportal
) — separate tickets if they have similar gaps.
Building DataDog dashboards/monitors on top of the new logs — follow-up once data is flowing.
Acceptance criteria
Shared field schema (DataDog standard attributes) — both apps must emit:
DataDog attribute
Required
Notes
http.method
✅
Replaces 
method
 / 
statusCode
 field collisions
http.url
✅
Sanitize sensitive query params before logging
http.url_details.host
✅
Auto-faceting per upstream
http.url_details.path
✅
Low-cardinality endpoint faceting
http.url_details.queryString
✅
Sanitized
http.status_code
✅
Numeric — replaces 
status
 / 
statusCode
http.request_id
✅
Correlation ID — see point 3 below
duration
✅
Nanoseconds (DataDog standard) 
or
 use 
duration_ms
 (numeric, not string)
network.bytes_read
recommended
Response body size
network.bytes_written
recommended
Request body size
error.message
✅ on failure
error.kind
✅ on failure
FetchError
, 
ZephrAuthError
, 
OpenSearchTimeout
, etc.
error.stack
✅ on failure
Required for DataDog Error Tracking grouping
usr.id
 
✅
Zephr session user — populates User Journey
dd.trace_id
✅
APM ↔ Logs ↔ RUM correlation
dd.span_id
✅
pei.upstream
✅
fusion-api
 / 
zephr
 / 
algolia
 / 
wordpress-graphql
 / 
opensearch
 / 
dynamodb
 / 
graphdb
pei.module
✅
Service/feature identifier (
view-counts
, 
data-ribbon
, 
transactions
, 
zephr-decision
)
pei.operation.id
✅
Per-call ID — 
${operationName}_${Date.now()}
pei.operation.name
✅
fetch_view_counts
, 
aggregate_transactions
, 
dispatch_opensearch_query
pei.outcome
✅
success
 / 
error
pei.brand
✅
PERE
 / 
II
pei.render.context
app-portfolio only
server
 / 
client
 / 
edge
pei.cache.tags
 / 
pei.cache.revalidate
 / 
pei.cache.hit
optional
Next.js cache details
pei.attempt
when retrying
Retry counter
status
 is reserved by DataDog for log severity
 (
info
/
warn
/
error
). Don't put HTTP code there. Existing 
LoggerMiddleware
 uses 
statusCode
 which is also non-standard — must rename to 
http.status_code
.
duration
 must be numeric
, not the string 
"123ms"
 currently emitted by logger.middleware.ts:32. Otherwise DataDog can't aggregate/percentile it.
Every outbound call from 
app-portfolio
 emits a 
start
 log (
info
) and an 
end
 log (
info
 on success, 
error
 on failure), sharing the same 
pei.operation.id
.
Trace correlation works end-to-end:
app-portfolio
 injects DataDog trace headers (
x-datadog-trace-id
, 
x-datadog-parent-id
, 
x-datadog-sampling-priority
, 
traceparent
) on every outbound call to 
api-fusion
. Header injection is partially scaffolded in fusion-api-data.service.ts:116-126 (browser only) — extend to server-side using the existing OpenTelemetry context from 
instrumentation.ts
.
api-fusion
 
LoggerMiddleware
 reads incoming trace headers, derives or reuses a request-scoped 
dd.trace_id
 / 
dd.span_id
 / 
http.request_id
, and binds them to a child logger that all downstream services in the request use.
Verification: a Logs Explorer query 
@dd.trace_id:<id>
 returns matching log entries from both 
service:app-portfolio
 AND 
service:api-fusion
.
api-fusion
 controllers and services emit per-operation structured logs
 (start/end with 
pei.operation.id
) for any non-trivial operation: DB queries, OpenSearch queries, Algolia queries, GraphDB queries, outbound HTTP calls to 3rd-parties. Not just request-boundary logging from the middleware.
No 
console.*
 calls remain in HTTP-handling code paths
 in either app.
apps/app-portfolio/lib/services/**
, 
apps/app-portfolio/lib/actions/**
, 
apps/app-portfolio/app/api/**
apps/api-fusion/src/modules/**
, 
apps/api-fusion/src/common/**
 (except inside 
CustomLoggerService
 itself)
Logs are visible in DataDog with the new schema
 — verified via the queries below in stage:
service:(app-portfolio OR api-fusion) @http.status_code:>=400
service:(app-portfolio OR api-fusion) @pei.upstream:fusion-api @pei.outcome:error
@dd.trace_id:<some-id>                                   # returns BOTH services' entries
service:api-fusion @pei.module:transactions @duration:>1000000000
service:app-portfolio @error.kind:FetchError
Reusable helpers are introduced (or existing ones extended):
app-portfolio
: a 
withHttpLog(log, { op, method, url }, async () => ...)
 wrapper that emits start/end + duration + DataDog-standard fields, so future services can't accidentally skip logging. Suggested location: 
apps/app-portfolio/lib/logger/with-http-logging.ts
.
api-fusion
: extend 
CustomLoggerService
 with a 
child({ traceId, requestId, ... })
 method bound by the middleware, so controllers/services can log inside the same trace context without re-plumbing IDs.

title: Search Engine Optimization - META Tags - Canonical link to reflect URL with trialing slash
description:
Background
As part of the OBX V2 SEO work, canonical tags are being implemented across article pages. To ensure consistency and avoid duplicate content issues, canonical URLs must always be rendered in a standardised format — specifically, all canonical links must include a trailing slash regardless of the URL format of the incoming request.
User Story 
As a Developer, 

I want to ensure that canonical URLs on OBX article pages are always rendered server-side with a trailing slash,

 So that we avoid duplicate content indexing caused by inconsistent URL formats.
Scope 
To implement server-side logic that standardises canonical link URLs to always include a trailing slash on OBX article pages.
Business Rules
 
The canonical tag rendered on the OBX article page must always include a trailing slash at the end of the URL.
This logic must be applied server-side to ensure the canonical tag is correct on initial page load.
If the incoming URL does not include a trailing slash, the server-side canonical tag generation must append one before rendering.
This rule applies to all editorial templates (A, B, C, and D).
Out of Scope
Redirecting users from non-trailing-slash URLs to trailing-slash URLs , this ticket covers canonical tag rendering only.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
SEO requirements sheet
 
Tabel 1: If no values are added in Meta titles, Meta descriptions, Canonical tags on WP for either page
Page
Canonical Link
Editorial Template A - Standard with image
Self-referencing with trailing slash
Editorial Template B - Standard with no image
Self-referencing with trailing slash
Editorial Template C - Full width
Self-referencing with trailing slash
Editorial Template D - Full bleed
Self-referencing with trailing slash
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Dependency- Yoast already provides: 
Meta titles
Meta descriptions
Canonical tags
These can be edited per article in the Yoast meta box
Relevant Documentation

OBX Platform_Search Engine Optimisation V2_Requirement List.xlsx
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 – Canonical tag includes trailing slash when URL already has one

GIVEN I navigate to an OBX article page using a URL that includes a trailing slash

WHEN the page renders

THEN the canonical tag in the page head should reflect that URL with the trailing slash intact.
Scenario 2 – Canonical tag includes trailing slash when URL does not have one
 

GIVEN I navigate to an OBX article page using a URL without a trailing slash 

WHEN the page renders 

THEN the canonical tag in the page head should reflect the URL with a trailing slash appended server-side.
Scenario 3 – Canonical tag is rendered server-side
 

GIVEN an OBX article page is requested

WHEN the page is served 

THEN the canonical tag with the standardised trailing slash URL must be present in the HTML on initial load without reliance on client-side rendering.

title: Add Heap Tracking to Article Page Paywall Gate
description:
Background
OBX article pages currently do not expose whether a paywall is present in the datalayer. This limits our ability to analyse article access conditions, understand how often users are being exposed to gated content, and segment downstream behaviour by paywall presence. The product and analytics teams need a simple page level signal so they can distinguish between article views where a paywall is shown and article views where it is not.
Pre-Requisite
Heap analytics utility already implemented at 
/apps/app-portfolio/lib/utils/heap-analytics.ts
OBX article pages must already publish a datalayer on page load
USER STORY 
As a product / analytics team member

I want a datalayer field on every OBX article page that tells me whether a paywall is present

So that I can track and analyse article behaviour by paywall presence
Scope
Add a new page-level field to the OBX article page datalayer that indicates whether a paywall is present on the page.
Business rules
If an OBX article page is rendered, then the datalayer must include a field indicating whether a paywall is present on that page
If a paywall is present on the OBX article page, then the field should return a positive value such as
present
 
If no paywall is present on the OBX article page, then the field should return a negative value such as 
not_present
The field should be populated on page load, not only after user interaction
The field should only apply to OBX article pages
If a paywall is present on an OBX article page, then the 
.paywall-article
 CSS selector must be applied to the paywalled content element on the page so that the paywall structured data schema can reference it correctly.

{

"obx_paywall": "present"

}
Out of Scope
Adding this field to any Classic pages

title: Watchlist Page - Related Articles - Update logic to not show new notification for historical articles
description:
Background
We have received customer feedback requesting access to historical articles for institutions and funds they are following. As part of the OBX watchlist feature, we will be updating the watchlist page to display articles from the last one year for institutions and funds a user is currently following.
Scope 
To update logic to not display the new notifications for historical articles on the OBX site.
Business Rules 
Ensure the count of new articles “  X New” is not displayed in the watchlist page WHEN the historical articles are displayed from the last one year and no new article has been published after the user has followed a fund/institution
Ensure the notification count and red icon are not displayed for historical articles.
IF a user starts following an institution/fund, AND only historical articles (from the last one year calculated from the date of following) are available, THEN do not display the red “X” notification count in the page header or the red dot notification in the bento box.
IF a user is currently following an institution/fund, AND historical articles are displayed due to the updated logic, THEN do not display the red “X” notification count in the page header or the red dot notification in the bento box.
Ensure no changes are made to the existing display of notifications for newly published articles after the user has followed the entity.

OBX Infrastructure Portfolio Site > OBX Homepage > Historical articles  > Red icon for new notification

GIVEN

 only historical articles (from the last one year calculated from the date of following) are displayed

the “X new” count should not be displayed on the watchlist page
AND 
the red “X New” notification count should not be displayed in the page header
AND
 the red dot notification should not be displayed in the bento box

 I am a user on the watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND

THEN
 the red “X New” count should be displayed in the watchlist page
THEN
 the red “X New” notification count should be displayed in the page header
AND
 the red dot notification should be displayed in the bento box
AND
 the notification count should represent only newly published articles since I last viewed the page

title: Replace current 404 response with a designed page not found error page
description:
Background
When a reader navigates to an article URL that does not exist, the OBX platform currently returns a detour response. The team has agreed to replace this with a properly designed page not found error page with helpful navigation within the OBX design system.
Pre-Requisite
UX/UI design for the 404 page must be confirmed before implementation
USER STORY 
As a reader, 

I want to see a designed page not found message when I navigate to a URL that does not exist,

 So that I understand the page is unavailable and can navigate elsewhere.
Scope
Replace the current page not found error with a properly designed 404 page for the OBX article page.
Business Rules
IF a reader navigates to an article URL that does not exist, THEN the 404 page must be displayed showing the "Detour Ahead!" message and supporting copy.
IF the 404 page is displayed, THEN a "Link to home page" CTA must be present and must navigate the reader to the II homepage.
IF the 404 page is displayed, THEN links need to be displayed below the error message (Links based on UX decision) 

 
For now it is a single link
IF the 404 page is displayed, THEN ads must NOT to display.
Usability / Design UX/UI
UX - the article body area must display an error message for all break points (1440px, 1024px, 1920px, 768px,375px)
Out of Scope

title: Replace "Detour Ahead" error with a proper error state when WP content API fails to load article body
description:
Background
The OBX article page relies on the WP content rendering API to retrieve and display the article body. Currently, when this API fails, the page displays a generic "Detour Ahead" message. The team has agreed to replace this with a proper, contextually appropriate error state.
Pre-Requisite
UX/UI design for the error state must be confirmed before implementation
USER STORY 
As a reader,

I want to see a relevant error message when an article fails to load,

So that I understand the content is unavailable rather than seeing a generic error page.
Scope
Replace the current "Detour Ahead" display with a defined error state when the WP content rendering API fails to load the article body.
Business Rules
IF the WP content API returns an error or times out, THEN the article body area must display a proper content error state in place of the current "Detour Ahead" message.
IF the WP content API fails, THEN no RHS components or ads must be displayed on the page.
The error message should be shown as per desing across breakpoints 1440. 1024, 1920, 768,375
Out of Scope
Define what is included/ and not included in the scope for the User story

Miro:
Confluence:
Glossary:
Sample test data:
UX:
UI:

TO BE:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Identify Assumptions, risks, and dependencies during requirements gathering for use cases and user stories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1

 GIVEN a reader navigates to an article page

 WHEN the WP content API returns an error or times out

THEN the article body area must display a proper content unavailable error message 

AND the "Detour Ahead" page must not be shown and be rsponsive across breakpoints 1440, 1024, 1920, 768,375 px

AND ads or RHS COMPONENTS should not display

title: Watchlist Page - Related Articles - Update logic to display historical articles
description:
Background
We have received customer feedback requesting access to historical articles for institutions and funds they are following. As part of the OBX watchlist feature, we will be updating the watchlist page to display articles from the last one year for institutions and funds a user is currently following.
Scope 
To display articles from the last one year for user-followed entities (institution/fund) on the watchlist page on the OBX homepage.
Out of Scope
 Update logic to not show new notification for historical articles. This will be handled in 

 
Business Rules 
Ensure the existing watchlist page displays historical last one year data for user followed and following entities.
Ensure the last one year data is calculated from the date the user started following each entity.
IF a user starts following an institution/fund, AND historical articles exist for that institution/fund, AND the articles are tagged as 'About' in WordPress Editorial, THEN display all articles published about that institution/fund within the last one year from the date the user started following.
IF a user is currently following an institution/fund, AND historical articles exist for that institution/fund, AND the articles are tagged as 'About' in WordPress Editorial, THEN display all articles published about that institution/fund within the last one year from the date the user started following.
IF a user starts following an institution/fund or is currently following an institution/fund , AND no historical articles are available within the last one year from the date of following, AND no new articles have been published since the user started following, THEN display 
“There are currently no new updates for [institution / fund] name”.
 (As per existing behavior)
IF a user starts following or is currently following an institution/fund, AND no historical articles are available within the last one year from the date of following, AND new articles have been published after the user started following, THEN display articles (As per existing implementation.)
Ensure no other changes are made to the order of display, primary & secondary grouping logic, Article card items, Filters or navigation.
UX / UI 
OBX Infrastructure Portfolio Site > Watchlist Page > Historical Articles
Figma File: 
https://www.figma.com/design/DW8o5XuftdDA5JYILVcvlW/Watchlist?node-id=1-21368&t=YS1gwM3iQwfFPEMq-0
  
Assumptions / Open Questions / Dependencies / Documentation 
WatchList ARD
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1: Display historical articles for followed entity (new or existing)
GIVEN
 I am a user on the Watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND
 I start following or am already following an institution/fund
WHEN
 I view the page
THEN
 articles tagged as “About” for that institution/fund should be displayed
AND
 only articles published within the last one year from the date I started following the entity are shown.
Scenario 2: No historical or new articles available
GIVEN
 I am a user on the Watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND
 I start following or am already following an institution/fund
WHEN
 no articles are available within the last one year from the follow date
AND
 no new articles have been published since I started following
THEN
 the message “There are currently no new updates for [institution/fund name]” should be displayed
Scenario 3: New articles available but no historical articles
GIVEN
 I am a user on the Watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND
 I start following or am already following an institution/fund
WHEN
 no articles are available within the last one year from the follow date
AND
 new articles have been published after I started following
THEN
 the newly published articles should be displayed
Scenario 4: Historical articles ordering and grouping
GIVEN
 I am a user on the watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND
 historical & new articles are available for followed entities
WHEN
 I view the page 
THEN
 historical articles should be sorted from latest to oldest by published date
AND
 articles are grouped by month and year
AND
 within each group, articles are ordered from latest to oldest
Scenario 5: Consistent behavior across filters and no impact to existing logic
GIVEN
 I am a user on the watchlist page on the OBX Homepage
AND
 I view “All Followed” or select a specific institution/fund
WHEN
 articles are displayed
THEN
 the same ordering, grouping, and filtering logic is applied
AND
 no changes are made to existing grouping logic, article cards, filters, or navigation

title: OBX Homepage - News & Analysis - Fix carousel autoplay
description:
Background
The News & Analysis carousel in the bento box section of the OBX II homepage currently displays 4 articles but requires manual interaction to cycle through them. To improve content discoverability and engagement, the carousel should automatically advance through articles at a defined interval, pausing when a user hovers over it to allow them to read or interact without interruption.
USER STORY 
AS A 
user visiting the OBX II homepage,

I WANT
 the News & Analysis carousel to automatically cycle through articles,

SO THAT 
I can discover content without needing to manually interact with the carousel.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement autoplay functionality on the News & Analysis carousel in the bento box section of the OBX II homepage. The carousel must advance automatically every 8 seconds and pause on hover.
Out of Scope
Any changes to the articles displayed in the carousel or their content
Any changes to the carousel design or layout
Autoplay on any other carousels on the OBX II site
Any changes to the carousel on classic WordPress pages
Business Rules: 
The carousel must automatically advance to the next article every 8 seconds.
The carousel must pause autoplay when a user hovers over it.
The carousel must resume autoplay when the user moves their cursor away from it.
Autoplay must loop continuously through all 4 articles and return to the first article after the last one is reached.
If a user manually navigates the carousel, autoplay must resume from the currently active article after the cursor leaves the carousel.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
Autoplay must not cause any layout shifts or visual glitches when the carousel advances.
Out of Scope
None
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
No design changes expected. The carousel must retain its existing appearance.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks/ Open Questions
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
AC1: Carousel Advances Automatically
GIVEN 
a user visits the OBX II homepage,

WHEN 
the News & Analysis carousel is visible and no user interaction has occurred,

THEN 
the carousel automatically advances to the next article every 8 seconds.
AC2: Carousel Loops Continuously
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
the last article is reached,

THEN 
the carousel returns to the first article and continues looping without interruption.
AC3: Autoplay Pauses on Hover
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
a user hovers over the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay pauses and the current article remains visible for as long as the cursor is over the carousel.
AC4: Autoplay Resumes on Cursor Leave
GIVEN 
a user has hovered over the carousel and autoplay has paused,

WHEN 
the user moves their cursor away from the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay resumes from the currently active article.
AC5: Manual Navigation Respected
GIVEN 
a user manually navigates to a specific article in the carousel,

WHEN 
the user's cursor leaves the carousel,

THEN 
autoplay resumes from the manually selected article.
AC6: No Visual Glitches
GIVEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

WHEN 
the carousel advances to the next article,

THEN 
the transition occurs smoothly with no layout shifts or visual glitches.
AC7: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user views the OBX II homepage at 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths,

WHEN 
the carousel is autoplaying,

THEN 
the autoplay behaviour functions correctly and the carousel remains visually consistent across all supported breakpoints.

title: Investor name chevron icon separates onto a new line when entity name is long
description:
DESCRIPTION
Beta feedback has been raised regarding the chevron (›)  indicator in the Investor data panel. 

When an investor name is long enough to wrap, the chevron drops to a new line, visually detaching from the label and resembling an accordion toggle rather than a navigation link.
ENVIRONMENTS | BROWSERS VERSIONS | URLs 
Prod
STEPS TO REPLICATE 
Navigate to an article page containing the Investor data panel where the investor name is long enough to wrap across multiple lines (e.g. Abu Dhabi Investment Authority).
EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR
The chevron indicator must always remain anchored to the end of the last line of the investor name text. Interactive labels and their  indicators must never be separated across lines.
OBSERVED BEHAVIOUR
The chevron wraps onto a new line below the investor name, making the element appear to be an accordion toggle rather than a view profile navigation action.
Impact
Misleads users into interpreting the chevron as an expand/collapse control rather than a link to the investor profile.

title: Paywall CTA buttons to navigate to the correct destination
description:

www.infrastructureinvestor.com/memberships

 top of the  subscriptions/memberships page.

 Web activity brand
 
= PEIPI
The Web Activity Sub-Type = demo_request

ser should be navigated to the submission confirmation page

-demo/confirmation
 on the same tab after the user clicks the

 button AND a successful response is received back

title: Re-enable R&A Tabs on Data Pages
description:
Background
When a user from OBX II navigates to the data pages, the R&A tabs on the data pages were previously disabled and replaced with breadcrumbs. The business has now decided to re-enable the tabs while retaining the breadcrumbs above them. This ticket covers restoring the tabs to their previous design and ensuring they co-exist correctly with the breadcrumbs.
User Story
AS A
 user navigating from the OBX II homepage to data pages,

I WANT
 the R&A tabs to be visible and functional alongside the breadcrumbs,

SO THAT
 I can navigate between data page sections as intended while retaining the breadcrumb navigation above.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to re-enable the R&A tabs on the data pages and to ensure the breadcrumbs remain in place above the tabs.
Out of Scope
Any changes to tab content or the data presented within each tab
Any changes to data pages on classic WordPress
Business Rules
The R&A tabs must be re-enabled on all data pages where they were previously disabled.
The tabs must be restored to their previous design as they appeared before being disabled.
All tabs must function correctly and navigate the user to the appropriate section on click.
NFRs
Responsive across the 1024, 1440, and 1920 viewports.
UX/UI
No new Figma design provided. The previous tab design must be used as the reference.
Previous view: 
Current view:
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Confirm that the path must be added to all environments and not just production.
Acceptance Criteria 
AC1: R&A Tabs Re-enabled
GIVEN 
a user visits a data page from OBX II homepage,

WHEN 
the page renders,

THEN 
the R&A tabs are visible and displayed in their previous design as they appeared before being disabled.
AC2: Breadcrumbs Retained Above Tabs
GIVEN 
a user visits a data page from OBX II homepage,

WHEN 
the page renders,

THEN 
the breadcrumbs are displayed above the R&A tabs and maintain their existing behaviour.
AC3: Tabs Navigate Correctly
GIVEN 
a user clicks a tab on a data page,

WHEN 
the click is registered,

THEN 
the user is taken to the correct section corresponding to the selected tab.
AC4: No Regression to Breadcrumbs
GIVEN 
the tabs have been re-enabled,

WHEN 
a user interacts with the breadcrumbs,

THEN 
the breadcrumb behaviour is unaffected and functions as it did prior to this change.
AC5: Responsive Behaviour
GIVEN 
a user views an OBX II data page at 1024px, 1440px, and 1920px viewport widths.

WHEN 
the page renders,

THEN 
the tabs are correctly displayed and functional across all supported breakpoints.

title: [QA] -Verify paywalls are displayed correctly on data & editorial pages for anonymous, lite and base users

Background
We need to implement a common homepage state for Registered, Lite, and Platinum users on the OBX Infrastructure portfolio site, where users are shown different paywall experiences upon clicking content, to support both the beta release and general availability.
User Story

user,
I want

So that 
I clearly understand my access level and what content is available to me.
Test Case Scope

Out of scope

Business Rules:  
IF the user is Anonymous, Registered, or Subscribed AND the user has exhausted their free access limits OR their subscription does not provide access to the content THEN the appropriate paywall should be displayed based on the page type:

title: Request a demo -(Anonymous Users Only) - Add a loading spinner during form submission
description:
Background
We’re introducing a new Request a Demo page with an improved user interface to enable prospects to submit demo requests and allow the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. The new Request a Demo page will be implemented on the new OBX infrastructure Fusion site
User Story
As a
 Prospect, 
I want
 to see a spinning loader after submitting the demo form,
So that
 I know my submission is being processed and I don’t attempt to resubmit the form.
Scope 
To display a spinning loader within the Request your demo CTA on the demo form
Business Rules: 
IF the user clicks on the “ Request your demo” CTA after successfully completing all mandatory fields, THEN a spinning loader must be displayed within the button AND the label in the button should change to “Loading”. 
The loader must appear immediately upon submission and remain visible until a response is received from the API. 
Once the loader is triggered, the “ Request your demo” button must be disabled to prevent multiple submissions.
While the loader is in progress, the entire form must be frozen, and the user must not be able to edit any input fields, change dropdown selections, select or deselect checkboxes, or interact with any other form elements
IF the submission is successful, the spinner should no longer be displayed AND the user must be redirected to the confirmation page
IF the submission fails due to an error, the spinner should no longer be displayed, AND the label in the button must revert to “ Request your demo”, AND the CTA must be re-enabled, AND the form fields must be editable again.
UX/UI
Request a demo > Spinning Loader on form submission
Figma Link: 
https://www.figma.com/design/vZl7jzgmLoA9tYPVAqt3Jf/Registration?node-id=185-34420&m=dev
 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None  
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 -  Loading spinner 
GIVEN
 I am a Prospect user, 
WHEN 
I click on the “ Request your demo” CTA after completing all mandatory fields
THEN
 I should see a spinning loader with the label changed to “Loading” 
AND
 the form should be disabled while the request is in progress 
AND
 I should be redirected to the confirmation page upon successful submission.

title: TECH - Ask PEI Optimization
description:
Background
As part of the OBX II performance improvement initiative, lazy loading is being applied across the application. The Ask PEI chat system currently loads all of its components as part of the initial page bundle, contributing significant weight regardless of whether a user interacts with it. This ticket applies lazy loading to the Ask PEI system so that its components only load when a user chooses to engage with it, with no impact on users who do not.
User Story
AS A 
user visiting OBX II,

I WANT 
the Ask PEI chat system to load only when I choose to interact with it,

SO THAT 
the initial page load is not slowed down by components I may never use.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to apply lazy loading to all identified Ask PEI components. All implementations must use the shared lazy loading infrastructure established in 

 .
Out of Scope 
Any changes to Ask PEI functionality, logic, or destination
Business Rules:
The Ask PEI Widget must be fully lazy loaded and must not be included in the initial page bundle.
The Ask PEI Dialog must only load when a user interacts with the Ask PEI entry point.
The Ask PEI Modal must load via dynamic import and must not be included in the initial page bundle.
The Ask PEI Split Wrapper and Authenticated Ask PEI Modal must lazy load, with chat hooks and utilities bundled separately from the main bundle.
The Ask PEI button must display a loading state while the Ask PEI components are loading following user interaction.
An error boundary must be in place to handle failed Ask PEI component loads.
A retry mechanism must be in place for Ask PEI load failures caused by network issues.
Users who do not interact with Ask PEI must experience no impact on page performance as a result of this change.
NFRs:
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link:  
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Ask PEI Components Lazy Loaded
 
GIVEN 
a user visits any OBX II page,

WHEN
 the page initially loads,

THEN 
the Ask PEI Widget, Ask PEI Dialog, Ask PEI Modal, Ask PEI Split Wrapper, and Authenticated Ask PEI Modal are not included in the initial page bundle.
AC2: Ask PEI Loads on Interaction
GIVEN
 a user clicks the Ask PEI entry point,

WHEN 
the interaction is registered,

THEN 
the Ask PEI components begin loading and a loading state is displayed on the Ask PEI button until the chat interface is ready.
AC3: Chat Interface Load Time
GIVEN 
a user interacts with the Ask PEI entry point,

WHEN
 the Ask PEI components have loaded,

THEN 
the chat interface is ready for use within one second of the user's interaction.
AC4: Error Handling and Retry
GIVEN
 a user attempts to load the Ask PEI chat interface,

WHEN 
a component fails to load due to a network issue,

THEN 
the error boundary prevents any impact to the rest of the page and a retry mechanism attempts to reload the Ask PEI components.
AC5: No Impact on Non-Ask PEI Users
GIVEN 
a user visits an OBX II page and does not interact with Ask PEI,

WHEN
 the page loads and the user browses,

THEN 
no Ask PEI components are loaded and there is no measurable impact on page performance for that user

title: TECH - Article Page optimization
description:
Background
As part of the OBX II performance improvement initiative, lazy loading is being applied across the application following the establishment of shared lazy loading infrastructure. This ticket applies that infrastructure to article page components, which currently load all content upfront regardless of whether a user scrolls to it. Optimizing these components will improve load times and the overall article reading experience.
User Story
AS A 
user reading an article on OBX II,

I WANT 
the page to load quickly and additional content to appear as I scroll,

SO THAT
 I can start reading without delay and related content loads smoothly when I need it.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to apply lazy loading to identified components on OBX II article pages, including related content, fund components, and article-specific UI components. All implementations must use the shared lazy loading infrastructure established in 

 .
Out of Scope 
Any changes to the content, layout, or functionality of affected components
Lazy loading on any pages other than OBX II article pages
Business Rules:
The Related News and Mentioned News components must only load when the user scrolls to the related content section of the article page.
Fund components displayed in the context of an article page, including 

RHS Latest New (Fund/Institution)

RHS Fund Information

RHS Intititution Information

RHS Key mentions

RHS Investor tools

RHS Fund Raising tool

RHS Latest News Generic

RHS Upcoming events

Bottom Latest new (Primary Tags)

RHS Ads, must lazy load and not be included in the initial page render.
The Responsive Portal component must lazy load, with any modal components loading on demand rather than on initial page render.
Date formatting components must be optimized as part of this ticket.
Load time should be within 2.5 seconds

NFRs:
Article page bundle size must be reduced as a result of this change.
Time to Interactive must be improved as a result of this change.
Related content must load without causing layout shifts.
UX/UI
Screenshot:
https://peimedia.atlassian.net/browse/EP-92
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Related Content Lazy Loading
GIVEN 
a user visits an OBX II article page,

WHEN 
the page initially loads,

THEN 
the Related News and Mentioned News components are not loaded until the user scrolls to the related content section of the page
AC2: Fund Components Lazy Loading
GIVEN 
a user visits an OBX II article page that includes fund components,

WHEN 
the page initially loads,

THEN 
RHS Latest New (Fund/Institution)

RHS Fund Information

RHS Intititution Information

RHS Key mentions

RHS Investor tools

RHS Fund Raising tool

RHS Latest News Generic

RHS Upcoming events

Bottom Latest new (Primary Tags)

RHS Ads are not included in the initial page render and load on demand
AC3: Article UI Components Lazy Loading
GIVEN 
a user visits an OBX II article page,

WHEN 
the page initially loads,

THEN 
the Responsive Portal component is not loaded until required, and any modal components load on demand rather than on initial page render
AC4: Performance Improvement
GIVEN 
the lazy loading changes have been implemented,

WHEN 
an OBX II article page is loaded,

THEN 
the page bundle size is reduced, Time to Interactive is improved, and related content loads without layout shifts compared to the pre-change baseline

title: To view paywall as per my user access rule
description:
Background
On paywalled article pages, the user's access tier is resolved by the Zephr SDK at page load. Based on this, the article body must be locked at the appropriate point and the paywall gate must be triggered. This ticket covers the logic for determining when a user hits a paywall and rendering the correct gate at the right point in the article body and via the RHS lock icon.
Pre-Requisite
Zephr SDK integration must be in place and able to expose user tier to the frontend

 
User tier taxonomy must be defined and implemented (Anonymous, Lite, Monthly Subscriber)
Paywall Modal and Paywall Banner Storybook components must be complete 

 
USER STORY 
As an end user,

I want to be shown a paywall gate when I attempt to access content I don't have permission to view

 So that I understand my access level and am given a clear path to register or upgrade.
Scope
This story covers the display logic that consumes the Zephr SDK response and conditionally renders the paywall gate or article body based on user tier and article category. It does not cover the masking of the article body or RHS panel (covered in Ticket 3 – Masking)

 , the article counter display (covered in Ticket 4)

 , nor the build of the paywall gate components 

 
Business Rules
Anonymous User
IF user is Anonymous, THEN the user always hits a paywall AND the paywall display rule applies.
Lite User (Registered) (No products assigned)
IF user is Lite AND the article page content type is Podcast, THEN the full article body is visible AND the free article allowance is unaffected.
IF user is Lite AND free articles are remaining, THEN the full article body is visible.
IF user is Lite AND the free article allowance is exhausted, THEN the user hits a paywall AND the paywall display rule applies.
Base User Product Assigned 
pei-pi-base-membership
, 
pei-pi-base-membership-trial
IF user is a Base user AND the article page has content type as Podcast, Tags as Awards, or the page is 

 , THEN the full article body is visible AND the monthly article allowance is unaffected.
IF user is Base user AND monthly articles are remaining, THEN the full article body is visible.
IF user is Base user AND the monthly article allowance is exhausted, THEN the user hits a paywall AND the paywall display rule applies.
Follow Functionality
IF user is  a Base user or Unlimited user, THEN the user should be able to follow profiles.
IF user is Anonymous or Lite, THEN follow buttons are not displayed.
RHS Lock Icon
IF the article is locked for any tier, THEN clicking the locked component in the RHS panel triggers the correct gate variant in the RHS context AND the gate is dismissible via close button or click-outside.
IF the gate is rendered in the article body, THEN it is not dismissible.
Fallback
IF Zephr fails to return a user tier, THEN the gate defaults to the Anonymous variant for an anonymous user
IF Zephr fails to return a user tier, THEN the gate defaults to the Unlimited variant for all users other than anonymous user
Products on Zephyr

pei-pi-unlimited-membership
,

 
pei-pi-base-membership

pei-pi-base-membership-trial

pei-pi-unlimited-membership-trial
Out of Scope
Visual masking of the article body and RHS panel (covered in Ticket 3 – Masking)
Build of the paywall gate components (Ticket 1)
Article counter display logic (Ticket 4)
Counter reset logic (server-side, covered separately)
Full Subscriber and Legacy Platinum user behaviour
Functionality of Request Demo and Find Out More CTAs
Legacy products
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
Miro:

 
UI:
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Assumptions:
 User tier is provided by Zephr at page load. Article category is available to the frontend at page load.
Dependencies:
 Zephr SDK integration. Ticket 1 (Paywall Gate components) must be complete.
Risks:
 If Zephr fails to return a user tier, the fallback (Anonymous gate) must be in place. Category exemption logic must correctly handle articles tagged under multiple categories.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 – Anonymous User: Paywall

 GIVEN the user is Anonymous

 WHEN they land on any article page

 THEN the paywall display rule applies
AC2 – Lite User: Free Article Access
 

GIVEN the user is Lite AND free articles are remaining 

WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the full article body is visible
AC3 – Lite User: Allowance Exhausted

 GIVEN the user is Lite AND the free article allowance is exhausted

 WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the paywall display rule applies
AC4 – Lite User: Podcast Exemption
 

GIVEN the user is Lite AND the article page has content type as Podcast as a category

WHEN they land on the page

 THEN the full article body is visible AND the free article allowance is unaffected
AC5 – 
Base user
 User: Monthly Article Access

 GIVEN the user is Base user AND monthly articles are remaining

 WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the full article body is visible
AC6 – 
Base user
: Allowance Exhausted

title: OBX Homepage - Performance Optimisation - Data Tables & Charts
description:
Background
The OBX II homepage and fund-related pages include a number of data-heavy components — charts and tables — that contribute to slower load times due to the size of their underlying libraries and data requirements. To improve performance, these components should load strategically, only when needed, without impacting the user's ability to interact with them once loaded.
User Story
AS A
 user viewing data-heavy pages on OBX II,

I WANT
 charts and tables to load progressively and remain responsive,

SO THAT
 the page loads faster and I can interact with data components without delay.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to introduce application-level caching using Next.js built-in caching (
fetch
, 
revalidate
, and 
tags
) for high-traffic modules. This will reduce dependency on external APIs, improve performance, and minimize latency, especially under peak load.
Caching should be applied to the following modules:
Bento Box > Latest investor intentions and RFP's
Fundraising Intelligence
Latest fundraising news
Recently added investor profile
Latest investor commitments table
Live fundraising chart
Investor Activity
Investor intentions and RFPs
Latest investor news
Upcoming investor meetings
Fund Performance
10 Largest infrastructure funds by size table
Recent Fund Performance table
Business Rules:
Use Next.js Fetch Caching
All external API calls must use Next.js 
fetch
 with caching enabled.
Example:
fetch(url, {
  next: {
    revalidate: 600,
    tags: ['component-api-unauthenticated']
  }
})Cache TTL (Revalidation)
 Use 
revalidate: 600
 (10 minutes) for all applicable APIs.
 TTL should be configurable via environment/config if needed.
Cache Tag Strategy
Introduce standardized cache tags:
component-api-authenticated
component-api-unauthenticated Apply tags based on user state:
 Logged-in → 
component-api-authenticated
 Logged-out → 
component-api-unauthenticated
Avoid Duplicate Cache Entries
 Ensure identical API calls reuse the same cache by:
 Using consistent request URLs
 Avoiding unnecessary query params
 Do not create multiple cache entries for the same data.
Server Component Usage
 All cached API calls should be made in 
Server Components
 or server-side utilities.
 Avoid client-side fetching for these APIs where caching is required.
Fallback Handling
 If API fails:
 Serve stale cached data (Next.js ISR behavior)
 Avoid breaking UI
Logging & Observability
 Add logging around API calls to identify:
 Cache hits (served from Next.js cache)
 Cache misses (actual API call)
 Logs should help measure reduction in external API traffic
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
Scenario 1 :  All listed modules use Next.js 
fetch
 with 
revalidate
 and 
tags
Scenario 2 : Cache TTL is set to 10 minutes
Scenario 3 : Cache tags are applied consistently (auth vs unauth)
Scenario 4 :  Duplicate API calls are minimized
Scenario 5 : No user-specific data is incorrectly cached/shared
Scenario 6 : Logs are available for cache hit/miss tracking
Scenario 7 :  Performance improvement observed (reduced API calls)

title: Blaize Session Not Updated After Magic Link Login
description:
Steps To Reproduce:

When a user accesses 
app.infrastructureinvestor.com/database/gp
, clicks on 
Sign In
, receives a magic link, and logs in via the link, they are redirected back to the same page and appear signed in. However, the 
classic site still shows a paywall
 because the Blaize session is not updated and continues to use the anonymous session.
Expected Behavior:

After successful login via magic link, the Blaize session should be updated correctly, and the paywall should not be displayed.
Actual Behavior:
User is authenticated
Blaize session remains anonymous
Paywall is still shown on the classic site
Additional Observation:

When the user logs in via the homepage instead of the magic link, the Blaize session is correctly initialized and the paywall is not shown.

title: To view relevant paywall copy as per user access rules
description:
Background
Once the gate logic and masking are in place, the correct paywall gate variant and article counter must be rendered based on the user's tier and remaining article count as resolved by Zephr. This ticket covers the end-to-end display of the correct gate variant per user tier, and the article counter for Lite and Professional Subscriber users.
Pre-Requisite
Zephr SDK must be in place and able to expose user tier and remaining article count to the frontend
User tier taxonomy must be defined and implemented (Anonymous, Lite, Monthly Subscriber)
Paywall gate and article counter Storybook components must be complete 

 
Paywall gate logic and section locking must be implemented (Ticket 2)
Masking: Main Content & RHS must be implemented (Ticket 3)
USER STORY 
As an end user,

I want to see the correct paywall gate and article counter based on my access tier

 So that I receive messaging and view counts relevant to my subscription level and understand what actions are available to me.
Scope
This story covers the rendering of the correct paywall gate variant and article counter based on the user tier and remaining article count resolved by Zephr. It does not cover the logic for when a user hits a paywall (Ticket 2), the masking of the article body or RHS panel (Ticket 3), or the build of the components (Ticket 1).

Business Rules
Anonymous User
IF user is Anonymous, THEN the Anonymous gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed.
Lite User
IF user is Lite AND free articles are remaining, THEN the article counter is displayed at the top of the article body reading "You have X free articles remaining" AND the gate is not displayed.
IF user is Lite AND the free article allowance is exhausted, THEN the Lite gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed.
IF user is Lite AND the article page category is Podcast, THEN the full article body is visible AND the article counter is not affected.
Professional User
IF user is Professional AND monthly articles are remaining, THEN the article counter is displayed at the top of the article body reading "You have X articles remaining this month" AND the gate is not displayed.
IF user is Professional AND the monthly article allowance is exhausted, THEN the Professional gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed.
IF user is Professional AND the article page category is Podcast, Awards, or Calendar, THEN the full article body is visible AND the monthly article allowance is unaffected.
Fallback
IF Zephr fails to return a user tier, THEN the Anonymous gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed.
Out of Scope
Paywall gate logic and section locking (Ticket 2)
Visual masking of article body and RHS panel (Ticket 3)
Build of paywall gate and article counter components (Ticket 1)
Counter reset logic (server-side, covered separately)
Full Subscriber and Legacy Platinum user behaviour
Functionality of Request Demo and Find Out More CTAs
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
Figma: 

 
Miro: 

 
UI: 
RAID Analysis - Assumptions / Dependencies / Risks 
Assumptions:
 User tier and remaining article count are provided by Zephr at page load. Article category is available to the frontend at page load.
Dependencies:
 Tickets 1–3 must be complete. Zephr SDK must reliably expose user tier and remaining article count.
Risks:
 If Zephr returns an unexpected or missing tier, the fallback (Anonymous gate) must be in place. Counter copy must correctly reflect the tier-specific wording.
ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA - Functionality 
AC1 – Anonymous User: Gate Display

 GIVEN the user is Anonymous 

WHEN they land on any article page

 THEN the Anonymous gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed
AC2 – Lite User: Counter Display
 

GIVEN the user is Lite AND free articles are remaining 

WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the article counter is displayed at the top of the article body reading "You have X free articles remaining" with the correct count
AC3 – Lite User: Gate Display

 GIVEN the user is Lite AND the free article allowance is exhausted 

WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the Lite gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed
AC4 – Lite User: Podcast Exemption
 

GIVEN the user is Lite AND the article page category is Podcast 

WHEN they land on the page 

THEN the full article body is visible AND the article counter is unaffected
AC5 – Professional User: Counter Display
 

GIVEN the user is Professional AND monthly articles are remaining 

WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the article counter is displayed at the top of the article body reading "You have X articles remaining this month" with the correct count
AC6 – Professional User: Gate Display
 

GIVEN the user is Professional AND the monthly article allowance is exhausted 

WHEN they land on a paywalled article page

 THEN the Professional gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed
AC7 – Professional User: Exempt Categories
 

GIVEN the user is Professional AND the article page category is Podcast, Awards, or Calendar 

WHEN they land on the page T

HEN the full article body is visible AND the monthly article allowance is unaffected
AC8 – Fallback State

 GIVEN Zephr fails to return a user tier

 WHEN the page renders

 THEN the Anonymous gate variant is displayed AND the article counter is not displayed

description:
Background
 As part of the OBX initiative, we will be building a watchlist feature. In the first iteration, users will receive in-platform notifications when a news article is published about an institution or fund they have added to their watchlist.

So that

Scope

Business Rules: 
IF a user is anonymous or signed-out on the OBX Infrastructure Fusion site, AND they attempt to access the Watchlist page (via direct URL, bookmarked link, or any entry point), THEN they must automatically be navigated to the signed-out OBX Homepage.
Ensure the watchlist page on the OBX Infrastructure Fusion site is accessible only to authenticated (signed-in) users.

title: Ask PEI - Error Messaging
description:
Background
Error handling was simplified from an earlier three-state spec. Rate limiting errors have been consolidated into the generic/fallback state. The two agreed error states cover all expected failure scenarios from the Ask PEI backend API.
These states were agreed with John Anderson (backend) and communicated to the design team (Andre Livingston) for UI treatment. Designs to be attached to this ticket prior to development.
User Story
AS A
 logged-in user of Ask PEI,

I WANT
 to see a clear, user-friendly and non-technical error message when there is an issue,

SO THAT
 I can understand there is an issue.
Scope 
The scope of this ticket is to implement two standardized error message states within the Ask PEI interface to handle API and service failures gracefully.
Out of Scope 
Business Rules:
Error messages must 
never expose HTTP status codes or technical error details
 to the user
The user's 
prompt/question must be preserved
 in the input field after an error — they should be able to retry without re-typing
Both error states display 
inline within the Ask PEI response area
 — not as a page-level banner or modal
Error state UI must be 
consistent with the Ask PEI design language
 — refer to Figma designs from Andre Livingston
The 
"Try again"
 action re-submits the exact same user query to the API
If consecutive errors occur, the same error state persists — no escalation to a different message
NFRs:
Responsive across the 1024, 1440 and 1920 viewports.
Add data test id to the component.
Error State Specifications
Error State 1: 
Temporary Service Issue
HTTP codes handled: 500, 503, 408, 504
User-facing message:
"Ask PEI is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few moments."
When to display: Server errors, gateway timeouts, and service unavailability. These are transient issues where retrying is likely to succeed.
UI behaviour:
Display error message inline within the Ask PEI chat interface (replacing the loading/response state)
Include a "Try again" CTA that re-submits the user's last question
Do not clear the user's input from the prompt field
Error should be dismissible / replaced on next successful response
Error State 2:
 Generic / Fallback Error
HTTP codes handled: 400, 401, 403, 404, 429
User-facing message:
"We couldn't process your question right now. Please try again or contact support if this continues."
When to display: Bad requests, authentication failures, permission errors, not found, and rate limiting. These may or may not resolve on retry.
UI behaviour:
UI is below for the message UI structure, but the messages themselves, should be taken from this ticket. 
Display error message inline within the Ask PEI chat interface
Include a "Try again" CTA (shown as refresh on design) – Currently not possible.
Do not clear the user's input from the prompt field
Error should be dismissible / replaced on next successful response
UX/UI
Screenshot:
Figma Link: 

 
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Temporary Service Issue displays for 5xx / timeout errors
GIVEN I have submitted a question in Ask PEI,

WHEN the API returns a 
500
, 
503
, 
408
, or 
504
 response,

THEN I see the message: 
"Ask PEI is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few moments."

AND I see a "Try again" CTA.

AND my original question remains in the input field.
AC2: Generic error displays for 4xx errors
GIVEN I have submitted a question in Ask PEI,

WHEN the API returns a 
400
, 
401
, 
403
, 
404
, or 
429
 response,

THEN I see the message: 
"We couldn't process your question right now. Please try again or contact support if this continues."

AND I see a "Try again" CTA and a "Contact support" link.

AND my original question remains in the input field.
AC3: Try again re-submits the question
GIVEN an error state is displayed,

WHEN I click "Try again",

THEN the same question is re-submitted to the API

AND the loading state is shown while the response is being processed.
AC4: No HTTP codes or technical detail visible to user
GIVEN any error state is triggered,

WHEN the error message is displayed,

THEN the user sees only the agreed user-facing message copy — no status codes, stack traces, or API error detail.
AC5: Error state is replaced on successful response
GIVEN an error state is currently displayed,

WHEN a subsequent query is submitted and returns successfully,

THEN the error message is replaced by the new successful response.
AC6: Error UI matches design specification
GIVEN either error state is triggered,

WHEN the message is displayed,

THEN the layout, typography, and component treatment match the Figma designs supplied by Andre Livingston.

title: Standfirst on the article page to be sourced from the 'Sub-title' field in WP Admin

Update the article page to source the Standfirst from the 'Sub-title' WP Admin field instead of the 'Excerpt' field.

IF an article has a 'Sub-title' field populated in WP Admin, THEN the Standfirst on the article page should display the value from the 'Sub-title' field. ELSE 'Excerpt' field populated in WP Admin THEN Standfirst should be displayed from the 'Excerpt’ field ELSE no standfirst SHOULD BE DISPLAYED

NA

title: [QA] Search Engine Optimisation - Performance - to analyse CWV for fusion across web/mobile
description:

As part of the OBX V2 customer-facing release, we want to ensure the website is fully SEO-optimized. This will help Google and external agents crawl the site effectively. Our goal is to improve overall search visibility and ranking.

Even if 90% of your users are on desktop

Your mobile performance directly impacts ALL rankings (mobile AND desktop)

o that

title: Search Engine Optimisation -URL Architecture - iPullRank- URL Parity fix for trailing slashes
description:
Background 
As part of the OBX V2 customer-facing release, we want to ensure the website is fully SEO-optimized. This will help Google and external agents crawl the site effectively. Our goal is to improve overall search visibility and ranking.
SEO Concern:
 
During the SEO audit, multiple redirect chains were identified (308 → 200 status codes) due to inconsistencies between OBX URL patterns and WordPress canonical URLs. This could be by the OBX fusion 
headless  frontend CMS urls different from the source CMS (Wordpress)  increased risk of crawl inefficiency and canonical inconsistency.
SEO Requirement: 
Lock permalink logic before launch and prevent frontend routing from reinterpreting URLs. Preserve existing URL structure and trailing slash behavior.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
SEO - Quick Wins Deck: 

 
Prioritistation Spreadsheet: 

 
User Story 
As a
 site visitor

I want
 pages to load with a single request without multiple redirects

So that
 I experience faster page loads and search engines can efficiently crawl the site
Scope
Based on Slide#51 from the SEO Quick Wins 
App level configuration to disable on the next.js app to not remove the  trailing slash and respect the URL with the trailing slash
 
Cloundfront function update 
- To preserve URL structure for Legacy WP on the Fusion OBX URLs  to maintain same URL architecture with a  trailing slash 
IF incoming is URL THEN redirect with URL/  
NOTE
 Not applicable on the API
Assumptions / Open Questions / Dependency
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1 : Respect URL trailinsg slash if already exists 
Given the incoming URL has a trailing slash
WHEN the url loads
Then the URL should retain the trailing slash and not remove
AC2: All top nav links should have a trailing slash 
Given I am on the Top nav
When I click on any of the Top nav links URL
Then The URL should have a trailing slash 
AC3 : All WP pages
Given I am on the fusion site
When I access any WP pages
Then these should have trialing slasj
Scenarios 
WP Landing pages - taxonomies, author, tags
WP Special-report links should have trailing slash 
AC4 : All fusion pages
Given I am on the fusion site
When I access any fusion pages
Then these URLs should have trialing slashes
Scenarios 
Marketing page
Register page
Request demo
Watchlist
Search results page
Article pages 
Test case 
Crawl a representative sample of live
WordPress URLs (articles, categories, tags, archives).
Request each URL in the headless environment and verify:
Single 200 response (no multi-hop redirects)
Full HTML rendering
Trailing-slash parity with the live site
Self-referencing canonical matching the rendered URL
Confirm no duplicate fetches or redirect loops introduced by service workers or CDN routing.

title: Registrations - Address design snags from sprint 4 and 5

Issue
Description

UI Comments/Feedback
Whole of the form panel should stay fixed grid between breakpoints
The whole form panel is currently fluid in build across 2xl,xl and lg breakpoints.

title: Request a Demo - (Anonymous Users Only) - Integrate Form with Zephr / CRM
description:
Background
We’re introducing a new Request a Demo page with an improved user interface to enable prospects to submit demo requests and allow the sales team to follow up with a personalised walkthrough. The new Request a Demo page will be implemented on the new OBX infrastructure Fusion site
As a
 Anonymous user, 
I want 
the request a demo form to be integrated with Zephr / CRM 
So that
 my details are stored, a free account is created and my request is added to sales queue. 
Scope 
To integrate the new request a demo form with Zephr /CRM
Note: the request a demo form is only required for 
anonymous users
 only. The request a demo form is not required for 
registered users
 as their details will already be stored in our system. Registered users will just see the confirmation message when they click any request a demo CTA. This is as per existing behaviour on classic site.
Out of Scope
The following features will be handled in separate tickets:
Creating a new request a demo page and form.
Adding testimonial & image to the request a demo page
Adding field validation & error messages to the request a demo form
Adding server side validations
Mobile responsive and making the pages available to crawlers
Business Rules & Web Hooks
IF a prospective successfully submits a demo form, AND the contact does not exist in CRM under that email address that was provided in the request a demo form,  THEN a 
new account 
should be create in Zephr for the user,
AND a new contact should automatically be created in CRM and a web activity created against that contact. The Web activity brand = [brand name i.e. Infrastructure Investor] and Web Activity Sub Type = Demo Request, 
Zephr Registration endpoint: (In zephr documentation 
(/blaize/register)
There is Request demo SDK (Pei ra  frontend, already exists - the existing sdk needs to be triggered)
Registration: The following Zephr web activity attributes should be sent to CRM. (
 Web activity brand
 
= PEIPI
The Web Activity Sub-Type = 
17868cea-64ee-e911-a812-000d3a86d6ba
Request DemoThe following Zephr web activity attributes should be sent to CRM.
 Web activity brand
 
= PEIPI
The Web Activity Sub-Type = demo_request  
( cb88bfe6-555a-ef11-bfe3-000d3a0cfd22 No longer exists in the doc)
The existing CRM web activity rules should be trigged based web activity attributes mentioned above. No change required to existing flow.
Assumptions / Dependencies / Open Questions / Documentations
Assumption - use the existing Infrastructure Investor brand attribute to unblock this ticket. We will update the brand once it’s amended in CRM and Zephr. 
Acceptance Criteria 
Scenario 1 
 - Successfully Submit Request a Demo Form - Create Web Activity
GIVEN
 that I am Anonymous user, 
WHEN
 I enter all mandatory fields on the request a demo form, 
AND
 I successfully submit a demo request,
THEN
 a new account should be created in Zephr for the user,
AND
 a contact should be created in CRM, 
AND
 the boolean `infra_demo_request_at_registration` should be populated in the user attribute in Zephr

title: Search Engine Optimisation  - Redirects - to implement article redirects via Cloudfront using Key Value store (KVS)
description:
Background 
It is very common that Editorials can change the article title, which will then result in a new URL for an article. 
Currently, article redirects are managed at the WP level, with Fusion serving as a headless WP. Fusion is not looking for the redirected articles; as a result, user experience is negatively impacted with a page not found.
Issue
: Article redirects where the redirect is not respected due to the path being managed by Fusion (so it never gets sent to WP where the 301 redirect would be actioned).
E.g. 
Redirect issue here:

No redirect happens

https://app.infrastructureinvestor.com/the-pipeline-macquaries-e1-2bn-european-haul-ares-hires-gip-credit-partner-blackstone-forms-gas-jv-2
Should be:


 

301 to

  
Solution
 : A new process to map the redirects on the Fusion OBX II Fusion by maintaining a master list of redirected URL and any redirects will now be handled at the edge via CloudFront, using a KeyValueStore (KVS) to store all redirect mappings and a CloudFront Function to intercept requests and action the appropriate 301  and follow the redirected URL
User Story
AS A
 user following a link to an article that has been redirected,

I WANT
 to be taken to the correct destination automatically,

SO THAT
 I do not land on a broken or incorrect page due to a redirect not being respected.
SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION
SEO  WAAC Requirements 

 
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to implement a CloudFront KeyValueStore containing all WordPress redirect mappings, and a CloudFront Function to intercept viewer requests and action 301 redirects to the new URL at the edge before requests reach Fusion. 
Out of Scope:
No changes to WordPress redirect configuration
Redirects for non-article paths unless explicitly confirmed as in scope
Not a mandate for GA release based on the assumption noted - A  process must be in place for automatic update of KVS values going forward,  <
separate ticket to be linked
>. Till then, any update should happen manually by DMO/
TBD?
Technical Solution 
The CloudFront Function must be associated with the CloudFront distribution as a Viewer Request trigger.
Redirects must be actioned at the edge before requests reach Fusion, ensuring no dependency on WordPress or Fusion for redirect resolution.
Business rules
: 
All existing WordPress article redirects must be migrated into the KVS as a one-time initial population.
A CloudFront KeyValueStore must be created to store all redirect mappings (
reference to 36k URLs
), where keys are the request URIs and values are the full target URLs.
A CloudFront Function must be created to intercept viewer requests, look up the request URI in the KVS, 
If a match is found in the KVS 
THEN
 and return a 301 redirect 
and
 follow the redirect link to fusion
If no match is found in the KVS, 
THEN
 the request must be passe  to Fusion 
 Deployment across all environments  - dev/stage/ prod
A pipeline must be created to automatically deploy CloudFront Functions across all environments.
A CloudFront KeyValueStore must be created for every environment: 
NFRs
DMO / Editorial process on any changes to the URLs, so the devOPs can update the new lsit 
Redirects must be returned with the correct 301 status code and location header.
No degradation to page load performance or existing routing behavior for non-redirected URLs.
Assumptions / Open Questions / Dependency
It looks like there have been 6 redirects added to II since the beginning of this year (~2.5  months)
Those who typically add the redirects will be Production staff and DMO – so it should be easy enough to inform that group of a change in process in the short term. 
Acceptance Criteria
 
AC1: Onetime migration of All Redirect URLS to a CloudFront KeyValueStore in Place
GIVEN 
the implementation has been completed,

WHEN 
the CloudFront KVS is reviewed,

THEN 
a KeyValueStore exists for every environment, populated with all redirect mappings where keys are request URIs and values are full target URLs.
AC2:  Redirect Actioned at the Edge
GIVEN 
a user follows a URL that exists as a key in the CloudFront KVS,

WHEN 
the request is intercepted by the CloudFront Function at the Viewer Request trigger,
IF
 a match is found in the KVS 
THEN
 return a 301 redirect 
and
 follow the redirect link
IF
 no match is found in the KVS, 
THEN
 the request must be passed through to Fusion 
AC3: Deployment Pipeline in Place
GIVEN 
the implementation has been completed,

WHEN 
a CloudFront Function update is required,

THEN 
an automated deployment pipeline exists to deploy the function across all environments. namely development, stage and production
AC4: NFRS on user experience 
GIVEN
  a request encouters a redirected URL 
WHEN
 transitions to the new redirected URL 
THEN
 no degradation to page load performance should be expeirenced by the user 
AND
 existing routing behavior for non-redirected URLs shoudl not be affected for the user
