title: Ask PEI - Implement source linking (in chat)
description:
Background
To increase transparency and help users verify information provided by the Ask PEI chatbot, we need to implement inline source citations within chat responses. When users hover over or click on these source links, a popover overlay should display related articles or sources, allowing users to quickly preview and access the underlying content that informed the chatbot's response.
User Story
AS A
 user reading responses from the Ask PEI chatbot, 

I WANT 
to see inline source citations and access detailed source information via a popover,

SO THAT
 I can verify the information provided and access the original sources for deeper research.
Scope
The scope of this ticket is to enable the display of inline source citations within chatbot responses and provides an interactive popover overlay that shows related source article(s) when users interact with the citation links.
Business Rules:
Source citations appear as inline links within the chatbot response text.
Source links display a category/type label (e.g., "Infrastructure Investor", "News & Analysis", "Data").
A "+N" indicator shows when multiple sources are cited.
Hovering over or clicking a source link triggers a popover overlay.
The popover displays a list of related articles/sources with titles and published dates.
Users can click on articles within the popover to navigate to the full source.
The popover dismisses when the user clicks outside of it or moves away from the source link
UX / UI
Figma Link: 

 
 UI:  
Assumptions / Open Questions
None
Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Display Inline Source Links in Chat Response
GIVEN
 the chatbot has generated a response with source citations,

WHEN
 the response is displayed in the chat interface,

THEN
 source citations should appear as inline links within the response text,

AND
 each source link should display a category/type label (e.g., "Infrastructure Investor", "News & Analysis", "Data"),

AND
 the source link styling should differentiate it from regular text as per design specifications,

AND
 source links should be positioned inline with the relevant text content.
AC2: Display Multiple Source Indicator
GIVEN
 multiple sources are cited within the response,

WHEN
 the response is displayed,

THEN
 a "+N" indicator should be shown (where N is the number of additional sources),

AND
 the indicator should be styled according to design specifications,

AND
 the indicator should be clickable/hoverable to show all related sources.
AC3: Trigger Popover on 
Hover/click?
GIVEN
 I am viewing a chat response with inline source links,

WHEN
 I hover my cursor over a source link,

THEN
 a popover overlay should appear near the source link,

AND
 the popover should remain visible while my cursor is over the link or the popover itself.
AC4: Display Source Articles in Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is triggered for a source link,

WHEN
 the popover is displayed,

THEN
 the popover should show a list of related source articles,

AND
 each article should display its title,

AND
 each article should display its publication date,

AND
 each article should display its category/source type (e.g., "News and analysis", "Data"),

AND
 the popover content should be styled according to design specifications.
AC5: Popover Positioning
GIVEN
 a popover is triggered by hovering or clicking a source link,

WHEN
 the popover is displayed,

THEN
 the popover should be positioned near the source link,

AND
 the popover should not obscure the source link or critical content,

AND
 the popover should adjust its position if it would extend beyond the viewport boundaries,

AND
 the popover positioning should follow design specifications.
AC7: Navigate to Source Article from Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is displayed with a list of source articles,

WHEN
 I click on an article title within the popover,

THEN
 I should be navigated to the full article page,

AND
 the popover should close after navigation.
AC8: Dismiss Popover
GIVEN
 the popover overlay is currently displayed,

WHEN
 I click outside of the popover area,

THEN
 the popover should close and disappear.
